/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba components/dashboard/ProbabilitySurface.tsx | sed -n '1,340p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"use client"
     2	
     3	import React, { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react"
     4	
     5	interface ForecastTarget {
     6	  id: string
     7	  horizonDays: number
     8	  horizonLabel: string
     9	  priceLow: number | null
    10	  priceHigh: number | null
    11	  oofPrice: number | null
    12	  coveragePct: number | null
    13	  isMissing?: boolean
    14	}
    15	
    16	const BIN_COUNT = 11
    17	const AG_HORIZON_DAYS = [30, 90, 180] as const
    18	
    19	function horizonLabel(days: number): string {
    20	  if (days === 30) return "1M"
    21	  if (days === 90) return "3M"
    22	  if (days === 180) return "6M"
    23	  return `${days}d`
    24	}
    25	
    26	function clamp(v: number, min: number, max: number): number {
    27	  return Math.min(max, Math.max(min, v))
    28	}
    29	
    30	function cellAlpha(price: number, t: ForecastTarget): number {
    31	  if (t.priceLow === null || t.priceHigh === null || t.oofPrice === null) {
    32	    return 0.08
    33	  }
    34	
    35	  const low = Math.min(t.priceLow, t.priceHigh)
    36	  const high = Math.max(t.priceLow, t.priceHigh)
    37	  const halfWidth = Math.max((high - low) / 2, 0.1)
    38	  const centerDist = Math.abs(price - t.oofPrice) / halfWidth
    39	  const insideZone = price >= low && price <= high
    40	
    41	  if (insideZone) {
    42	    return clamp(1 - centerDist * 0.75, 0.25, 1)
    43	  }
    44	
    45	  const outsideDist = Math.abs(price - t.oofPrice) / (halfWidth * 2.5)
    46	  return clamp(0.18 - outsideDist * 0.12, 0.04, 0.18)
    47	}
    48	
    49	export function ProbabilitySurface() {
    50	  const [targets, setTargets] = useState<ForecastTarget[]>([])
    51	  const [asOfDate, setAsOfDate] = useState<string | null>(null)
    52	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    53	  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    54	
    55	  useEffect(() => {
    56	    let cancelled = false
    57	
    58	    async function loadTargets() {
    59	      setLoading(true)
    60	      setError(null)
    61	      try {
    62	        const res = await fetch("/api/zl/forecast-targets", { cache: "no-store" })
    63	        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    64	        const json = await res.json()
    65	        if (cancelled) return
    66	        setTargets(
    67	          (json.targets ?? [])
    68	            .filter(
    69	              (t: ForecastTarget) =>
    70	                AG_HORIZON_DAYS.includes(t?.horizonDays as (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number]) &&
    71	                typeof t?.horizonDays === "number" &&
    72	                typeof t?.priceLow === "number" &&
    73	                typeof t?.priceHigh === "number" &&
    74	                typeof t?.oofPrice === "number",
    75	            )
    76	            .map((t: ForecastTarget) => ({
    77	              ...t,
    78	              horizonLabel: horizonLabel(t.horizonDays),
    79	            }))
    80	            .sort((a: ForecastTarget, b: ForecastTarget) => a.horizonDays - b.horizonDays),
    81	        )
    82	        setAsOfDate(json.asOfDate ?? null)
    83	      } catch (e) {
    84	        if (cancelled) return
    85	        setError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Failed to load target zones")
    86	      } finally {
    87	        if (!cancelled) setLoading(false)
    88	      }
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    loadTargets()
    92	    return () => { cancelled = true }
    93	  }, [])
    94	
    95	  const priceBins = useMemo(() => {
    96	    const resolvedTargets = targets.filter(
    97	      (t) => t.priceLow !== null && t.priceHigh !== null && t.oofPrice !== null,
    98	    )
    99	    if (resolvedTargets.length === 0) return [] as number[]
   100	
   101	    const low = Math.min(...resolvedTargets.map((t) => Math.min(t.priceLow!, t.priceHigh!)))
   102	    const high = Math.max(...resolvedTargets.map((t) => Math.max(t.priceLow!, t.priceHigh!)))
   103	    if (!Number.isFinite(low) || !Number.isFinite(high)) return [] as number[]
   104	
   105	    const span = Math.max(high - low, 1)
   106	    const min = low - span * 0.05
   107	    const max = high + span * 0.05
   108	    const step = (max - min) / (BIN_COUNT - 1)
   109	
   110	    return Array.from(
   111	      { length: BIN_COUNT },
   112	      (_, idx) => Number((max - idx * step).toFixed(2)),
   113	    )
   114	  }, [targets])
   115	
   116	  const displayTargets = useMemo(() => {
   117	    const byHorizon = new Map<number, ForecastTarget>()
   118	    for (const t of targets) byHorizon.set(t.horizonDays, t)
   119	
   120	    return AG_HORIZON_DAYS.map((horizonDays) => {
   121	      const resolved = byHorizon.get(horizonDays)
   122	      if (resolved) return resolved
   123	
   124	      return {
   125	        id: `missing-${horizonDays}`,
   126	        horizonDays,
   127	        horizonLabel: horizonLabel(horizonDays),
   128	        priceLow: null,
   129	        priceHigh: null,
   130	        oofPrice: null,
   131	        coveragePct: null,
   132	        isMissing: true,
   133	      } satisfies ForecastTarget
   134	    })
   135	  }, [targets])
   136	
   137	  const gridTemplateColumns = `56px repeat(${Math.max(displayTargets.length, 1)}, minmax(0,1fr))`
   138	
   139	  return (
   140	    <div className="w-full bg-[#0a0a0a] border border-white/5 rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm overflow-hidden">
   141	      <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2">
   142	        <div>
   143	          <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wider">
   144	            L3 Probability Surface
   145	          </h3>
   146	          <p className="text-xs text-slate-500">
   147	            Real target zone density from latest AG trading-model forecast (1M/3M/6M)
   148	            {asOfDate ? ` (as of ${asOfDate})` : ""}
   149	          </p>
   150	        </div>
   151	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 text-[10px] text-slate-500">
   152	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
   153	            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-[1px] bg-cyan-400/90" />
   154	            High
   155	          </div>
   156	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
   157	            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-[1px] bg-cyan-400/20" />
   158	            Low
   159	          </div>
   160	        </div>
   161	      </div>
   162	
   163	      {loading && (
   164	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-slate-400">Loading forecast density...</div>
   165	      )}
   166	
   167	      {!loading && error && (
   168	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-red-400">Failed to load probability data: {error}</div>
   169	      )}
   170	
   171	      {!loading && !error && targets.length === 0 && (
   172	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-slate-400">
   173	          No production target zones available yet. This view populates automatically once forecast rows are present.
   174	        </div>
   175	      )}
   176	
   177	      {!loading && !error && targets.length > 0 && (
   178	        <div className="mt-6 space-y-2">
   179	          <div className="grid gap-1" style={{ gridTemplateColumns }}>
   180	            <div />
   181	            {displayTargets.map((t) => (
   182	              <div key={`h-${t.id}`} className="text-center text-[10px] text-slate-500 font-mono">
   183	                <div>{t.horizonLabel}</div>
   184	                {t.isMissing && (
   185	                  <div className="text-[9px] text-slate-600">pending</div>
   186	                )}
   187	              </div>
   188	            ))}
   189	
   190	            {priceBins.map((price) => (
   191	              <React.Fragment key={`row-${price}`}>
   192	                <div className="text-[10px] text-slate-600 font-mono pr-2 text-right">
   193	                  ${price.toFixed(2)}
   194	                </div>
   195	                {displayTargets.map((t) => {
   196	                  const alpha = cellAlpha(price, t)
   197	                  const title = t.isMissing
   198	                    ? `${t.horizonLabel} | awaiting forecast rows`
   199	                    : `${t.horizonLabel} | zone $${t.priceLow!.toFixed(2)}-$${t.priceHigh!.toFixed(2)} | median $${t.oofPrice!.toFixed(2)}`
   200	                  return (
   201	                    <div
   202	                      key={`${t.id}-${price}`}
   203	                      className="h-5 rounded-[2px] border border-white/5"
   204	                      style={{
   205	                        backgroundColor: `rgba(34, 211, 238, ${alpha})`,
   206	                        boxShadow: alpha > 0.75 ? "0 0 10px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.35)" : "none",
   207	                      }}
   208	                      title={title}
   209	                    />
   210	                  )
   211	                })}
   212	              </React.Fragment>
   213	            ))}
   214	          </div>
   215	
   216	          <div className="grid gap-1 pt-2 border-t border-white/5" style={{ gridTemplateColumns }}>
   217	            <div className="text-[10px] text-slate-600 font-mono pr-2 text-right">Coverage</div>
   218	            {displayTargets.map((t) => (

   220	                {t.coveragePct !== null ? `${t.coveragePct}%` : "--"}
   221	              </div>

   223	          </div>

   226	    </div>

     1	"use client"
     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react"
     4	import {
     5	  createChart,
     6	  LineSeries,
     7	  HistogramSeries,
     8	  ColorType,
     9	  LineStyle,
    10	  type IChartApi,
    11	  type Time,
    12	} from "lightweight-charts"
    13	
    14	type MarketRegime = "BULLISH" | "BEARISH" | "NEUTRAL" | "SUPPLY_CRISIS" | "DEMAND_SHOCK"
    15	
    16	const REGIME_COLORS: Record<MarketRegime, { bg: string; border: string }> = {
    17	  BULLISH: { bg: "rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.15)", border: "#22C55E" },
    18	  BEARISH: { bg: "rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.15)", border: "#EF4444" },
    19	  NEUTRAL: { bg: "rgba(107, 114, 128, 0.1)", border: "#6B7280" },
    20	  SUPPLY_CRISIS: { bg: "rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.25)", border: "#DC2626" },
    21	  DEMAND_SHOCK: { bg: "rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.25)", border: "#16A34A" },
    22	}
    23	
    24	interface RegimeZone {
    25	  start: string
    26	  end: string
    27	  regime: MarketRegime
    28	}
    29	
    30	function toChartDay(dateStr: string): Time {
    31	  return dateStr.slice(0, 10) as unknown as Time
    32	}
    33	
    34	function rollingSma(values: number[], period: number): Array<number | null> {
    35	  const out: Array<number | null> = Array(values.length).fill(null)
    36	  if (period <= 0 || values.length < period) return out
    37	
    38	  let sum = 0
    39	  for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i += 1) {
    40	    sum += values[i]
    41	    if (i >= period) sum -= values[i - period]
    42	    if (i >= period - 1) out[i] = sum / period
    43	  }
    44	  return out
    45	}
    46	
    47	export function RegimeAnalysisChart({
    48	  height = 400,
    49	  timeRange = "1Y",
    50	}: {
    51	  height?: number
    52	  timeRange?: "1M" | "3M" | "6M" | "1Y"
    53	}) {
    54	  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    55	  const chartRef = useRef<IChartApi | null>(null)
    56	  const [selectedRange, setSelectedRange] = useState(timeRange)
    57	  const [priceData, setPriceData] = useState<{ timestamp: string; close: number }[]>([])
    58	  const [currentRegime, setCurrentRegime] = useState<MarketRegime>("NEUTRAL")
    59	
    60	  useEffect(() => {
    61	    fetch("/api/zl/price-1d")
    62	      .then((r) => r.json())
    63	      .then((json) => {
    64	        if (json.data) {
    65	          setPriceData(
    66	            json.data.map((d: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
    67	              timestamp: String(d.tradeDate),
    68	              close: Number(d.close),
    69	            })),
    70	          )
    71	        }
    72	      })
    73	      .catch(() => {})
    74	  }, [])
    75	
    76	  useEffect(() => {
    77	    if (!containerRef.current || priceData.length === 0) return
    78	
    79	    // Filter by selected range
    80	    const days = { "1M": 30, "3M": 90, "6M": 180, "1Y": 365 }[selectedRange]
    81	    const cutoff = new Date()
    82	    cutoff.setDate(cutoff.getDate() - days)
    83	    const filtered = priceData.filter((d) => new Date(d.timestamp) >= cutoff)
    84	    if (filtered.length === 0) return
    85	
    86	    if (chartRef.current) {
    87	      try { chartRef.current.remove() } catch { /* disposed */ }
    88	      chartRef.current = null
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    const chart = createChart(containerRef.current, {
    92	      width: containerRef.current.clientWidth,
    93	      height,
    94	      layout: {
    95	        background: { type: ColorType.Solid, color: "transparent" },
    96	        textColor: "#525252",
    97	        attributionLogo: false,
    98	      },
    99	      grid: {
   100	        vertLines: { visible: false },
   101	        horzLines: { color: "rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03)" },
   102	      },
   103	      handleScroll: false,
   104	      handleScale: false,
   105	      crosshair: {
   106	        vertLine: {
   107	          color: "rgba(0, 212, 255, 0.2)",
   108	          width: 1,
   109	          style: LineStyle.Dashed,
   110	          labelVisible: true,
   111	        },
   112	        horzLine: { visible: false, labelVisible: false },
   113	      },
   114	      timeScale: {
   115	        visible: true,
   116	        borderVisible: false,
   117	        fixLeftEdge: true,
   118	        fixRightEdge: true,
   119	      },
   120	      rightPriceScale: { borderVisible: false, visible: false },
   121	      leftPriceScale: { visible: true, borderVisible: false },
   122	    })
   123	
   124	    chartRef.current = chart
   125	
   126	    const lineData = filtered
   127	      .map((d) => ({ time: toChartDay(d.timestamp), value: d.close }))
   128	      .sort((a, b) => String(a.time).localeCompare(String(b.time)))
   129	
   130	    // SMA-based regime detection
   131	    const regimeZones: RegimeZone[] = []
   132	    let currentZoneStart = lineData[0]?.time as string
   133	    let prevRegime: MarketRegime = "NEUTRAL"
   134	
   135	    const closeValues = lineData.map((d) => d.value)
   136	    const shortPeriod = Math.min(20, Math.max(8, Math.floor(lineData.length * 0.35)))
   137	    const longPeriod = Math.min(50, Math.max(shortPeriod + 8, Math.floor(lineData.length * 0.75)))
   138	    const bullThreshold = longPeriod >= 50 ? 1.02 : 1.01
   139	    const bearThreshold = longPeriod >= 50 ? 0.98 : 0.99
   140	    const supplyThreshold = longPeriod >= 50 ? 0.95 : 0.97
   141	    const demandThreshold = longPeriod >= 50 ? 1.08 : 1.05
   142	    const smaShort = rollingSma(closeValues, shortPeriod)
   143	    const smaLong = rollingSma(closeValues, longPeriod)
   144	
   145	    for (let i = 0; i < lineData.length; i += 1) {
   146	      const sShort = smaShort[i]
   147	      const sLong = smaLong[i]
   148	      if (sShort == null || sLong == null) continue
   149	
   150	      const price = lineData[i].value
   151	      let regime: MarketRegime = "NEUTRAL"
   152	      if (sShort > sLong * bullThreshold && price > sShort) regime = "BULLISH"
   153	      else if (sShort < sLong * bearThreshold && price < sShort) regime = "BEARISH"
   154	      else if (price < sLong * supplyThreshold) regime = "SUPPLY_CRISIS"
   155	      else if (price > sLong * demandThreshold) regime = "DEMAND_SHOCK"
   156	
   157	      if (regime !== prevRegime) {
   158	        if (currentZoneStart) {
   159	          regimeZones.push({ start: currentZoneStart, end: lineData[i].time as string, regime: prevRegime })
   160	        }
   161	        currentZoneStart = lineData[i].time as string
   162	        prevRegime = regime
   163	      }
   164	    }
   165	
   166	    if (currentZoneStart && lineData.length > 0) {
   167	      regimeZones.push({ start: currentZoneStart, end: lineData[lineData.length - 1].time as string, regime: prevRegime })
   168	      setCurrentRegime(prevRegime)
   169	    }
   170	
   171	    // Draw regime zones as histogram background
   172	    for (const zone of regimeZones) {
   173	      const zoneData = lineData
   174	        .filter((d) => (d.time as string) >= zone.start && (d.time as string) <= zone.end)
   175	        .map((d) => ({ time: d.time, value: 1, color: REGIME_COLORS[zone.regime].bg }))
   176	
   177	      if (zoneData.length > 0) {
   178	        const maxPrice = Math.max(...lineData.map((d) => d.value))
   179	        const minPrice = Math.min(...lineData.map((d) => d.value))
   180	        const range = maxPrice - minPrice
   181	
   182	        const zoneSeries = chart.addSeries(HistogramSeries, {
   183	          priceLineVisible: false,
   184	          lastValueVisible: false,
   185	          priceScaleId: "regime",
   186	          color: REGIME_COLORS[zone.regime].bg,
   187	        })
   188	
   189	        zoneSeries.setData(
   190	          zoneData.map((d) => ({
   191	            time: d.time,
   192	            value: maxPrice + range * 0.1,
   193	            color: REGIME_COLORS[zone.regime].bg,
   194	          })),
   195	        )
   196	      }
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    // Price line on top
   200	    const priceLine = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, {
   201	      color: "#ffffff",
   202	      lineWidth: 2,
   203	      priceLineVisible: false,
   204	      lastValueVisible: true,
   205	      priceScaleId: "left",
   206	    })
   207	    priceLine.setData(lineData)
   208	
   209	    chart.timeScale().fitContent()
   210	
   211	    let disposed = false
   212	    const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
   213	      if (disposed || !entries[0]) return
   214	      const { width } = entries[0].contentRect
   215	      try { chart.applyOptions({ width, height }) } catch { /* disposed */ }
   216	    })
   217	    observer.observe(containerRef.current)
   218	
   219	    return () => {
   220	      disposed = true
   221	      observer.disconnect()
   222	      try { chart.remove() } catch { /* disposed */ }
   223	      chartRef.current = null
   224	    }
   225	  }, [priceData, selectedRange, height])
   226	
   227	  return (
   228	    <div className="bg-[#0a0a0a] border border-white/5 rounded-xl overflow-hidden">
   229	      {/* Header */}
   230	      <div className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 border-b border-white/5">
   231	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   232	          <h3 className="text-sm font-bold text-white">ZL Futures - Regime Analysis</h3>
   233	          <span className="px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded text-[9px] font-bold uppercase bg-amber-500/20 text-amber-400 border border-amber-500/30">
   234	            Beta
   235	          </span>
   236	          <span
   237	            className="px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-[10px] font-bold uppercase"
   238	            style={{
   239	              backgroundColor: REGIME_COLORS[currentRegime].bg,
   240	              color: REGIME_COLORS[currentRegime].border,
   241	              border: `1px solid ${REGIME_COLORS[currentRegime].border}30`,
   242	            }}
   243	          >
   244	            {currentRegime.replace("_", " ")}
   245	          </span>
   246	        </div>
   247	
   248	        <div className="flex items-center bg-white/5 rounded-lg p-0.5">
   249	          {(["1M", "3M", "6M", "1Y"] as const).map((range) => (
   250	            <button
   251	              key={range}
   252	              onClick={() => setSelectedRange(range)}
   253	              className={`px-3 py-1 text-xs font-medium rounded-md transition-all ${
   254	                selectedRange === range ? "bg-white/10 text-white" : "text-slate-500 hover:text-white"
   255	              }`}
   256	            >
   257	              {range}
   258	            </button>
   259	          ))}
   260	        </div>
   261	      </div>
   262	
   263	      {/* Chart */}
   264	      <div ref={containerRef} style={{ height }} />
   265	
   266	      {/* Legend */}
   267	      <div className="flex items-center justify-center gap-6 py-2 border-t border-white/5 bg-white/[0.02]">
   268	        {(Object.entries(REGIME_COLORS) as [MarketRegime, { bg: string; border: string }][])
   269	          .map(([regime, colors]) => (
   270	            <div key={regime} className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
   271	              <div
   272	                className="w-3 h-3 rounded-sm"
   273	                style={{ backgroundColor: colors.bg, border: `1px solid ${colors.border}40` }}
   274	              />
   275	              <span className="text-[10px] text-slate-500 uppercase">
   276	                {regime.replace("_", " ")}
   277	              </span>
   278	            </div>
   279	          ))}
   280	      </div>
   281	    </div>
   282	  )
   283	}

     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react"
     4	
     5	type DriverData = {
     6	  score: number | null
     7	  components?: Record<string, number | null>
     8	}
     9	
    10	type RiskFactorsResponse = {
    11	  drivers?: {
    12	    vix_stress?: DriverData
    13	    crush_pressure?: DriverData
    14	    china_tension?: DriverData
    15	    tariff_threat?: DriverData
    16	    energy_stress?: DriverData
    17	  }
    18	  summary?: {
    19	    average_pressure?: number
    20	  }
    21	}
    22	
    23	type TickerRow = {
    24	  label: string
    25	  sublabel: string
    26	  value: number | null
    27	  score: number | null
    28	}
    29	
    30	const PRESSURE_DOMINANCE_THRESHOLD_PCT = 55
    31	const RISK_HIGH = 55
    32	const RISK_LOW = 45
    33	const HOURLY_REFRESH_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000
    34	
    35	function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number {
    36	  return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, value))
    37	}
    38	
    39	function toNumber(value: unknown): number | null {
    40	  const parsed = Number(value)
    41	  return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null
    42	}
    43	
    44	function stateFromScore(score: number | null): -1 | 0 | 1 {
    45	  if (score == null) return 0
    46	  if (score >= RISK_HIGH) return -1
    47	  if (score <= RISK_LOW) return 1
    48	  return 0
    49	}
    50	
    51	function stateLabel(state: -1 | 0 | 1): string {
    52	  if (state === 1) return "▲"
    53	  if (state === -1) return "▼"
    54	  return "—"
    55	}
    56	
    57	function stateColor(state: -1 | 0 | 1): string {
    58	  if (state === 1) return "#26C6DA"
    59	  if (state === -1) return "#FF0000"
    60	  return "rgba(255,255,255,0.20)"
    61	}
    62	
    63	function formatValue(label: string, value: number | null): string {
    64	  if (value == null) return "—"
    65	  if (label === "CNY") return value.toFixed(4)
    66	  return value.toFixed(2)
    67	}
    68	
    69	export function MarketSymbolPressureBar() {
    70	  const [riskFactors, setRiskFactors] = useState<RiskFactorsResponse | null>(null)
    71	
    72	  useEffect(() => {
    73	    let active = true
    74	
    75	    async function load() {
    76	      try {
    77	        const riskRes = await fetch("/api/dashboard/risk-factors", { cache: "no-store" })
    78	        const riskJson = (await riskRes.json()) as RiskFactorsResponse
    79	
    80	        if (!active) return
    81	        setRiskFactors(riskJson)
    82	      } catch {
    83	        if (!active) return
    84	        setRiskFactors(null)
    85	      }
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    void load()
    89	    const interval = setInterval(load, HOURLY_REFRESH_MS)
    90	    return () => {
    91	      active = false
    92	      clearInterval(interval)
    93	    }
    94	  }, [])
    95	
    96	  const tickerData = useMemo<TickerRow[]>(() => {
    97	    const drivers = riskFactors?.drivers
    98	    const vix = drivers?.vix_stress
    99	    const crush = drivers?.crush_pressure
   100	    const china = drivers?.china_tension
   101	    const tariff = drivers?.tariff_threat
   102	    const energy = drivers?.energy_stress
   103	
   104	    return [
   105	      {
   106	        label: "CL",
   107	        sublabel: "Crude Oil",
   108	        value: toNumber(energy?.components?.cl_price),
   109	        score: toNumber(energy?.score),
   110	      },
   111	      {
   112	        label: "VIX",
   113	        sublabel: "Volatility",
   114	        value: toNumber(vix?.components?.vix_value),
   115	        score: toNumber(vix?.score),
   116	      },
   117	      {
   118	        label: "OVX",
   119	        sublabel: "Oil Volatility",
   120	        value: toNumber(energy?.components?.ovx_value) ?? toNumber(vix?.components?.ovx_value),
   121	        score: toNumber(energy?.score) ?? toNumber(vix?.score),
   122	      },
   123	      {
   124	        label: "CNY",
   125	        sublabel: "Yuan",
   126	        value: toNumber(china?.components?.cny_rate),
   127	        score: toNumber(china?.score),
   128	      },
   129	      {
   130	        label: "CRUSH",
   131	        sublabel: "Crush Margin",
   132	        value: toNumber(crush?.components?.board_crush_value),
   133	        score: toNumber(crush?.score) ?? toNumber(tariff?.score),
   134	      },
   135	    ]
   136	  }, [riskFactors])
   137	
   138	  const laneImpacts = tickerData.map((row) => {
   139	    const score = row.score
   140	    if (score == null) return 0
   141	    return 50 - score
   142	  })
   143	  const upPressure = laneImpacts.reduce((sum, impact) => sum + Math.max(0, impact), 0)
   144	  const downPressure = laneImpacts.reduce((sum, impact) => sum + Math.max(0, -impact), 0)
   145	  const totalAbs = upPressure + downPressure
   146	  const upPressurePct = totalAbs > 0 ? (upPressure / totalAbs) * 100 : 50
   147	  const downPressurePct = 100 - upPressurePct
   148	  const confluencePct = Math.max(upPressurePct, downPressurePct)
   149	
   150	  const netUpDominant = upPressurePct >= PRESSURE_DOMINANCE_THRESHOLD_PCT
   151	  const netDownDominant = downPressurePct >= PRESSURE_DOMINANCE_THRESHOLD_PCT
   152	  const netDirectionLabel = netUpDominant ? "Bullish" : netDownDominant ? "Bearish" : "Balanced"
   153	
   154	  return (
   155	    <div
   156	      className="flex flex-col w-full flex-shrink-0"
   157	      style={{ borderBottom: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
   158	    >
   159	      <div className="flex items-center gap-0 w-full overflow-x-auto">
   160	        {tickerData.map((row, index) => {
   161	          const laneImpact = laneImpacts[index] ?? 0
   162	          const lanePressurePct = totalAbs > 0 ? (Math.abs(laneImpact) / totalAbs) * 100 : 0
   163	          const state = stateFromScore(row.score)
   164	          const scoreOpacity = row.score == null ? 0.45 : clamp(0.45 + (Math.abs(laneImpact) / 50) * 0.55, 0.45, 1)
   165	          const tintAlpha =
   166	            row.score == null
   167	              ? 0.04
   168	              : clamp(0.07 + (lanePressurePct / 100) * 0.22, 0.07, 0.30)
   169	          const laneBg =
   170	            state === 1
   171	              ? `rgba(38, 198, 218, ${tintAlpha})`
   172	              : state === -1
   173	                ? `rgba(242, 54, 69, ${tintAlpha})`
   174	                : "rgba(255,255,255,0.03)"
   175	
   176	          return (
   177	            <div
   178	              key={row.label}
   179	              className="flex-1 min-w-[90px] px-3 py-0 flex flex-col gap-0"
   180	              style={{
   181	                background: laneBg,
   182	                borderRight: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.04)",
   183	                transition: "background 0.3s ease",
   184	                opacity: scoreOpacity,
   185	              }}
   186	            >
   187	              <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
   188	                <span className="text-[11px] font-bold" style={{ color: stateColor(state) }}>
   189	                  {stateLabel(state)}
   190	                </span>
   191	                <span className="text-[11px] font-semibold text-white/55 tracking-wide">
   192	                  {row.label}
   193	                </span>
   194	                <span className="text-[9px] text-white tracking-wider">
   195	                  {row.sublabel}
   196	                </span>
   197	              </div>
   198	              <div className="flex items-baseline gap-2 flex-wrap">
   199	                <span className="text-sm font-medium text-white tabular-nums">
   200	                  {formatValue(row.label, row.value)}
   201	                </span>
   202	                <span className="text-[10px] text-white tabular-nums">
   203	                  {row.score == null ? "—" : `${Math.round(row.score)}`}
   204	                </span>
   205	                <span className="text-[9px] text-white tabular-nums">
   206	                  {lanePressurePct.toFixed(0)}%
   207	                </span>
   208	              </div>
   209	            </div>
   210	          )
   211	        })}
   212	
   213	        <div
   214	          className="flex-none min-w-[80px] px-3 py-0 flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-0"
   215	          style={{
   216	            background: netUpDominant
   217	              ? "rgba(38, 198, 218, 0.14)"
   218	              : netDownDominant
   219	                ? "rgba(242, 54, 69, 0.16)"
   220	                : "rgba(255,255,255,0.03)",
   221	            borderLeft: "1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08)",
   222	          }}
   223	        >
   224	          <span className="text-[9px] text-white/30 uppercase tracking-wider font-medium">
   225	            Pressure
   226	          </span>
   227	          <span
   228	            className="text-2xl leading-none font-extrabold tabular-nums"
   229	            style={{ color: "#FFFFFF" }}
   230	          >
   231	            {confluencePct.toFixed(0)}%
   232	          </span>
   233	          <span className="text-[9px] text-white uppercase tracking-wide">{netDirectionLabel}</span>
   234	        </div>
   235	      </div>
   236	    </div>
   237	  )
   238	}

     4	This wrapper keeps the upstream Quality Playbook checkout as the source of
     5	truth, verifies the repo-local installed bundles, and avoids the quarantined
     6	Codex.app binary path by resolving `codex` from PATH.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import argparse
    12	import json
    13	import os
    14	import signal
    15	import shutil
    16	import subprocess
    17	import sys
    18	import tempfile
    19	from pathlib import Path
    20	from typing import Any
    21	
    22	
    23	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    24	CONFIG_PATHS = (
    25	    ROOT / "docs" / "runbooks" / "qplaybook_config.json",
    26	    ROOT / "quality" / "qplaybook_config.json",
    27	)
    28	BLOCKED_CODEX_PATH = Path("/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/codex")
    29	
    30	REQUIRED_BUNDLE_PATHS = (
    31	    "SKILL.md",
    32	    "quality_gate.py",
    33	    "references/constitution.md",
    34	    "references/phase1_exploration_guide.md",
    35	    "references/phase2_generation_guide.md",
    36	    "references/phase6_verify_guide.md",
    37	    "references/role_map_queries.md",
    38	    "references/runners_and_models.md",
    39	    "phase_prompts/phase1.md",
    40	    "phase_prompts/phase6.md",
    41	    "agents/quality-playbook.agent.md",
    42	    "bin/citation_verifier.py",
    43	    "bin/reference_docs_ingest.py",
    44	    "bin/benchmark_lib.py",
    45	)
    46	
    47	
    48	def load_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
    49	    for config_path in CONFIG_PATHS:
    50	        if config_path.is_file():
    51	            return json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    52	    searched = ", ".join(str(path.relative_to(ROOT)) for path in CONFIG_PATHS)
    53	    raise SystemExit(f"missing config; searched: {searched}")
    54	
    55	
    56	def run_cmd(
    57	    argv: list[str],
    58	    *,
    59	    cwd: Path = ROOT,
    60	    env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    61	    input_text: str | None = None,
    62	    timeout: int = 120,
    63	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]:
    64	    return subprocess.run(
    65	        argv,
    66	        cwd=str(cwd),
    67	        env=env,
    68	        input=input_text,
    69	        text=True,
    70	        capture_output=True,
    71	        timeout=timeout,
    72	        check=False,
    73	    )
    74	
    75	
    76	def print_result(label: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> bool:
    77	    status = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
    78	    suffix = f" - {detail}" if detail else ""
    79	    print(f"{status} {label}{suffix}")
    80	    return ok
    81	
    82	
    83	def normalized_origin(url: str) -> str:
    84	    value = url.strip()
    85	    if value.endswith(".git"):
    86	        value = value[:-4]
    87	    return value
    88	
    89	
    90	def check_repo_root() -> bool:
    91	    result = run_cmd(["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"])
    92	    top = Path(result.stdout.strip()).resolve() if result.stdout.strip() else None
    93	    return print_result("repo root", result.returncode == 0 and top == ROOT.resolve(), str(top or result.stderr.strip()))
    94	
    95	
    96	def check_source(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    97	    source = Path(config["source_repo"])
    98	    expected_origin = normalized_origin(config["source_origin"])
    99	    expected_commit = config["source_commit"]
   100	
   101	    ok_exists = print_result("QPB source checkout", (source / ".git").is_dir(), str(source))
   102	
   103	    origin = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "remote", "get-url", "origin"])
   104	    actual_origin = normalized_origin(origin.stdout)
   105	    ok_origin = print_result(
   106	        "QPB source origin",
   107	        origin.returncode == 0 and actual_origin == expected_origin,
   108	        actual_origin or origin.stderr.strip(),
   109	    )
   110	
   111	    head = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "rev-parse", "HEAD"])
   112	    actual_head = head.stdout.strip()
   113	    ok_head = print_result(
   114	        "QPB source commit",
   115	        head.returncode == 0 and actual_head == expected_commit,
   116	        actual_head or head.stderr.strip(),
   117	    )
   118	
   119	    status = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "status", "--short"])
   120	    ok_clean = print_result(
   121	        "QPB source clean",
   122	        status.returncode == 0 and not status.stdout.strip(),
   123	        status.stdout.strip() or "clean",
   124	    )
   125	
   126	    return ok_exists and ok_origin and ok_head and ok_clean
   127	
   128	
   129	def resolve_codex_binary(config: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   130	    configured = config.get("codex_binary", "codex")
   131	    if os.sep in configured:
   132	        resolved = Path(configured)
   133	    else:
   134	        found = shutil.which(configured)
   135	        if found is None:
   136	            return False, f"{configured} not found on PATH"
   137	        resolved = Path(found)
   138	
   139	    real = resolved.resolve()
   140	    blocked = real == BLOCKED_CODEX_PATH or str(real).startswith("/Applications/Codex.app/")
   141	    if blocked:
   142	        return False, f"blocked quarantined Codex.app path: {real}"
   143	
   144	    version = run_cmd([str(real), "--version"])
   145	    first_line = (version.stdout or version.stderr).strip().splitlines()
   146	    detail = first_line[0] if first_line else str(real)
   147	    ok_version = version.returncode == 0 and "codex-cli" in detail
   148	    if not ok_version:
   149	        return False, f"{real}: {detail}"
   150	    return True, f"{real} ({detail})"
   151	
   152	
   153	def check_codex(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   154	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   155	    return print_result("codex binary", ok, detail)
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_installed_bundle(root: Path, label: str) -> bool:
   159	    missing = [item for item in REQUIRED_BUNDLE_PATHS if not (root / item).is_file()]
   160	    ok_required = print_result(
   161	        f"{label} installed bundle",
   162	        not missing,
   163	        str(root) if not missing else "missing: " + ", ".join(missing),
   164	    )
   165	
   166	    nested_git = [path for path in root.rglob(".git") if path.exists()]
   167	    ok_no_git = print_result(
   168	        f"{label} no nested git",
   169	        not nested_git,
   170	        "none" if not nested_git else ", ".join(str(path) for path in nested_git),
   171	    )
   172	    return ok_required and ok_no_git
   173	
   174	
   175	def check_installs(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   176	    ok_all = True
   177	    for item in config["installed_skill_paths"]:
   178	        ok_all = check_installed_bundle(ROOT / item["path"], item["name"]) and ok_all
   179	    return ok_all
   180	
   181	
   182	def doctor(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   183	    checks = [
   184	        check_repo_root(),
   185	        check_source(config),
   186	        check_codex(config),
   187	        check_installs(config),
   188	    ]
   189	    return all(checks)
   190	
   191	
   192	def codex_full_access_args(config: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
   193	    return list(config.get("codex_exec_args", []))
   194	
   195	
   196	def profile_model(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> str:
   197	    try:
   198	        return str(config["profiles"][profile]["model"])
   199	    except KeyError as exc:
   200	        choices = ", ".join(sorted(config["profiles"]))
   201	        raise SystemExit(f"unknown profile {profile!r}; choose one of: {choices}") from exc
   202	
   203	
   204	def smoke_llm(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> bool:
   205	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   206	    if not ok:
   207	        return print_result(f"Codex smoke ({profile})", False, detail)
   208	
   209	    codex_path = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   210	    model = profile_model(config, profile)
   211	    expected = f"zinc-fusion-v16-qpb-{profile}-ok"
   212	    command = [
   213	        codex_path,
   214	        "exec",
   215	        *codex_full_access_args(config),
   216	        "-m",
   217	        model,
   218	        "-",
   219	    ]
   220	    result = run_cmd(
   221	        command,
   222	        input_text=f"Return exactly: {expected}\n",
   223	        timeout=120,
   224	    )
   225	    output = result.stdout.strip()
   226	    ok_smoke = result.returncode == 0 and expected in output
   227	    if not ok_smoke and result.stderr:
   228	        print(result.stderr[-2000:], file=sys.stderr)
   229	    return print_result(
   230	        f"Codex smoke ({profile})",
   231	        ok_smoke,
   232	        f"model={model}, returncode={result.returncode}, stdout={output[-120:]}",
   233	    )
   234	
   235	
   236	def cmd_doctor(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   237	    return 0 if doctor(load_config()) else 1
   238	
   239	
   240	def cmd_smoke(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   241	    config = load_config()
   242	    checks = [doctor(config)]
   243	    if args.no_llm:
   244	        print_result(f"Codex smoke ({args.profile})", True, "skipped by --no-llm")
   245	    else:
   246	        checks.append(smoke_llm(config, args.profile))
   247	    return 0 if all(checks) else 1
   248	
   249	
   250	def write_codex_shim(config: dict[str, Any], shim_dir: Path) -> Path:
   251	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   252	    if not ok:
   253	        raise SystemExit(detail)
   254	    real_codex = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   255	    full_args = codex_full_access_args(config)
   256	    shim_path = shim_dir / "codex"
   257	    shim_path.write_text(
   258	        "\n".join(
   259	            [
   260	                "#!/usr/bin/env python3",
   261	                "import os",
   262	                "import sys",
   263	                f"REAL_CODEX = {real_codex!r}",
   264	                f"FULL_ARGS = {full_args!r}",
   265	                "args = sys.argv[1:]",
   266	                "if args[:1] == ['exec']:",
   267	                "    rest = [arg for arg in args[1:] if arg != '--full-auto']",
   268	                "    os.execv(REAL_CODEX, [REAL_CODEX, 'exec', *FULL_ARGS, *rest])",
   269	                "os.execv(REAL_CODEX, [REAL_CODEX, *args])",
   270	                "",
   271	            ]
   272	        ),
   273	        encoding="utf-8",
   274	    )
   275	    shim_path.chmod(0o755)
   276	    return shim_path
   277	
   278	
   279	def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   280	    if not args.allow_quality_artifacts:
   281	        print(
   282	            "Refusing to run Quality Playbook phases without --allow-quality-artifacts. "
   283	            "This install is tooling-only by default.",
   284	            file=sys.stderr,
   285	        )
   286	        return 64
   287	
   288	    config = load_config()
   289	    if not doctor(config):
   290	        return 1
   291	
   292	    source = Path(config["source_repo"])
   293	    model = profile_model(config, args.profile)
   294	    qpb_args = list(args.qpb_args)
   295	    if qpb_args and qpb_args[0] == "--":
   296	        qpb_args = qpb_args[1:]
   297	
   298	    command = [
   299	        sys.executable,
   300	        "-m",
   301	        "bin.run_playbook",
   302	        "--codex",
   303	        "--model",
   304	        model,
   305	        *qpb_args,
   306	        str(ROOT),
   307	    ]
   308	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="zinc-fusion-v16-qpb-codex-") as shim:
   309	        shim_dir = Path(shim)
   310	        write_codex_shim(config, shim_dir)
   311	        env = os.environ.copy()
   312	        env["PATH"] = f"{shim_dir}{os.pathsep}{env.get('PATH', '')}"
   313	        result = subprocess.run(command, cwd=str(source), env=env, check=False)
   314	        return result.returncode
   315	
   316	
   317	def cmd_gate(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   318	    config = load_config()
   319	    timeout = int(config.get("quality_gate_timeout_seconds", 300))
   320	    gate = ROOT / ".claude" / "skills" / "quality-playbook" / "quality_gate.py"

    30	def load_qplaybook_module():
    31	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("qplaybook", QPLAYBOOK_PATH)
    32	    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    33	    assert spec.loader is not None
    34	    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    35	    return module
    36

    38	class QPlaybookWrapperContractTest(unittest.TestCase):
    39	    def test_config_pins_current_upstream_source(self):
    40	        config = json.loads((ROOT / "docs/runbooks/qplaybook_config.json").read_text())
    41	
    42	        self.assertEqual(config["source_version"], EXPECTED_VERSION)
    43	        self.assertEqual(config["source_commit"], EXPECTED_COMMIT)
    44	        self.assertIsInstance(config["quality_gate_timeout_seconds"], int)
    45	        self.assertGreater(config["quality_gate_timeout_seconds"], 0)
    46	
    47	    def test_wrapper_requires_v157_critical_bundle_files(self):
    48	        qplaybook = load_qplaybook_module()
    49	
    50	        self.assertTrue(CRITICAL_BUNDLE_FILES.issubset(set(qplaybook.REQUIRED_BUNDLE_PATHS)))
    51	
    52	    def test_installed_bundles_contain_v157_critical_files(self):

    54	            ROOT / ".claude/skills/quality-playbook",

    56	        ]
    57	
    58	        for install_root in install_roots:
    59	            with self.subTest(install_root=install_root):
    60	                for relative_path in sorted(CRITICAL_BUNDLE_FILES):
    61	                    self.assertTrue(
    62	                        (install_root / relative_path).is_file(),
    63	                        f"missing {relative_path} in {install_root}",
    64	                    )
    65	
    66	    def test_reference_docs_sentinel_tree_exists(self):
    67	        self.assertTrue((ROOT / "reference_docs/.gitkeep").is_file())
    68	        self.assertTrue((ROOT / "reference_docs/cite/.gitkeep").is_file())
    69	
    70	    def test_yarn_sentinel_tree_exists_for_upstream_runner(self):

    72	            ".yarn/patches/.gitkeep",
    73	            ".yarn/plugins/.gitkeep",
    74	            ".yarn/releases/.gitkeep",
    75	            ".yarn/versions/.gitkeep",
    76	        ):
    77	            with self.subTest(relative_path=relative_path):
    78	                self.assertTrue((ROOT / relative_path).is_file())
    79	
    80	    def test_yarn_gitignore_unignores_concrete_sentinel_files(self):

    82	
    83	        for relative_path in (
    84	            ".yarn/patches/.gitkeep",
    85	            ".yarn/plugins/.gitkeep",
    86	            ".yarn/releases/.gitkeep",
    87	            ".yarn/versions/.gitkeep",
    88	        ):
    89	            with self.subTest(relative_path=relative_path):
    90	                self.assertIn(f"!{relative_path}", gitignore)
    91	
    92	    def test_gate_timeout_returns_failure(self):
    93	        qplaybook = load_qplaybook_module()
    94	
    95	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:
    96	            temp_root = Path(temp_dir)
    97	            config_path = temp_root / "docs/runbooks/qplaybook_config.json"
    98	            gate_path = temp_root / ".claude/skills/quality-playbook/quality_gate.py"
    99	            config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True)
   100	            gate_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True)
   101	            config_path.write_text('{"quality_gate_timeout_seconds": 1}', encoding="utf-8")
   102	            gate_path.write_text(
   103	                "import time\n"
   104	                "time.sleep(30)\n",
   105	                encoding="utf-8",
   106	            )
   107	
   108	            old_root = qplaybook.ROOT
   109	            old_config_paths = qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS
   110	            try:
   111	                qplaybook.ROOT = temp_root
   112	                qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS = (config_path,)
   113	                result = qplaybook.cmd_gate(argparse.Namespace())
   114	            finally:
   115	                qplaybook.ROOT = old_root
   116	                qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS = old_config_paths
   117	
   118	        self.assertEqual(result, 124)

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba scripts/fusion_guard.py | sed -n '1,340p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Fail-closed guard runner for ZINC Fusion V16.
     3	
     4	The guard writes an audit log on every run and reports one final status:
     5	PASS only when every required check passes; INCOMPLETE when any check fails,
     6	is unavailable, times out, or is not run.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import argparse
    12	import json
    13	import subprocess
    14	import sys
    15	from dataclasses import dataclass
    16	from datetime import datetime, timezone
    17	from pathlib import Path
    18	
    19	
    20	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    21	LOG_ROOT = ROOT / "logs" / "fusion-guard"
    22	
    23	PASS = "PASS"
    24	FAIL = "FAIL"
    25	NOT_RUN = "NOT RUN"
    26	
    27	AUTHORITY_DOCS = (
    28	    "AGENTS.md",
    29	    "docs/INDEX.md",
    30	    "docs/MASTER_PLAN.md",
    31	    "docs/agent-safety-gates.md",
    32	    "docs/runbooks/session-startup.md",
    33	    "docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md",
    34	)
    35	
    36	PRODUCT_PREFIXES = (
    37	    "app/",
    38	    "components/",
    39	    "lib/",
    40	    "python/",
    41	    "scripts/",
    42	    "supabase/",
    43	    "package.json",
    44	    "package-lock.json",
    45	    "eslint.config.mjs",
    46	)
    47	
    48	DOC_PREFIXES = (
    49	    "AGENTS.md",
    50	    "docs/",
    51	    ".github/",
    52	    ".kilo/",
    53	)
    54	
    55	QUALITY_PREFIX = "quality/"
    56	
    57	SCAN_ROOTS = (
    58	    "AGENTS.md",
    59	    "package.json",
    60	    "eslint.config.mjs",
    61	    "docs",
    62	    "scripts",
    63	    "app",
    64	    "components",
    65	    "lib",
    66	    "python",
    67	    "supabase",
    68	    ".github",
    69	    ".kilo",
    70	    "quality",
    71	)
    72	
    73	
    74	@dataclass
    75	class Check:
    76	    name: str
    77	    status: str
    78	    detail: str
    79	
    80	    def line(self) -> str:
    81	        return f"{self.status}: {self.name} - {self.detail}"
    82	
    83	
    84	def utc_stamp() -> str:
    85	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
    86	
    87	
    88	def rel(path: Path) -> str:
    89	    try:
    90	        return str(path.relative_to(ROOT))
    91	    except ValueError:
    92	        return str(path)
    93	
    94	
    95	def run_cmd(argv: list[str], *, timeout: int) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
    96	    try:
    97	        result = subprocess.run(
    98	            argv,
    99	            cwd=ROOT,
   100	            text=True,
   101	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   102	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   103	            timeout=timeout,
   104	            check=False,
   105	        )
   106	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
   107	        return None, str(exc)
   108	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
   109	        output = exc.stdout or ""
   110	        if isinstance(output, bytes):
   111	            output = output.decode(errors="replace")
   112	        return -124, f"timeout after {timeout}s\n{output}"
   113	    return result.returncode, result.stdout
   114	
   115	
   116	def git_lines(args: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   117	    code, output = run_cmd(["git", *args], timeout=30)
   118	    if code != 0:
   119	        return []
   120	    return [line.strip() for line in output.splitlines() if line.strip()]
   121	
   122	
   123	def untracked_files() -> list[str]:
   124	    return git_lines(["ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"])
   125	
   126	
   127	def changed_files(mode: str) -> tuple[list[str], Check | None]:
   128	    if mode == "pre-commit":
   129	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   130	        return files, None
   131	
   132	    if mode == "pre-push":
   133	        upstream = git_lines(["rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "--symbolic-full-name", "@{upstream}"])
   134	        if not upstream:
   135	            return [], Check("upstream range", NOT_RUN, "no upstream configured; cannot check full push range")
   136	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR", f"{upstream[0]}...HEAD"])
   137	        return files, Check("upstream range", PASS, upstream[0])
   138	
   139	    files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   140	    files.extend(untracked_files())
   141	    return sorted(set(files)), None
   142	
   143	
   144	def has_prefix(path: str, prefixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   145	    return path in prefixes or any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in prefixes)
   146	
   147	
   148	def check_authority_docs() -> list[Check]:
   149	    checks: list[Check] = []
   150	    missing = [path for path in AUTHORITY_DOCS if not (ROOT / path).is_file()]
   151	    if missing:
   152	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", FAIL, "missing: " + ", ".join(missing)))
   153	    else:
   154	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", PASS, ", ".join(AUTHORITY_DOCS)))
   155	    return checks
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_changed_file_contract(files: list[str]) -> list[Check]:
   159	    checks: list[Check] = []
   160	    if not files:
   161	        checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, "no changed files in selected scope"))
   162	        return checks
   163	
   164	    product = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, PRODUCT_PREFIXES)]
   165	    docs = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, DOC_PREFIXES)]
   166	    quality = [path for path in files if path.startswith(QUALITY_PREFIX)]
   167	
   168	    checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, f"{len(files)} file(s) inspected"))
   169	
   170	    if product and not docs:
   171	        checks.append(
   172	            Check(
   173	                "contract-sync rule",
   174	                FAIL,
   175	                "product/config/tooling changes require docs/contracts in the same change",
   176	            )
   177	        )
   178	    else:
   179	        checks.append(Check("contract-sync rule", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, docs={len(docs)}"))
   180	
   181	    if product and not quality:
   182	        checks.append(
   183	            Check(
   184	                "quality-artifact sync",
   185	                FAIL,
   186	                "product/config/tooling changes require regenerated quality artifacts or explicit incomplete status",
   187	            )
   188	        )
   189	    else:
   190	        checks.append(Check("quality-artifact sync", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, quality={len(quality)}"))
   191	
   192	    if product and quality:
   193	        product_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in product if (ROOT / path).exists())
   194	        quality_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in quality if (ROOT / path).exists())
   195	        if product_mtime > quality_mtime:
   196	            checks.append(
   197	                Check(
   198	                    "quality-artifact freshness",
   199	                    FAIL,
   200	                    "source/config/tooling edits are newer than generated quality artifacts",
   201	                )
   202	            )
   203	        else:
   204	            checks.append(Check("quality-artifact freshness", PASS, "generated artifacts are not older than source edits"))
   205	
   206	    return checks
   207	
   208	
   209	def forbidden_terms() -> list[str]:
   210	    # Keep these assembled so the repository does not itself contain the banned tokens.
   211	    base = "dock" + "er"
   212	    return [base, base + "file", base + " compose", base + "-compose"]
   213	
   214	
   215	def check_forbidden_runtime_terms() -> Check:
   216	    matches: list[str] = []
   217	    candidates: list[Path] = []
   218	    for item in SCAN_ROOTS:
   219	        root = ROOT / item
   220	        if root.is_file():
   221	            candidates.append(root)
   222	        elif root.is_dir():
   223	            candidates.extend(path for path in root.rglob("*") if path.is_file())
   224	
   225	    ignored_dirs = {"node_modules", ".next", "Data", "data", "models", "logs", "__pycache__"}
   226	    for path in candidates:
   227	        if not path.is_file():
   228	            continue
   229	        parts = set(path.relative_to(ROOT).parts)
   230	        if parts & ignored_dirs:
   231	            continue
   232	        try:
   233	            text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   234	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
   235	            continue
   236	        lowered = text.lower()
   237	        for term in forbidden_terms():
   238	            if term in lowered:
   239	                matches.append(rel(path))
   240	                break
   241	    if matches:
   242	        return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", FAIL, "matches: " + ", ".join(sorted(set(matches))[:20]))
   243	    return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", PASS, "no forbidden runtime-stack terms found")
   244	
   245	
   246	def check_quality_finalizer() -> Check:
   247	    progress = ROOT / "quality" / "PROGRESS.md"
   248	    if not progress.is_file():
   249	        return Check("quality finalizer status", NOT_RUN, "quality/PROGRESS.md missing")
   250	    text = progress.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   251	    for phase in range(1, 7):
   252	        marker = f"- [x] Phase {phase}"
   253	        if marker not in text:
   254	            return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, f"missing {marker}")
   255	    if "Gate status: ABORTED" in text:
   256	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "latest workbook finalizer is ABORTED")
   257	    if "NOT_RUN" in text or "NOT RUN" in text:
   258	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "workbook contains NOT RUN evidence")
   259	    return Check("quality finalizer status", PASS, "all phases marked and no aborted/not-run markers")
   260	
   261	
   262	def command_check(name: str, argv: list[str], timeout: int, *, clean_gate: bool = False) -> Check:
   263	    code, output = run_cmd(argv, timeout=timeout)
   264	    if code is None:
   265	        return Check(name, NOT_RUN, output)
   266	    if code == -124:
   267	        return Check(name, FAIL, output.splitlines()[0])
   268	    if code != 0:
   269	        tail = "\n".join(output.splitlines()[-8:])
   270	        return Check(name, FAIL, f"exit {code}; {tail}")
   271	    if clean_gate and (" WARN" in output or "WARN:" in output or "WARN " in output):
   272	        return Check(name, FAIL, "warnings present; clean gate requires 0 WARN")
   273	    return Check(name, PASS, f"exit {code}")
   274	
   275	
   276	def pytest_check(timeout: int) -> list[Check]:
   277	    checks: list[Check] = []
   278	    version = command_check("pytest availability", ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "--version"], timeout)
   279	    checks.append(version)
   280	    if version.status != PASS:
   281	        return checks
   282	    checks.append(
   283	        command_check(
   284	            "pytest quality tests",
   285	            ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "quality/test_functional.py", "quality/test_regression.py"],
   286	            timeout,
   287	        )
   288	    )
   289	    return checks
   290	
   291	
   292	def full_gate_checks(args: argparse.Namespace) -> list[Check]:
   293	    checks = [
   294	        command_check("npm lint", ["npm", "run", "lint"], args.command_timeout),
   295	        command_check("npm build", ["npm", "run", "build"], args.build_timeout),
   296	        command_check("qplaybook doctor", ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "doctor"], args.command_timeout),
   297	        command_check(
   298	            "qplaybook smoke no-llm",
   299	            ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "smoke", "--profile", "code", "--no-llm"],
   300	            args.command_timeout,
   301	        ),
   302	    ]
   303	
   304	    mechanical = ROOT / "quality" / "mechanical" / "verify.sh"
   305	    if mechanical.is_file():
   306	        checks.append(command_check("quality mechanical verify", ["bash", str(mechanical)], args.command_timeout))
   307	    else:
   308	        checks.append(Check("quality mechanical verify", NOT_RUN, "quality/mechanical/verify.sh missing"))
   309	
   310	    gate = ROOT / ".claude" / "skills" / "quality-playbook" / "quality_gate.py"
   311	    if gate.is_file():
   312	        checks.append(command_check("quality gate clean", ["python3", str(gate), "."], args.command_timeout, clean_gate=True))
   313	    else:
   314	        checks.append(Check("quality gate clean", NOT_RUN, "installed quality_gate.py missing"))
   315	
   316	    checks.extend(pytest_check(args.command_timeout))
   317	    return checks
   318	
   319	
   320	def write_audit(mode: str, checks: list[Check], files: list[str]) -> Path:
   321	    LOG_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   322	    path = LOG_ROOT / f"{utc_stamp()}-{mode}.log"
   323	    status = final_status(checks)
   324	    payload = {
   325	        "timestamp": utc_stamp(),
   326	        "mode": mode,
   327	        "status": status,
   328	        "changed_files": files,
   329	        "checks": [check.__dict__ for check in checks],
   330	    }
   331	    lines = [
   332	        f"mode: {mode}",
   333	        f"status: {status}",
   334	        f"changed_files: {len(files)}",
   335	        "",
   336	        *[check.line() for check in checks],
   337	        "",
   338	        json.dumps(payload, indent=2),
   339	        "",
   340	    ]

   138	
   139	def load_local_env(path: Path) -> None:
   140	    if not path.exists():
   141	        return
   142	    for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
   143	        line = line.strip()
   144	        if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
   145	            continue
   146	        key, value = line.split("=", 1)
   147	        key = key.strip()
   148	        value = value.strip().strip("'").strip('"')
   149	        os.environ.setdefault(key, value)
   150	
   151	
   152	def ensure_db_url() -> str:
   153	    env_path = ROOT / ".env.local"
   154	    load_local_env(env_path)
   155	    for key in ("POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING", "DATABASE_URL", "SUPABASE_DB_URL"):
   156	        value = os.getenv(key)
   157	        if value:
   158	            return value
   159	    raise RuntimeError("Missing database URL in POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING/DATABASE_URL/SUPABASE_DB_URL")
   160	
   161	
   162	def request_text(url: str, *, timeout: int = 30) -> str:
   163	    last_error: Exception | None = None
   164	    for attempt in range(3):
   165	        try:
   166	            res = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=timeout)
   167	            res.raise_for_status()
   168	            return res.text
   169	        except Exception as err:  # noqa: BLE001
   170	            last_error = err
   171	            time.sleep(0.75 * (attempt + 1))
   172	    try:
   173	        # Some providers intermittently reset TLS streams for requests.
   174	        # Fallback to curl transport before failing the entire run.
   175	        proc = subprocess.run(
   176	            ["curl", "-fsSL", "--max-time", str(timeout), url],
   177	            check=True,
   178	            capture_output=True,
   179	            text=True,
   180	        )
   181	        if proc.stdout:
   182	            return proc.stdout
   183	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   184	        pass
   185	    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed request: {url} :: {last_error}")
   186	
   187	
   188	def request_json(url: str, *, timeout: int = 30) -> dict[str, Any] | list[Any]:
   189	    text = request_text(url, timeout=timeout)
   190	    return json.loads(text)
   191	
   192	
   193	def safe_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
   194	    try:
   195	        if value is None:
   196	            return None
   197	        n = float(value)
   198	        if math.isfinite(n):
   199	            return n
   200	        return None
   201	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   202	        return None
   203	
   204	
   205	def to_iso_z(dt: datetime | None) -> str | None:
   206	    if dt is None:
   207	        return None
   208	    return dt.astimezone(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   209	
   210	
   211	def dt_from_epoch(epoch_value: int | float | None) -> datetime | None:
   212	    if epoch_value is None:
   213	        return None
   214	    try:
   215	        return datetime.fromtimestamp(float(epoch_value), tz=timezone.utc)
   216	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   217	        return None
   218	
   219	
   220	def fetch_yahoo_series(symbol: str) -> YahooSeries:
   221	    query = urlencode({"interval": "1d", "range": "3mo"})
   222	    url = f"https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/{symbol}?{query}"
   223	    payload = request_json(url)
   224	    result = (
   225	        payload.get("chart", {})
   226	        .get("result", [{}])[0]
   227	    )
   228	    timestamps = result.get("timestamp", []) or []
   229	    close_values_raw = (
   230	        result.get("indicators", {})
   231	        .get("quote", [{}])[0]
   232	        .get("close", [])
   233	        or []
   234	    )
   235	    points: list[tuple[datetime, float]] = []
   236	    for ts, close in zip(timestamps, close_values_raw, strict=False):
   237	        close_val = safe_float(close)
   238	        dt = dt_from_epoch(ts)
   239	        if dt is None or close_val is None:
   240	            continue
   241	        points.append((dt, close_val))
   242	
   243	    if not points:
   244	        return YahooSeries(value=None, as_of=None, change_5d=None, close_values=[])
   245	
   246	    latest_dt, latest_close = points[-1]
   247	    close_values = [p[1] for p in points]
   248	    anchor_close: float | None = None
   249	    if len(close_values) >= 6:
   250	        anchor_close = close_values[-6]
   251	    change_5d = None
   252	    if anchor_close and anchor_close != 0:
   253	        change_5d = (latest_close - anchor_close) / anchor_close
   254	
   255	    return YahooSeries(
   256	        value=latest_close,
   257	        as_of=latest_dt,
   258	        change_5d=change_5d,
   259	        close_values=close_values,
   260	    )
   261	
   262	
   263	def fetch_fred_latest(series_id: str) -> tuple[float | None, datetime | None, list[tuple[date, float]]]:
   264	    url = f"https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id={series_id}"
   265	    text = request_text(url)
   266	    rows: list[tuple[date, float]] = []
   267	    for line in text.strip().splitlines()[1:]:
   268	        parts = line.split(",", 1)
   269	        if len(parts) != 2:
   270	            continue
   271	        d, v = parts[0].strip(), parts[1].strip()
   272	        if not d or not v or v == ".":
   273	            continue
   274	        try:
   275	            parsed_date = datetime.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
   276	            parsed_value = float(v)
   277	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   278	            continue
   279	        rows.append((parsed_date, parsed_value))
   280	    if not rows:
   281	        return None, None, []
   282	    latest_date, latest_value = rows[-1]
   283	    latest_dt = datetime.combine(latest_date, datetime.min.time(), tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   284	    return latest_value, latest_dt, rows
   285	
   286	
   287	def fetch_cftc_soybean_oil() -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   288	    params = {
   289	        "$where": "commodity_name='SOYBEAN OIL'",
   290	        "$order": "report_date_as_yyyy_mm_dd DESC",
   291	        "$limit": "6",
   292	    }
   293	    url = f"https://publicreporting.cftc.gov/resource/6dca-aqww.json?{urlencode(params)}"
   294	    payload = request_json(url)
   295	    if not isinstance(payload, list) or not payload:
   296	        return None
   297	    latest = payload[0]
   298	    obs_raw = latest.get("report_date_as_yyyy_mm_dd")
   299	    observation_date = None
   300	    if isinstance(obs_raw, str):
   301	        try:
   302	            observation_date = datetime.fromisoformat(obs_raw.replace("Z", "+00:00")).date()
   303	        except ValueError:
   304	            pass
   305	
   306	    noncomm_long = safe_float(latest.get("noncomm_positions_long_all")) or 0.0
   307	    noncomm_short = safe_float(latest.get("noncomm_positions_short_all")) or 0.0
   308	    open_interest = safe_float(latest.get("open_interest_all")) or 0.0
   309	    net = noncomm_long - noncomm_short
   310	    ratio = (net / open_interest) if open_interest > 0 else 0.0
   311	    if ratio >= 0.08:
   312	        bias = "bullish"
   313	    elif ratio <= -0.08:
   314	        bias = "bearish"
   315	    else:
   316	        bias = "neutral"
   317	
   318	    return {
   319	        "symbol": "ZL",
   320	        "observation_date": observation_date,
   321	        "bias": bias,
   322	        "managed_money_net": net,
   323	        "managed_money_ratio": ratio,
   324	        "open_interest": open_interest,
   325	        "payload": latest,
   326	    }

   328	
   329	def classify_tags(text: str) -> list[str]:
   330	    t = text.lower()
   331	    tags: set[str] = set()
   332	    if any(k in t for k in ("vix", "ovx", "volatility")):
   333	        tags.add("volatility")
   334	    if any(k in t for k in ("crush", "meal", "soybean", "soy oil", "soybean oil")):
   335	        tags.add("crush")
   336	    if "china" in t or "cny" in t:
   337	        tags.add("china")
   338	    if any(k in t for k in ("tariff", "trade policy", "trade", "duties")):
   339	        tags.add("tariff")
   340	    if any(k in t for k in ("crude", "oil", "energy", "diesel", "petroleum", "lng")):

    32	class TrainingContractError(RuntimeError):
    33	    pass

    36	LOCKED_PRESET = "best_quality"
    37	LOCKED_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS = 3600
    38	LOCKED_NUM_BAG_FOLDS = 5
    39	LOCKED_NUM_STACK_LEVELS = 1
    40	LOCKED_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE = "full_zoo"

    42	LOCKED_MIN_DIRECTIONAL_ACCURACY = 0.70
    43	MAX_DEFAULT_NUM_CPUS = 8
    44	MODEL_SELECTION_MODES = {"full_zoo", "include_only", "exclude_only"}
    45	TRAINING_SOURCE_MODES = {"local_postgres", "parquet_artifacts"}
    46	INTERNAL_MODEL_TYPE_EXCLUSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (

    57	def _parse_int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
    58	    value = os.getenv(name)
    59	    if value is None or not value.strip():
    60	        return default
    61	    return int(value)
    62	
    63	
    64	def _parse_float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
    65	    value = os.getenv(name)
    66	    if value is None or not value.strip():
    67	        return default
    68	    return float(value)
