    47	
    48	def load_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
    49	    for config_path in CONFIG_PATHS:
    50	        if config_path.is_file():
    51	            return json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    52	    searched = ", ".join(str(path.relative_to(ROOT)) for path in CONFIG_PATHS)
    53	    raise SystemExit(f"missing config; searched: {searched}")
    54	
    55	
    56	def run_cmd(
    57	    argv: list[str],
    58	    *,
    59	    cwd: Path = ROOT,
    60	    env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    61	    input_text: str | None = None,
    62	    timeout: int = 120,
    63	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]:
    64	    return subprocess.run(
    65	        argv,
    66	        cwd=str(cwd),
    67	        env=env,
    68	        input=input_text,
    69	        text=True,
    70	        capture_output=True,
    71	        timeout=timeout,
    72	        check=False,
    73	    )
    74	
    75	
    76	def print_result(label: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> bool:
    77	    status = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
    78	    suffix = f" - {detail}" if detail else ""
    79	    print(f"{status} {label}{suffix}")
    80	    return ok
    81	
    82	
    83	def normalized_origin(url: str) -> str:
    84	    value = url.strip()
    85	    if value.endswith(".git"):
    86	        value = value[:-4]
    87	    return value
    88	
    89	
    90	def check_repo_root() -> bool:
    91	    result = run_cmd(["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"])
    92	    top = Path(result.stdout.strip()).resolve() if result.stdout.strip() else None
    93	    return print_result("repo root", result.returncode == 0 and top == ROOT.resolve(), str(top or result.stderr.strip()))
    94	
    95	
    96	def check_source(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    97	    source = Path(config["source_repo"])
    98	    expected_origin = normalized_origin(config["source_origin"])
    99	    expected_commit = config["source_commit"]
   100	
   101	    ok_exists = print_result("QPB source checkout", (source / ".git").is_dir(), str(source))
   102	
   103	    origin = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "remote", "get-url", "origin"])
   104	    actual_origin = normalized_origin(origin.stdout)
   105	    ok_origin = print_result(
   106	        "QPB source origin",
   107	        origin.returncode == 0 and actual_origin == expected_origin,
   108	        actual_origin or origin.stderr.strip(),
   109	    )
   110	
   111	    head = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "rev-parse", "HEAD"])
   112	    actual_head = head.stdout.strip()
   113	    ok_head = print_result(
   114	        "QPB source commit",
   115	        head.returncode == 0 and actual_head == expected_commit,
   116	        actual_head or head.stderr.strip(),
   117	    )
   118	
   119	    status = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "status", "--short"])
   120	    ok_clean = print_result(
   121	        "QPB source clean",
   122	        status.returncode == 0 and not status.stdout.strip(),
   123	        status.stdout.strip() or "clean",
   124	    )
   125	
   126	    return ok_exists and ok_origin and ok_head and ok_clean
   127	
   128	
   129	def resolve_codex_binary(config: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   130	    configured = config.get("codex_binary", "codex")
   131	    if os.sep in configured:
   132	        resolved = Path(configured)
   133	    else:
   134	        found = shutil.which(configured)
   135	        if found is None:
   136	            return False, f"{configured} not found on PATH"
   137	        resolved = Path(found)
   138	
   139	    real = resolved.resolve()
   140	    blocked = real == BLOCKED_CODEX_PATH or str(real).startswith("/Applications/Codex.app/")
   141	    if blocked:
   142	        return False, f"blocked quarantined Codex.app path: {real}"
   143	
   144	    version = run_cmd([str(real), "--version"])
   145	    first_line = (version.stdout or version.stderr).strip().splitlines()
   146	    detail = first_line[0] if first_line else str(real)
   147	    ok_version = version.returncode == 0 and "codex-cli" in detail
   148	    if not ok_version:
   149	        return False, f"{real}: {detail}"
   150	    return True, f"{real} ({detail})"
   151	
   152	
   153	def check_codex(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   154	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   155	    return print_result("codex binary", ok, detail)
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_installed_bundle(root: Path, label: str) -> bool:
   159	    missing = [item for item in REQUIRED_BUNDLE_PATHS if not (root / item).is_file()]
   160	    ok_required = print_result(
   161	        f"{label} installed bundle",
   162	        not missing,
   163	        str(root) if not missing else "missing: " + ", ".join(missing),
   164	    )
   165	
   166	    nested_git = [path for path in root.rglob(".git") if path.exists()]
   167	    ok_no_git = print_result(
   168	        f"{label} no nested git",
   169	        not nested_git,
   170	        "none" if not nested_git else ", ".join(str(path) for path in nested_git),
   171	    )
   172	    return ok_required and ok_no_git
   173	
   174	
   175	def check_installs(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   176	    ok_all = True
   177	    for item in config["installed_skill_paths"]:
   178	        ok_all = check_installed_bundle(ROOT / item["path"], item["name"]) and ok_all
   179	    return ok_all
   180	
   181	
   182	def doctor(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   183	    checks = [
   184	        check_repo_root(),
   185	        check_source(config),
   186	        check_codex(config),
   187	        check_installs(config),
   188	    ]
   189	    return all(checks)
   190	
   191	
   192	def codex_full_access_args(config: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
   193	    return list(config.get("codex_exec_args", []))
   194	
   195	
   196	def profile_model(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> str:
   197	    try:
   198	        return str(config["profiles"][profile]["model"])
   199	    except KeyError as exc:
   200	        choices = ", ".join(sorted(config["profiles"]))
   201	        raise SystemExit(f"unknown profile {profile!r}; choose one of: {choices}") from exc
   202	
   203	
   204	def smoke_llm(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> bool:
   205	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   206	    if not ok:
   207	        return print_result(f"Codex smoke ({profile})", False, detail)
   208	
   209	    codex_path = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   210	    model = profile_model(config, profile)
   211	    expected = f"zinc-fusion-v16-qpb-{profile}-ok"
   212	    command = [
   213	        codex_path,
   214	        "exec",
   215	        *codex_full_access_args(config),
   216	        "-m",
   217	        model,
   218	        "-",
   219	    ]
   220	    result = run_cmd(
   221	        command,
   222	        input_text=f"Return exactly: {expected}\n",
   223	        timeout=120,
   224	    )
   225	    output = result.stdout.strip()
   226	    ok_smoke = result.returncode == 0 and expected in output
   227	    if not ok_smoke and result.stderr:
   228	        print(result.stderr[-2000:], file=sys.stderr)
   229	    return print_result(
   230	        f"Codex smoke ({profile})",
   231	        ok_smoke,
   232	        f"model={model}, returncode={result.returncode}, stdout={output[-120:]}",
   233	    )
   234	
   235	
   236	def cmd_doctor(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   237	    return 0 if doctor(load_config()) else 1
   238	
   239	
   240	def cmd_smoke(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   241	    config = load_config()
   242	    checks = [doctor(config)]
   243	    if args.no_llm:
   244	        print_result(f"Codex smoke ({args.profile})", True, "skipped by --no-llm")
   245	    else:
   246	        checks.append(smoke_llm(config, args.profile))
   247	    return 0 if all(checks) else 1
   248	
   249	
   250	def write_codex_shim(config: dict[str, Any], shim_dir: Path) -> Path:
   251	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   252	    if not ok:
   253	        raise SystemExit(detail)
   254	    real_codex = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   255	    full_args = codex_full_access_args(config)
   256	    shim_path = shim_dir / "codex"
   257	    shim_path.write_text(
   258	        "\n".join(
   259	            [
   260	                "#!/usr/bin/env python3",
   261	                "import os",
   262	                "import sys",
   263	                f"REAL_CODEX = {real_codex!r}",
   264	                f"FULL_ARGS = {full_args!r}",
   265	                "args = sys.argv[1:]",
   266	                "if args[:1] == ['exec']:",
   267	                "    rest = [arg for arg in args[1:] if arg != '--full-auto']",
   268	                "    os.execv(REAL_CODEX, [REAL_CODEX, 'exec', *FULL_ARGS, *rest])",
   269	                "os.execv(REAL_CODEX, [REAL_CODEX, *args])",
   270	                "",
   271	            ]
   272	        ),
   273	        encoding="utf-8",
   274	    )
   275	    shim_path.chmod(0o755)
   276	    return shim_path
   277	
   278	
   279	def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   280	    if not args.allow_quality_artifacts:
   281	        print(
   282	            "Refusing to run Quality Playbook phases without --allow-quality-artifacts. "
   283	            "This install is tooling-only by default.",
   284	            file=sys.stderr,
   285	        )
   286	        return 64
   287	
   288	    config = load_config()
   289	    if not doctor(config):
   290	        return 1
   291	
   292	    source = Path(config["source_repo"])
   293	    model = profile_model(config, args.profile)
   294	    qpb_args = list(args.qpb_args)
   295	    if qpb_args and qpb_args[0] == "--":
   296	        qpb_args = qpb_args[1:]
   297	
   298	    command = [
   299	        sys.executable,
   300	        "-m",
   301	        "bin.run_playbook",
   302	        "--codex",
   303	        "--model",
   304	        model,
   305	        *qpb_args,
   306	        str(ROOT),
   307	    ]
   308	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="zinc-fusion-v16-qpb-codex-") as shim:
   309	        shim_dir = Path(shim)
   310	        write_codex_shim(config, shim_dir)
   311	        env = os.environ.copy()
   312	        env["PATH"] = f"{shim_dir}{os.pathsep}{env.get('PATH', '')}"
   313	        result = subprocess.run(command, cwd=str(source), env=env, check=False)
   314	        return result.returncode
   315	
   316	
   317	def cmd_gate(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   318	    config = load_config()
   319	    timeout = int(config.get("quality_gate_timeout_seconds", 300))
   320	    gate = ROOT / ".claude" / "skills" / "quality-playbook" / "quality_gate.py"
   321	    if not gate.is_file():
   322	        print(f"missing gate: {gate}", file=sys.stderr)
   323	        return 1
   324	
   325	    process = subprocess.Popen(
   326	        [sys.executable, str(gate), "."],
   327	        cwd=str(ROOT),
   328	        text=True,
   329	        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   330	        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
   331	        start_new_session=True,
   332	    )
   333	    try:
   334	        stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=timeout)
   335	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
   336	        if exc.stdout:
   337	            print(exc.stdout, end="")
   338	        if exc.stderr:
   339	            print(exc.stderr, end="", file=sys.stderr)
   340	        try:
   341	            os.killpg(process.pid, signal.SIGKILL)
   342	        except ProcessLookupError:
   343	            pass
   344	        except OSError as kill_error:
   345	            print(f"FAIL quality gate timeout cleanup failed for pid {process.pid}: {kill_error}", file=sys.stderr)
   346	            return 124
   347	        try:
   348	            stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=5)
   349	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   350	            print(
   351	                f"FAIL quality gate timed out after {timeout}s and did not terminate cleanly; pid={process.pid}",
   352	                file=sys.stderr,
   353	            )
   354	            return 124
   355	        if stdout:
   356	            print(stdout, end="")
   357	        if stderr:
   358	            print(stderr, end="", file=sys.stderr)
   359	        print(f"FAIL quality gate timed out after {timeout}s", file=sys.stderr)
   360	        return 124

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Fail-closed guard runner for ZINC Fusion V16.
     3	
     4	The guard writes an audit log on every run and reports one final status:
     5	PASS only when every required check passes; INCOMPLETE when any check fails,
     6	is unavailable, times out, or is not run.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import argparse
    12	import json
    13	import subprocess
    14	import sys
    15	from dataclasses import dataclass
    16	from datetime import datetime, timezone
    17	from pathlib import Path
    18	
    19	
    20	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    21	LOG_ROOT = ROOT / "logs" / "fusion-guard"
    22	
    23	PASS = "PASS"
    24	FAIL = "FAIL"
    25	NOT_RUN = "NOT RUN"
    26	
    27	AUTHORITY_DOCS = (
    28	    "AGENTS.md",

    58	    "AGENTS.md",
    59	    "package.json",
    60	    "eslint.config.mjs",
    61	    "docs",
    62	    "scripts",
    63	    "app",
    64	    "components",
    65	    "lib",
    66	    "python",
    67	    "supabase",
    68	    ".github",
    69	    ".kilo",
    70	    "quality",
    71	)
    72	
    73	
    74	@dataclass
    75	class Check:
    76	    name: str
    77	    status: str
    78	    detail: str
    79	
    80	    def line(self) -> str:
    81	        return f"{self.status}: {self.name} - {self.detail}"
    82	
    83	
    84	def utc_stamp() -> str:
    85	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
    86	
    87	
    88	def rel(path: Path) -> str:
    89	    try:
    90	        return str(path.relative_to(ROOT))
    91	    except ValueError:
    92	        return str(path)
    93	
    94	
    95	def run_cmd(argv: list[str], *, timeout: int) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
    96	    try:
    97	        result = subprocess.run(
    98	            argv,
    99	            cwd=ROOT,
   100	            text=True,
   101	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   102	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   103	            timeout=timeout,
   104	            check=False,
   105	        )
   106	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
   107	        return None, str(exc)
   108	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
   109	        output = exc.stdout or ""
   110	        if isinstance(output, bytes):
   111	            output = output.decode(errors="replace")
   112	        return -124, f"timeout after {timeout}s\n{output}"
   113	    return result.returncode, result.stdout
   114	
   115	
   116	def git_lines(args: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   117	    code, output = run_cmd(["git", *args], timeout=30)
   118	    if code != 0:
   119	        return []
   120	    return [line.strip() for line in output.splitlines() if line.strip()]
   121	
   122	
   123	def untracked_files() -> list[str]:
   124	    return git_lines(["ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"])
   125	
   126	
   127	def changed_files(mode: str) -> tuple[list[str], Check | None]:
   128	    if mode == "pre-commit":
   129	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   130	        return files, None
   131	
   132	    if mode == "pre-push":
   133	        upstream = git_lines(["rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "--symbolic-full-name", "@{upstream}"])
   134	        if not upstream:
   135	            return [], Check("upstream range", NOT_RUN, "no upstream configured; cannot check full push range")
   136	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR", f"{upstream[0]}...HEAD"])
   137	        return files, Check("upstream range", PASS, upstream[0])
   138	
   139	    files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   140	    files.extend(untracked_files())
   141	    return sorted(set(files)), None
   142	
   143	
   144	def has_prefix(path: str, prefixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   145	    return path in prefixes or any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in prefixes)
   146	
   147	
   148	def check_authority_docs() -> list[Check]:
   149	    checks: list[Check] = []
   150	    missing = [path for path in AUTHORITY_DOCS if not (ROOT / path).is_file()]
   151	    if missing:
   152	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", FAIL, "missing: " + ", ".join(missing)))
   153	    else:
   154	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", PASS, ", ".join(AUTHORITY_DOCS)))
   155	    return checks
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_changed_file_contract(files: list[str]) -> list[Check]:
   159	    checks: list[Check] = []
   160	    if not files:
   161	        checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, "no changed files in selected scope"))
   162	        return checks
   163	
   164	    product = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, PRODUCT_PREFIXES)]
   165	    docs = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, DOC_PREFIXES)]
   166	    quality = [path for path in files if path.startswith(QUALITY_PREFIX)]
   167	
   168	    checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, f"{len(files)} file(s) inspected"))
   169	
   170	    if product and not docs:
   171	        checks.append(
   172	            Check(
   173	                "contract-sync rule",
   174	                FAIL,
   175	                "product/config/tooling changes require docs/contracts in the same change",
   176	            )
   177	        )
   178	    else:
   179	        checks.append(Check("contract-sync rule", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, docs={len(docs)}"))
   180	
   181	    if product and not quality:
   182	        checks.append(
   183	            Check(
   184	                "quality-artifact sync",
   185	                FAIL,
   186	                "product/config/tooling changes require regenerated quality artifacts or explicit incomplete status",
   187	            )
   188	        )
   189	    else:
   190	        checks.append(Check("quality-artifact sync", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, quality={len(quality)}"))
   191	
   192	    if product and quality:
   193	        product_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in product if (ROOT / path).exists())
   194	        quality_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in quality if (ROOT / path).exists())
   195	        if product_mtime > quality_mtime:
   196	            checks.append(
   197	                Check(
   198	                    "quality-artifact freshness",
   199	                    FAIL,
   200	                    "source/config/tooling edits are newer than generated quality artifacts",
   201	                )
   202	            )
   203	        else:
   204	            checks.append(Check("quality-artifact freshness", PASS, "generated artifacts are not older than source edits"))
   205	
   206	    return checks
   207	
   208	
   209	def forbidden_terms() -> list[str]:
   210	    # Keep these assembled so the repository does not itself contain the banned tokens.
   211	    base = "dock" + "er"
   212	    return [base, base + "file", base + " compose", base + "-compose"]
   213	
   214	
   215	def check_forbidden_runtime_terms() -> Check:
   216	    matches: list[str] = []
   217	    candidates: list[Path] = []
   218	    for item in SCAN_ROOTS:
   219	        root = ROOT / item
   220	        if root.is_file():
   221	            candidates.append(root)
   222	        elif root.is_dir():
   223	            candidates.extend(path for path in root.rglob("*") if path.is_file())
   224	
   225	    ignored_dirs = {"node_modules", ".next", "Data", "data", "models", "logs", "__pycache__"}
   226	    for path in candidates:
   227	        if not path.is_file():
   228	            continue
   229	        parts = set(path.relative_to(ROOT).parts)
   230	        if parts & ignored_dirs:
   231	            continue
   232	        try:
   233	            text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   234	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
   235	            continue
   236	        lowered = text.lower()
   237	        for term in forbidden_terms():
   238	            if term in lowered:
   239	                matches.append(rel(path))
   240	                break
   241	    if matches:
   242	        return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", FAIL, "matches: " + ", ".join(sorted(set(matches))[:20]))
   243	    return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", PASS, "no forbidden runtime-stack terms found")
   244	
   245	
   246	def check_quality_finalizer() -> Check:
   247	    progress = ROOT / "quality" / "PROGRESS.md"
   248	    if not progress.is_file():
   249	        return Check("quality finalizer status", NOT_RUN, "quality/PROGRESS.md missing")
   250	    text = progress.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   251	    for phase in range(1, 7):
   252	        marker = f"- [x] Phase {phase}"
   253	        if marker not in text:
   254	            return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, f"missing {marker}")
   255	    if "Gate status: ABORTED" in text:
   256	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "latest workbook finalizer is ABORTED")
   257	    if "NOT_RUN" in text or "NOT RUN" in text:
   258	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "workbook contains NOT RUN evidence")
   259	    return Check("quality finalizer status", PASS, "all phases marked and no aborted/not-run markers")
   260	
   261	
   262	def command_check(name: str, argv: list[str], timeout: int, *, clean_gate: bool = False) -> Check:
   263	    code, output = run_cmd(argv, timeout=timeout)
   264	    if code is None:
   265	        return Check(name, NOT_RUN, output)
   266	    if code == -124:
   267	        return Check(name, FAIL, output.splitlines()[0])
   268	    if code != 0:
   269	        tail = "\n".join(output.splitlines()[-8:])
   270	        return Check(name, FAIL, f"exit {code}; {tail}")
   271	    if clean_gate and (" WARN" in output or "WARN:" in output or "WARN " in output):
   272	        return Check(name, FAIL, "warnings present; clean gate requires 0 WARN")
   273	    return Check(name, PASS, f"exit {code}")
   274	
   275	
   276	def pytest_check(timeout: int) -> list[Check]:
   277	    checks: list[Check] = []
   278	    version = command_check("pytest availability", ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "--version"], timeout)
   279	    checks.append(version)
   280	    if version.status != PASS:
   281	        return checks
   282	    checks.append(
   283	        command_check(
   284	            "pytest quality tests",
   285	            ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "quality/test_functional.py", "quality/test_regression.py"],
   286	            timeout,
   287	        )
   288	    )
   289	    return checks
   290	
   291	
   292	def full_gate_checks(args: argparse.Namespace) -> list[Check]:
   293	    checks = [
   294	        command_check("npm lint", ["npm", "run", "lint"], args.command_timeout),
   295	        command_check("npm build", ["npm", "run", "build"], args.build_timeout),
   296	        command_check("qplaybook doctor", ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "doctor"], args.command_timeout),
   297	        command_check(
   298	            "qplaybook smoke no-llm",
   299	            ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "smoke", "--profile", "code", "--no-llm"],
   300	            args.command_timeout,
   301	        ),
   302	    ]
   303	
   304	    mechanical = ROOT / "quality" / "mechanical" / "verify.sh"
   305	    if mechanical.is_file():
   306	        checks.append(command_check("quality mechanical verify", ["bash", str(mechanical)], args.command_timeout))
   307	    else:
   308	        checks.append(Check("quality mechanical verify", NOT_RUN, "quality/mechanical/verify.sh missing"))
   309	
   310	    gate = ROOT / ".claude" / "skills" / "quality-playbook" / "quality_gate.py"
   311	    if gate.is_file():
   312	        checks.append(command_check("quality gate clean", ["python3", str(gate), "."], args.command_timeout, clean_gate=True))
   313	    else:
   314	        checks.append(Check("quality gate clean", NOT_RUN, "installed quality_gate.py missing"))
   315	
   316	    checks.extend(pytest_check(args.command_timeout))
   317	    return checks
   318	
   319	
   320	def write_audit(mode: str, checks: list[Check], files: list[str]) -> Path:
   321	    LOG_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   322	    path = LOG_ROOT / f"{utc_stamp()}-{mode}.log"
   323	    status = final_status(checks)
   324	    payload = {
   325	        "timestamp": utc_stamp(),
   326	        "mode": mode,
   327	        "status": status,
   328	        "changed_files": files,
   329	        "checks": [check.__dict__ for check in checks],
   330	    }
   331	    lines = [
   332	        f"mode: {mode}",
   333	        f"status: {status}",
   334	        f"changed_files: {len(files)}",
   335	        "",
   336	        *[check.line() for check in checks],
   337	        "",
   338	        json.dumps(payload, indent=2),
   339	        "",
   340	    ]
   341	    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
   342	    return path
   343	
   344	
   345	def final_status(checks: list[Check]) -> str:
   346	    if any(check.status != PASS for check in checks):
   347	        return "INCOMPLETE"
   348	    return PASS
   349	
   350	
   351	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
   352	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run ZINC Fusion V16 fail-closed guards.")
   353	    parser.add_argument("mode", choices=("pre-commit", "pre-push", "completion"))
   354	    parser.add_argument("--command-timeout", type=int, default=180)
   355	    parser.add_argument("--build-timeout", type=int, default=300)
   356	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   357	
   358	    files, range_check = changed_files(args.mode)
   359	    checks: list[Check] = []
   360	    if range_check is not None:
   361	        checks.append(range_check)
   362	    checks.extend(check_authority_docs())
   363	    checks.extend(check_changed_file_contract(files))
   364	    checks.append(check_forbidden_runtime_terms())
   365	
   366	    if args.mode in {"pre-push", "completion"}:
   367	        checks.append(check_quality_finalizer())
   368	        checks.extend(full_gate_checks(args))
   369	
   370	    audit = write_audit(args.mode, checks, files)
   371	
   372	    for check in checks:
   373	        print(check.line())
   374	    status = final_status(checks)
   375	    print(f"STATUS: {status}")
   376	    print(f"AUDIT_LOG: {rel(audit)}")
   377	    return 0 if status == PASS else 1
   378	
   379	
   380	if __name__ == "__main__":
   381	    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

    31	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("qplaybook", QPLAYBOOK_PATH)
    32	    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    33	    assert spec.loader is not None
    34	    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    35	    return module

     1	# ZINC Fusion V16 Master Plan
     2	
     3	## Canonical Plan
     4	
     5	The canonical build plan remains:
     6	
     7	`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`
     8	
     9	This file is the operational router. It does not replace the migration plan.
    10	
    11	## Current Completion Rule
    12	
    13	Completion is fail-closed:
    14	
    15	- Any failed required check means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    16	- Any required check recorded as `NOT RUN` means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    17	- Any aborted generated-quality finalizer means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    18	- Any warning in a required quality gate means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    19	- A standalone passing sub-gate does not override an incomplete full gate.
    20	
    21	## Current Quality State
    22	
    23	As of 2026-05-16, Quality Playbook artifacts exist and phases 1-6 were run.
    24	The run must still be treated as incomplete until the full completion guard
    25	passes with build, tests, quality gate, mechanical verification, source edit
    26	state, and generated artifact freshness all clean in the same run.
    27	
    28	## Required Work Loop
    29	
    30	1. Read authority docs from `docs/INDEX.md`.
    31	2. Inspect `git status --short`.
    32	3. Identify the active migration phase and gate from the canonical plan.
    33	4. Declare source lane and generated-artifact lane separately.
    34	5. Make the smallest scoped change.
    35	6. Update docs/contracts in the same change when behavior, config, schema, gate,
    36	   or operational truth changes.
    37	7. Regenerate quality artifacts after source/config/tooling changes that affect
    38	   quality evidence.
    39	8. Run the relevant guard:
    40	   - `npm run guard:pre-commit`
    41	   - `npm run guard:pre-push`
    42	   - `npm run guard:completion`
    43	9. Report `PASS`, `FAIL`, or `NOT RUN` per check. If any check is not `PASS`,
    44	   report `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    45	
    46	## Non-Negotiable Boundaries
    47	
    48	- Do not train models without explicit user approval.
    49	- Do not run Supabase migrations or `db push` without explicit approval.
    50	- Do not start local Supabase.
    51	- Do not use synthetic, placeholder, demo, or guessed data.
    52	- Do not copy legacy baseline code into V16.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Agent Safety Gates
     2	
     3	## Status Vocabulary
     4	
     5	Use only these closure states:
     6	
     7	| State | Meaning |
     8	| --- | --- |
     9	| `PASS` | The required command/check ran in this workspace and exited cleanly with no warning state. |
    10	| `FAIL` | The required command/check ran and failed, timed out, or reported an unsafe state. |
    11	| `NOT RUN` | The required check did not run because the runner, environment, credentials, or prerequisite was unavailable. |
    12	| `INCOMPLETE` | Final task status when any required check is `FAIL` or `NOT RUN`. |
    13	
    14	Do not use "complete" for work with `FAIL`, `NOT RUN`, warning-only gates, or
    15	aborted generated-quality finalizers.
    16	
    17	## Required Completion Gate
    18	
    19	`STATUS: PASS` requires all of the following in the same verification window:
    20	
    21	1. `npm run lint` passes.
    22	2. `npm run build` passes.
    23	3. Python test runner availability is proven and required tests run.
    24	4. Quality Playbook doctor and smoke checks pass.
    25	5. `quality/mechanical/verify.sh` passes when quality artifacts exist.
    26	6. Installed Quality Playbook `quality_gate.py` passes with zero warnings.
    27	7. Quality Playbook finalizer is not aborted.
    28	8. Source edits and generated `quality/` artifacts are in sync.
    29	9. Behavior, config, schema, gate, and operational-truth changes update docs or
    30	   contracts in the same change.
    31	
    32	If any item is missing, failed, timed out, unavailable, or warning-only, report:
    33	
    34	`STATUS: INCOMPLETE`
    35	
    36	## Source Lane vs Generated Lane
    37	
    38	Treat these as separate lanes:
    39	
    40	- Source lane: `app/`, `components/`, `lib/`, `python/`, `scripts/`,
    41	  `supabase/`, package/config files, and authority docs.
    42	- Generated lane: `quality/` artifacts and generated quality receipts.
    43	
    44	Rules:
    45	
    46	1. Source edits can invalidate generated quality artifacts.
    47	2. Generated artifacts do not prove source quality unless they were regenerated
    48	   after the source edits they describe.
    49	3. Do not manually clean generated artifacts to make string scans pass. Regenerate
    50	   artifacts or mark the quality lane stale.
    51	4. Guard audit logs are written under `logs/fusion-guard/` and are local runtime
    52	   evidence, not source-of-truth docs.
    53	
    54	## Guard Commands
    55	
    56	Use these commands:
    57	
    58	```bash
    59	npm run guard:pre-commit
    60	npm run guard:pre-push
    61	npm run guard:completion
    62	```
    63	
    64	`guard:pre-commit` checks staged file scope and contract synchronization.
    65	`guard:pre-push` checks the upstream range and full gates.
    66	`guard:completion` checks the current workspace and full gates.
    67	
    68	The guard scripts are intentionally fail-closed. A nonzero guard exit means the
    69	status is incomplete until the blocker is resolved and the guard is rerun.

    36	## Hard Rules
    37	
    38	### Architecture Rules
    39	
    40	1. **11 specialists — NEVER say 10.** The Big-11: crush, china, fx, fed, tariff, energy, biofuel, palm, volatility, substitutes, trump_effect.
    41	2. **Target = future PRICE LEVEL** (`close.shift(-horizon)`), columns named `target_price_{h}d`. Never returns.
    42	3. **Target Zones = horizontal lines** at price levels. NEVER say: cones, bands, funnels, confidence intervals.
    43	4. **Probability language:** "ZL has an X% chance of hitting XX.XX by [date]" — derived from Monte Carlo (10k runs) + pinball loss + MAE/accuracy %.
    44	5. **No Inngest. No Vercel Cron.** All scheduling via Supabase pg_cron + `http` extension. Vercel is frontend hosting ONLY.
    45	6. **9 schemas:** mkt, econ, alt, supply, training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas. No others.
    46	7. **ProFarmer is mandatory** ($500/month). Rebuilt as Python Playwright scraper, not Node.js Puppeteer.
    47	8. **Training gate:** NEVER start model training without explicit user approval.
    48	9. **Chart is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — zero modifications to behavior. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    49	10. **Landing page is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — preserve the design identity. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    50	11. **ZERO mock data.** No placeholders, no temps, no demo/synthetic/random data anywhere, ever. Empty state until real data flows. This is the HARDEST rule.
    51	12. **ZERO code copying.** Every line of V16 is written fresh. legacy baseline is a visual reference only. Clone-and-clean failed catastrophically — never again.
    52	13. **Cloud Supabase only.** Cloud Supabase is canonical. Supabase CLI is for cloud migrations (`db push`) only. No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. Local PostgreSQL is permitted only as the AG training-source staging database, never as canonical warehouse or local Supabase replacement.
    53	14. **No hardcoded port 3000.** Dev server port must be checked for availability first.
    54	15. **Design holdoff exception (locked 2026-05-07).** For page parity work, do not redesign. Reproduce locked source visuals exactly.
    55	16. **Locked page authority map (2026-05-07):** Strategy=V16, Vegas Intel=V16, Dashboard=V15, Legislation=V15, Sentiment=V15.
    56	17. **Global layout lock:** Full-width geometry is mandatory on ALL V16 pages. Do not keep narrow/containerized widths.
    57	18. **Pixel parity lock:** Typography, spacing, shadows, border/radius, gradients, hex colors, and all interaction states must match locked source exactly.
    58	19. **Cadence pivot lock:** Most non-price data moves to weekly batch ingest and weekly retraining; hourly chart freshness remains.
    59	20. **GPT content lock:** Sentiment and Legislation pipelines are GPT-driven from approved source feeds with traceable provenance.
    60	21. **AI-first cards lock (2026-05-08):** Dashboard/strategy/sentiment/legislation/vegas cards are primarily AI snapshot driven; avoid request-time external data pulls in API handlers.
    61	22. **Weekly pull lock (2026-05-08):** Non-price card refresh jobs run weekly cadence by default; AG training runs by manual batch trigger only unless explicitly changed.
    62	23. **Source reduction lock (2026-05-08):** Keep only strict high-impact feeds (FOMC/FED, ProFarmer, FRED aggregate series, constrained weather stations, core energy/FX/volatility signals).
    63	24. **Symbol budget lock (2026-05-08):** Maintain a compact universe (target ~20-30 symbols); do not reopen 50+ symbol spread without explicit approval.
    64	
    65	### Process Rules
    66	
    67	1. **Read the migration plan before touching code.** It defines every table, route, job, and phase.
    68	2. **Follow the phase order.** Phase 0 before Phase 1. Phase 1 before Phase 2. No skipping.
    69	3. **Run evaluation gates.** Each phase has specific checks. Don't declare done until they pass.
    70	4. **One task at a time.** Finish what was asked before touching anything else.
    71	5. **No "while I'm here" refactors.** Stay scoped.
    72	6. **Memory first.** Search memory at session start, store decisions immediately.
    73	7. **Brainstorm before building.** Use the superpowers brainstorming skill for any non-trivial feature.
    74	8. **Verify before claiming done.** Use the superpowers verification skill.
    75	9. **Checkpoint before implementation.** For non-trivial planning work, audit repo reality first, structure the plan as numbered decision checkpoints, run one Ralph Loop per checkpoint, and update docs after each locked decision.
    76	10. **Fail-closed completion.** Read `docs/INDEX.md`, `docs/MASTER_PLAN.md`, and `docs/agent-safety-gates.md` at startup. If any required check is `FAIL`, `NOT RUN`, warning-only, or aborted, report `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.

   123	
   124	## Key Supabase Patterns
   125	
   126	### Connection Strategy
   127	
   128	```
   129	Frontend (reads):      Supabase JS client with anon key + JWT
   130	Data ingestion:        pg_cron + http extension (runs inside Postgres — no external connection)
   131	Python (reads):        psycopg2 pooled connection to cloud (port 6543)
   132	Python (training src): local PostgreSQL staging DB for AG training only
   133	Python (promotes):     psycopg2 direct connection to cloud (port 5432) — validated outputs only
   134	Python (intermediates): local parquet files — never written to any database
   135	```
   136	
   137	### Migration Pattern
   138	
   139	```bash
   140	# Create a new migration
   141	supabase migration new <name>
   142	
   143	# Push directly to cloud (no local Supabase needed)
   144	supabase db push
   145	
   146	# Diff against cloud
   147	supabase db diff --linked
   148	```
   149	
   150	Never do manual DDL on cloud. Migrations are the single source of truth. No `supabase status`, no `supabase start`, and no local Supabase workflow.
   151	
   152	### Data Ingestion Pattern (pg_cron + http extension)
   153	
   154	All data ingestion runs inside Postgres as plpgsql functions:
   155	
   156	1. pg_cron triggers the function on schedule
   157	2. `http_get()` fetches from external API (synchronous, in-transaction)
   158	3. Parse JSON response in plpgsql
   159	4. UPSERT to target table
   160	5. Log to `ops.ingest_run`
   161	6. API keys from Supabase Vault via `current_setting()`
   162	
   163	No Vercel cron routes. No CRON_SECRET. No serverless functions for ingestion.
   164	
   165	### RLS Pattern
   166	
   167	```sql
   168	-- Enable RLS
   169	ALTER TABLE schema.table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   170	
   171	-- Authenticated users can read
   172	CREATE POLICY "authenticated_read" ON schema.table
   173	  FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
   174	
   175	-- Only service_role can write
   176	CREATE POLICY "service_role_write" ON schema.table
   177	  FOR ALL TO service_role USING (true);
   178	```
   179	
   180	---
   181	
   182	## Tips From Legacy Baseline Experience
   183	
   184	These are hard-won lessons. Don't repeat them.
   185	
   186	### Data Source Gotchas
   187	
   188	- **FRED daily** only fetches `limit=5` (latest obs). Full history needs `refresh_fred_api.py`.
   189	- **EIA API** has been intermittently down since Mar 2026. Build with graceful fallback.
   190	- **MPOB Palm** needs a valid FAS OpenData API key. The legacy baseline key was wrong (FoodData Central, not FAS).
   191	- **Yahoo Finance v8** downsamples to monthly for large date ranges. Use 1-year windows with `period1/period2`.
   192	- **FAS site** (fas.usda.gov) returns HTTP/2 stream errors. Needs retry logic.
   193	- **UCO/Tallow** prices: no free direct API. Use FRED PPI proxies: `WPU06410132` (Tallow PPI) + `PCU3116133116132` (Rendering PPI).
   194	
   195	### AutoGluon Gotchas
   196	
   197	- `TimeSeriesPredictor.load(path, require_version_match=False)` — version check bug fires even when versions match.
   198	- All covariates are OBSERVED, not KNOWN. This limits Chronos2 effectiveness.
   199	- CPU-only on macOS ARM. Deep learning models (DeepAR, TFT, etc.) are disabled.
   200	- Training the 19-model zoo takes significant time. Always `--dry-run` first.
   201	
   202	### Frontend Gotchas
   203	
   204	- The chart uses `lightweight-charts` (TradingView). Settings are precise — don't modify them.
   205	- `ForecastTargetsPrimitive` is a custom series primitive for drawing horizontal Target Zone lines.
   206	- Pivot labels use format `D(P)`, `D(R1)`, `D(S1)`, `W(P)`, `M(P)`, `Y(P)`.
   207	- The NeuralSphere on the landing page uses Three.js + `head.glb`. Resource-heavy but intentional.
   208	
   209	### Supabase-Specific Tips
   210	
   211	- Use `supabase db diff` to check for schema drift between local and cloud.
   212	- Connection pooler (port 6543) has a statement timeout. Use direct (5432) for long writes.
   213	- `pg_cron` jobs run as the `postgres` role. Make sure your functions/procedures are owned by `postgres`.
   214	- Supabase Auth JWTs expire after 1 hour by default. Configure refresh behavior in the client.
   215

   222	1. **Events calendar** — CES, SEMA, March Madness, conventions. Links events to oil demand spikes.

   282	
   283	## Agent Persona & Principles
   284	
   285	You are the lead data architect on many projects that involve data, design, engineering, physics and marketing.. You are an expert in database schema design, API architecture, data relationships, and modeling best practices. You care deeply about doing things the right way — no shortcuts, no sloppy schemas, no "we'll fix it later" compromises.
   286	Your principles:
   287	
   288	Normalization matters. Every table, every relationship, every constraint should have a clear reason to exist.
   289	Naming conventions are consistent and intentional — no ambiguity, no abbreviation soup.
   290	You think in terms of how the data actually flows through the system, not just how it sits at rest.
   291	API design follows from the data model, not the other way around. Get the model right and the API contracts become obvious.
   292	You document your reasoning. When you make a design decision, you explain why — not just what.
   293	You ask questions when something is ambiguous rather than assuming.
   294	
   295	You work systematically and methodically. Step one before step two. You don't jump to implementation. You inventory what exists, identify gaps, map relationships, validate assumptions, and then you design.
   296	
   297	You never cut corners. Not on naming. Not on constraints. Not on relationships. Not on documentation. If something feels like a shortcut, it is, and you don't take it. A half-built schema is worse than no schema — it's a lie baked into the foundation.
   298	You don't fake work. If you're unsure about something, you say so and go find the answer. You don't guess and dress it up as confidence. Honesty about what you know and don't know is how trust gets built.
   299	You document your reasoning as you go. Every design decision gets a why, not just a what. Six months from now, someone (probably you) needs to understand the thinking behind every table, every foreign key, every index.
   300	
   301	Your process — every time, no exceptions:
   302	
   303	Explore — Read the full codebase. Understand what exists. Map the current state.
   304	Inventory — Document what you found. What entities exist? What relationships? What's missing? What's broken?
   305	Clarify — Only now do you bring questions to me, and they should be sharp, specific questions that show you've already done your homework.
   306	Design — Propose the model. Show the schema, the relationships, the constraints. Explain every decision.
   307	Validate — Stress-test your own design. What breaks? What edge cases exist? What happens at scale?
   308	Implement — Build it right. Migrations, seed data, documentation — the whole thing, not just the pretty diagram.
   309	
   310	## ZINC Fusion V16 Ralph Loop Planning Standard
   311	
   312	For this repository only, every new or revised plan must follow [`plans/zinc-fusion-v16-ralph-loop-workflow-guide.md`](plans/zinc-fusion-v16-ralph-loop-workflow-guide.md) by default.
   313	
   314	Mandatory planning defaults:
   315	
   316	1. Audit repository reality before making architecture or refactoring decisions.
   317	2. Write plan documents as numbered checkpoints that capture decisions, not implementation tasks.
   318	3. Run one Ralph Loop per checkpoint and write a decision document for each checkpoint.
   319	4. Treat [`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md) as the canonical build plan. If a small plan or checkpoint note is needed, integrate it back into the canonical plan rather than letting it become a competing source of truth.
   320	5. Update canonical planning docs and [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) whenever a checkpoint changes verified ground truth.
   321	6. Implement only after all checkpoint decisions are locked.
   322	7. Keep all naming scoped to ZINC Fusion V16 in this repository. Do not introduce or reuse `external project` naming, references, or examples here.
   323	
   324	### Reasoning Guardrails
   325	
   326	- Prefer less complexity, fewer moving parts, and better naming.
   327	- Do not preserve old paths just because they already exist.
   328	- Do not keep both old and new paths alive unless there is a clear migration reason.
   329	- Do not let ephemeral live-feed logic become retained training truth.
   330	- Do not silently increase vendor cost exposure.
   331	- Do not introduce weak names like `v2`, `new`, `final`, or `tmp`.
   332	- Do not add any dependency, extension, or paid-plan assumption without an explicit reason.
   333	- Ground decisions in repo reality, not aspirational docs.
   334	
   335	---
   336	
   337	## Skills
   338	
   339	For Kilo, nine structured audit and planning skills live in `.kilo/skills/`. Treat `.kilo/` as the shared source of truth for Kilo rules, skills, and workflows. Each skill has a full loop-based workflow with approval gates, commit intent gates, and Hard Rules. Read the full SKILL.md before starting any skill — do not shortcut the loops.
   340

     2	  <img alt="Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit - the fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase" src="https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/opengraph-image.png">
     3	  <h1 align="center">Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit</h1>
     4	</a>
     5	
     6	<p align="center">
     7	 The fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase
     8	</p>
     9	
    10	<p align="center">
    11	  <a href="#features"><strong>Features</strong></a> ·
    12	  <a href="#demo"><strong>Demo</strong></a> ·
    13	  <a href="#deploy-to-vercel"><strong>Deploy to Vercel</strong></a> ·
    14	  <a href="#clone-and-run-locally"><strong>Clone and run locally</strong></a> ·

    17	</p>
    18	<br/>
    19	
    20	## Features
    21	
    22	- Works across the entire [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) stack
    23	  - App Router
    24	  - Pages Router
    25	  - Proxy
    26	  - Client
    27	  - Server
    28	  - It just works!
    29	- supabase-ssr. A package to configure Supabase Auth to use cookies
    30	- Password-based authentication block installed via the [Supabase UI Library](https://supabase.com/ui/docs/nextjs/password-based-auth)
    31	- Styling with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com)
    32	- Components with [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/)
    33	- Optional deployment with [Supabase Vercel Integration and Vercel deploy](#deploy-your-own)
    34	  - Environment variables automatically assigned to Vercel project
    35	
    36	## Demo
    37	
    38	You can view a fully working demo at [demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app](https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/).
    39	
    40	## Deploy to Vercel
    41	
    42	Vercel deployment will guide you through creating a Supabase account and project.
    43	
    44	After installation of the Supabase integration, all relevant environment variables will be assigned to the project so the deployment is fully functioning.
    45	
    46	[![Deploy with Vercel](https://vercel.com/button)](https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&project-name=nextjs-with-supabase&repository-name=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-title=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-description=This+starter+configures+Supabase+Auth+to+use+cookies%2C+making+the+user%27s+session+available+throughout+the+entire+Next.js+app+-+Client+Components%2C+Server+Components%2C+Route+Handlers%2C+Server+Actions+and+Middleware.&demo-url=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2F&external-id=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&demo-image=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2Fopengraph-image.png)
    47	
    48	The above will also clone the Starter kit to your GitHub, you can clone that locally and develop locally.
    49	
    50	If you wish to just develop locally and not deploy to Vercel, [follow the steps below](#clone-and-run-locally).
    51	
    52	## Clone and run locally
    53	
    54	1. You'll first need a Supabase project which can be made [via the Supabase dashboard](https://database.new)
    55	
    56	2. Create a Next.js app using the Supabase Starter template npx command
    57	
    58	   ```bash
    59	   npx create-next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    60	   ```
    61	
    62	   ```bash
    63	   yarn create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    64	   ```
    65	
    66	   ```bash
    67	   pnpm create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    68	   ```
    69	
    70	3. Use `cd` to change into the app's directory
    71	
    72	   ```bash
    73	   cd with-supabase-app
    74	   ```
    75	
    76	4. Rename `.env.example` to `.env.local` and update the following:
    77	
    78	  ```env
    79	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT URL]
    80	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT API PUBLISHABLE OR ANON KEY]
    81	  ```
    82	  > [!NOTE]
    83	  > This example uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, which refers to Supabase's new **publishable** key format.
    84	  > Both legacy **anon** keys and new **publishable** keys can be used with this variable name during the transition period. Supabase's dashboard may show `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`; its value can be used in this example.
    85	  > See the [full announcement](https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/29260) for more information.
    86	
    87	  Both `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` can be found in [your Supabase project's API settings](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/_?showConnect=true)
    88	
    89	5. You can now run the Next.js local development server:
    90	
    91	   ```bash
    92	   npm run dev
    93	   ```
    94	
    95	   The starter kit should now be running on [localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/).
    96	
    97	6. This template comes with the default shadcn/ui style initialized. If you instead want other ui.shadcn styles, delete `components.json` and [re-install shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/installation/next)
    98	
    99	> Check out [the docs for Local Development](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/local-development) to also run Supabase locally.
   100	
   101	## Feedback and issues
   102	
   103	Please file feedback and issues over on the [Supabase GitHub org](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/new/choose).
   104

   106	
   107	- [Next.js Subscription Payments Starter](https://github.com/vercel/nextjs-subscription-payments)

   109	- [Supabase Auth and the Next.js App Router](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/tree/master/examples/auth/nextjs)

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba components/viz/NeuralSphere.tsx | sed -n '1,360p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	'use client';
     2	
     3	import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';
     4	import * as THREE from 'three';
     5	
     6	interface NeuralSphereProps {
     7	  size?: number;
     8	  color?: string;
     9	  particleColor?: string;
    10	  wireframeOpacity?: number;
    11	  particleCount?: number;
    12	  rotationSpeed?: number;
    13	}
    14	
    15	/**
    16	 * NeuralSphere - A rotating neural network sphere visualization
    17	 * Based on AlgoSensei template design
    18	 */
    19	export function NeuralSphere({ 
    20	  size = 400,
    21	  color = '#2962ff',
    22	  particleColor = '#00d4ff',
    23	  wireframeOpacity = 0.3,
    24	  particleCount = 200,
    25	  rotationSpeed = 0.002,
    26	}: NeuralSphereProps) {
    27	  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    28	  const rendererRef = useRef<THREE.WebGLRenderer | null>(null);
    29	
    30	  useEffect(() => {
    31	    const container = containerRef.current;
    32	    if (!container) return;
    33	
    34	    // Scene setup
    35	    const scene = new THREE.Scene();
    36	    const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(75, 1, 0.1, 1000);
    37	    camera.position.z = 2.5;
    38	
    39	    // Renderer
    40	    const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({
    41	      antialias: true,
    42	      alpha: true
    43	    });
    44	    renderer.setSize(size, size);
    45	    renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2));
    46	    container.appendChild(renderer.domElement);
    47	    rendererRef.current = renderer;
    48	
    49	    // Convert hex to THREE color
    50	    const mainColor = new THREE.Color(color);
    51	    const pColor = new THREE.Color(particleColor);
    52	
    53	    // Wireframe sphere
    54	    const sphereGeometry = new THREE.IcosahedronGeometry(1, 2);
    55	    const wireframeMaterial = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({
    56	      color: mainColor,
    57	      wireframe: true,
    58	      transparent: true,
    59	      opacity: wireframeOpacity,
    60	    });
    61	    const sphere = new THREE.Mesh(sphereGeometry, wireframeMaterial);
    62	    scene.add(sphere);
    63	
    64	    // Inner glow sphere
    65	    const innerGeometry = new THREE.IcosahedronGeometry(0.95, 2);
    66	    const innerMaterial = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({
    67	      color: pColor,
    68	      wireframe: true,
    69	      transparent: true,
    70	      opacity: wireframeOpacity * 0.5,
    71	    });
    72	    const innerSphere = new THREE.Mesh(innerGeometry, innerMaterial);
    73	    scene.add(innerSphere);
    74	
    75	    // Floating particles
    76	    const particlesGeometry = new THREE.BufferGeometry();
    77	    const positions = new Float32Array(particleCount * 3);
    78	    
    79	    for (let i = 0; i < particleCount; i++) {
    80	      const theta = Math.random() * Math.PI * 2;
    81	      const phi = Math.acos(2 * Math.random() - 1);
    82	      const r = 1.2 + Math.random() * 0.5;
    83	      
    84	      positions[i * 3] = r * Math.sin(phi) * Math.cos(theta);
    85	      positions[i * 3 + 1] = r * Math.sin(phi) * Math.sin(theta);
    86	      positions[i * 3 + 2] = r * Math.cos(phi);
    87	    }
    88	    
    89	    particlesGeometry.setAttribute('position', new THREE.BufferAttribute(positions, 3));
    90	    
    91	    const particlesMaterial = new THREE.PointsMaterial({
    92	      color: pColor,
    93	      size: 0.02,
    94	      transparent: true,
    95	      opacity: 0.6,
    96	    });
    97	    
    98	    const particles = new THREE.Points(particlesGeometry, particlesMaterial);
    99	    scene.add(particles);
   100	
   101	    // Animation
   102	    let animationId: number;
   103	    const animate = () => {
   104	      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(animate);
   105	      
   106	      sphere.rotation.x += rotationSpeed;
   107	      sphere.rotation.y += rotationSpeed * 1.5;
   108	      
   109	      innerSphere.rotation.x -= rotationSpeed * 0.5;
   110	      innerSphere.rotation.y -= rotationSpeed;
   111	      
   112	      particles.rotation.y += rotationSpeed * 0.5;
   113	      
   114	      renderer.render(scene, camera);
   115	    };
   116	    animate();
   117	
   118	    // Cleanup
   119	    return () => {
   120	      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
   121	      renderer.dispose();
   122	      sphereGeometry.dispose();
   123	      wireframeMaterial.dispose();
   124	      innerGeometry.dispose();
   125	      innerMaterial.dispose();
   126	      particlesGeometry.dispose();
   127	      particlesMaterial.dispose();
   128	      if (container && renderer.domElement) {
   129	        container.removeChild(renderer.domElement);
   130	      }
   131	    };
   132	  }, [size, color, particleColor, wireframeOpacity, particleCount, rotationSpeed]);
   133	
   134	  return (
   135	    <div 
   136	      ref={containerRef} 
   137	      style={{ 
   138	        width: size, 
   139	        height: size,
   140	        display: 'flex',
   141	        alignItems: 'center',
   142	        justifyContent: 'center',
   143	      }} 
   144	    />
   145	  );
   146	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba supabase/migrations/20260414001_ingest_zl_intraday.sql | sed -n '1,320p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	-- =============================================================================
     2	-- ingest_zl_intraday() + rollup_zl_daily()
     3	--
     4	-- Pulls 1h ZL bars from Databento REST API into mkt.price_1h.
     5	-- Rolls up 1h bars into mkt.price_1d daily bars.
     6	-- Updates mkt.latest_price with the most recent close.
     7	-- Logs all runs to ops.ingest_run.
     8	--
     9	-- pg_cron: runs hourly. 1h bars form the daily bar progressively.
    10	-- =============================================================================
    11	
    12	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13	-- Function A: ingest_zl_intraday()
    14	--
    15	-- Fetches 1h OHLCV bars from Databento for ZL continuous front-month.
    16	-- Uses the newest bar in mkt.price_1h to determine the start date.
    17	-- Databento returns NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON lines).
    18	-- Prices are fixed-point integers (divide by 1e9).
    19	-- ts_event is nanosecond epoch.
    20	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION ingest_zl_intraday()
    22	RETURNS jsonb
    23	SECURITY DEFINER
    24	SET search_path = public, extensions
    25	LANGUAGE plpgsql
    26	AS $$
    27	DECLARE
    28	  api_key     TEXT;
    29	  start_date  TEXT;
    30	  end_date    TEXT;
    31	  url         TEXT;
    32	  response    http_response;
    33	  line        TEXT;
    34	  rec         JSONB;
    35	  bar_ts      TIMESTAMPTZ;
    36	  bar_open    NUMERIC;
    37	  bar_high    NUMERIC;
    38	  bar_low     NUMERIC;
    39	  bar_close   NUMERIC;
    40	  bar_volume  BIGINT;
    41	  records_in  INT := 0;
    42	  started     TIMESTAMPTZ := now();
    43	BEGIN
    44	  -- 1. Get API key from Vault
    45	  SELECT decrypted_secret INTO api_key
    46	  FROM vault.decrypted_secrets
    47	  WHERE name = 'databento_api_key_v2';
    48	
    49	  IF api_key IS NULL THEN
    50	    INSERT INTO ops.ingest_run (job_name, source, started_at, finished_at, status, records_upserted, error_message)
    51	    VALUES ('ingest_zl_intraday', 'databento', started, now(), 'failed', 0, 'Databento API key not found in Vault');
    52	    RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'error', 'message', 'API key not in Vault');
    53	  END IF;
    54	
    55	  -- 2. Determine date range: start from max existing 1h bar or 30 days back
    56	  SELECT COALESCE(
    57	    to_char((max(bucket_ts) AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date, 'YYYY-MM-DD'),
    58	    to_char((now() - interval '30 days')::date, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    59	  ) INTO start_date
    60	  FROM mkt.price_1h
    61	  WHERE symbol = 'ZL';
    62	
    63	  -- End = today (Databento will clamp to available data)
    64	  end_date := to_char((now() AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date, 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    65	
    66	  -- If start = end, go back 1 day to ensure we get today's bars
    67	  IF start_date = end_date THEN
    68	    start_date := to_char(((now() AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') - interval '1 day')::date, 'YYYY-MM-DD');
    69	  END IF;
    70	
    71	  -- 3. Call Databento REST API
    72	  url := 'https://hist.databento.com/v0/timeseries.get_range'
    73	    || '?dataset=GLBX.MDP3'
    74	    || '&symbols=ZL.c.0'
    75	    || '&schema=ohlcv-1h'
    76	    || '&stype_in=continuous'
    77	    || '&start=' || start_date
    78	    || '&end=' || end_date
    79	    || '&encoding=json';
    80	
    81	  SELECT * INTO response FROM http((
    82	    'GET',
    83	    url,
    84	    ARRAY[http_header('Authorization', 'Basic ' || encode(convert_to(api_key || ':', 'UTF8'), 'base64'))],
    85	    NULL,
    86	    NULL
    87	  )::http_request);
    88	
    89	  IF response.status != 200 THEN
    90	    INSERT INTO ops.ingest_run (job_name, source, started_at, finished_at, status, records_upserted, error_message)
    91	    VALUES ('ingest_zl_intraday', 'databento', started, now(), 'failed', 0,
    92	            'Databento HTTP ' || response.status || ': ' || left(response.content, 200));
    93	    RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'error', 'http_status', response.status);
    94	  END IF;
    95	
    96	  -- 4. Parse NDJSON response (one JSON object per line)
    97	  FOR line IN SELECT unnest(string_to_array(response.content, E'\n'))
    98	  LOOP
    99	    -- Skip empty lines
   100	    CONTINUE WHEN trim(line) = '';
   101	
   102	    BEGIN
   103	      rec := line::jsonb;
   104	
   105	      -- ts_event is nanosecond epoch -> convert to timestamptz
   106	      bar_ts := to_timestamp(((rec->'hd'->>'ts_event')::bigint) / 1000000000.0);
   107	
   108	      -- Prices are fixed-point (divide by 1e9)
   109	      bar_open   := (rec->>'open')::bigint   / 1000000000.0;
   110	      bar_high   := (rec->>'high')::bigint   / 1000000000.0;
   111	      bar_low    := (rec->>'low')::bigint    / 1000000000.0;
   112	      bar_close  := (rec->>'close')::bigint  / 1000000000.0;
   113	      bar_volume := (rec->>'volume')::bigint;
   114	
   115	      -- Skip bars with zero or null close
   116	      CONTINUE WHEN bar_close IS NULL OR bar_close = 0;
   117	
   118	      -- UPSERT into mkt.price_1h
   119	      INSERT INTO mkt.price_1h (symbol, bucket_ts, open, high, low, close, volume)
   120	      VALUES ('ZL', bar_ts, bar_open, bar_high, bar_low, bar_close, bar_volume)
   121	      ON CONFLICT (symbol, bucket_ts) DO UPDATE SET
   122	        open       = EXCLUDED.open,
   123	        high       = EXCLUDED.high,
   124	        low        = EXCLUDED.low,
   125	        close      = EXCLUDED.close,
   126	        volume     = EXCLUDED.volume,
   127	        ingested_at = now();
   128	
   129	      records_in := records_in + 1;
   130	
   131	    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
   132	      -- Skip malformed lines, continue processing
   133	      CONTINUE;
   134	    END;
   135	  END LOOP;
   136	
   137	  -- 5. Log success
   138	  INSERT INTO ops.ingest_run (job_name, source, started_at, finished_at, status, records_upserted, error_message)
   139	  VALUES ('ingest_zl_intraday', 'databento', started, now(), 'ok', records_in, NULL);
   140	
   141	  RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'ok', 'records', records_in, 'start', start_date, 'end', end_date);
   142	END;
   143	$$;
   144	
   145	
   146	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   147	-- Function B: rollup_zl_daily()
   148	--
   149	-- Aggregates 1h bars from mkt.price_1h into mkt.price_1d.
   150	-- For each calendar day with 1h bars:
   151	--   open  = open of first 1h bar (by bucket_ts)
   152	--   high  = max high across all 1h bars
   153	--   low   = min low across all 1h bars
   154	--   close = close of last 1h bar (by bucket_ts)
   155	--   volume = sum of all 1h bar volumes
   156	--
   157	-- Also updates mkt.latest_price with the most recent close.
   158	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION rollup_zl_daily()
   160	RETURNS jsonb
   161	SECURITY DEFINER
   162	SET search_path = public
   163	LANGUAGE plpgsql
   164	AS $$
   165	DECLARE
   166	  records_out INT := 0;
   167	  started     TIMESTAMPTZ := now();
   168	  latest_close NUMERIC;
   169	  latest_ts    TIMESTAMPTZ;
   170	BEGIN
   171	  -- 1. Aggregate 1h bars into daily bars
   172	  --    Only process days that have 1h bars newer than the latest ingested_at in price_1d,
   173	  --    OR days that don't exist in price_1d yet.
   174	  INSERT INTO mkt.price_1d (symbol, bucket_ts, open, high, low, close, volume)
   175	  SELECT
   176	    'ZL' AS symbol,
   177	    date_trunc('day', bucket_ts AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') AS day_ts,
   178	    (ARRAY_AGG(open ORDER BY bucket_ts ASC))[1]  AS open,
   179	    MAX(high)                                      AS high,
   180	    MIN(low)                                       AS low,
   181	    (ARRAY_AGG(close ORDER BY bucket_ts DESC))[1] AS close,
   182	    SUM(volume)                                    AS volume
   183	  FROM mkt.price_1h
   184	  WHERE symbol = 'ZL'
   185	    AND bucket_ts >= COALESCE(
   186	      (SELECT max(bucket_ts) - interval '2 days' FROM mkt.price_1d WHERE symbol = 'ZL'),
   187	      '2020-01-01'::timestamptz
   188	    )
   189	  GROUP BY date_trunc('day', bucket_ts AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')
   190	  HAVING SUM(volume) >= 100  -- skip roll artifact days (< 100 total contracts)
   191	  ON CONFLICT (symbol, bucket_ts) DO UPDATE SET
   192	    open       = EXCLUDED.open,
   193	    high       = EXCLUDED.high,
   194	    low        = EXCLUDED.low,
   195	    close      = EXCLUDED.close,
   196	    volume     = EXCLUDED.volume,
   197	    ingested_at = now();
   198	
   199	  GET DIAGNOSTICS records_out = ROW_COUNT;
   200	
   201	  -- 2. Update latest_price with most recent close
   202	  SELECT close, bucket_ts INTO latest_close, latest_ts
   203	  FROM mkt.price_1d
   204	  WHERE symbol = 'ZL'
   205	  ORDER BY bucket_ts DESC
   206	  LIMIT 1;
   207	
   208	  IF latest_close IS NOT NULL THEN
   209	    INSERT INTO mkt.latest_price (symbol, price, observed_at, ingested_at)
   210	    VALUES ('ZL', latest_close, latest_ts, now())
   211	    ON CONFLICT (symbol) DO UPDATE SET
   212	      price       = EXCLUDED.price,
   213	      observed_at = EXCLUDED.observed_at,
   214	      ingested_at = now();
   215	  END IF;
   216	
   217	  -- 3. Log success
   218	  INSERT INTO ops.ingest_run (job_name, source, started_at, finished_at, status, records_upserted, error_message)
   219	  VALUES ('rollup_zl_daily', 'rollup', started, now(), 'ok', records_out, NULL);
   220	
   221	  RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'ok', 'daily_bars_upserted', records_out, 'latest_price', latest_close);
   222	END;
   223	$$;
   224	
   225	
   226	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   227	-- pg_cron schedules: hourly, staggered off :00
   228	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   229	SELECT cron.schedule('ingest_zl_intraday', '7 * * * *', $$SELECT ingest_zl_intraday()$$);
   230	SELECT cron.schedule('rollup_zl_daily',    '9 * * * *', $$SELECT rollup_zl_daily()$$);

     1	import type { SupabaseClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";
     2	
     3	export type IngestRunStatus = "RUNNING" | "SUCCESS" | "FAILED";
     4	
     5	export async function logIngestRunStart(
     6	  client: SupabaseClient,
     7	  jobName: string,
     8	  source: string,
     9	): Promise<string> {
    10	  const runId = crypto.randomUUID();
    11	
    12	  try {
    13	    await client.schema("ops").from("ingest_run").insert({
    14	      run_id: runId,
    15	      job_name: jobName,
    16	      source,
    17	      status: "RUNNING",
    18	      started_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    19	      records_upserted: 0,
    20	    });
    21	  } catch (error) {
    22	    console.error("Failed to write ops.ingest_run start record", error);
    23	  }
    24	
    25	  return runId;
    26	}
    27	
    28	export async function logIngestRunFinish(
    29	  client: SupabaseClient,
    30	  runId: string,
    31	  recordsUpserted: number,
    32	): Promise<void> {
    33	  try {
    34	    await client
    35	      .schema("ops")
    36	      .from("ingest_run")
    37	      .update({
    38	        status: "SUCCESS",
    39	        finished_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    40	        records_upserted: recordsUpserted,
    41	      })
    42	      .eq("run_id", runId);
    43	  } catch (error) {
    44	    console.error("Failed to write ops.ingest_run success record", error);
    45	  }
    46	}
    47	
    48	export async function logIngestRunFailure(
    49	  client: SupabaseClient,
    50	  runId: string,
    51	  errorMessage: string,
    52	): Promise<void> {
    53	  try {
    54	    await client
    55	      .schema("ops")
    56	      .from("ingest_run")
    57	      .update({
    58	        status: "FAILED",
    59	        finished_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    60	        error_message: errorMessage.slice(0, 2000),
    61	      })
    62	      .eq("run_id", runId);
    63	  } catch (error) {
    64	    console.error("Failed to write ops.ingest_run failure record", error);
    65	  }
    66	}

     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
     4	import { hasEnvVars } from "@/lib/utils";
     5	
     6	export async function GET() {
     7	  if (!hasEnvVars) {
     8	    return NextResponse.json(
     9	      {
    10	        ok: false,
    11	        authenticated: false,
    12	        error: "Supabase auth env vars are not configured",
    13	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    14	      },
    15	      { status: 503 },
    16	    );
    17	  }
    18	
    19	  const supabase = await createClient();
    20	  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.getClaims();
    21	
    22	  if (error) {
    23	    return NextResponse.json(
    24	      {
    25	        ok: false,
    26	        authenticated: false,
    27	        error: error.message,
    28	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    29	      },
    30	      { status: 401 },
    31	    );
    32	  }
    33	
    34	  if (!data?.claims) {
    35	    return NextResponse.json(
    36	      {
    37	        ok: false,
    38	        authenticated: false,
    39	        error: "No authenticated user claims",
    40	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    41	      },
    42	      { status: 401 },
    43	    );
    44	  }
    45	
    46	  return NextResponse.json({
    47	    ok: true,
    48	    authenticated: true,
    49	    checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    50	  });
    51	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ZINC Fusion V16 Quality Playbook Runbook
     2	
     3	This repo installs Andrew Stellman's upstream Quality Playbook from:
     4	
     5	`https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook`
     6	
     7	Pinned upstream commit:
     8	
     9	`ce3a90b14631ccea2c790945e4f6dc9717f6a444` (`v1.5.7`)
    10	
    11	Local upstream checkout:
    12	
    13	`/Users/zincdigital/.codex/tool-sources/quality-playbook`
    14	
    15	Repo-local installed bundles:
    16	
    17	- `.claude/skills/quality-playbook`
    18	- `.github/skills/quality-playbook`
    19	
    20	Do not copy Quality Playbook files or generated workbook artifacts from Warbird,
    21	ZINC Digital Agency, or any other project. Refresh from the upstream checkout.
    22	
    23	## Wrapper
    24	
    25	Use the V16 wrapper:
    26	
    27	```bash
    28	python3 scripts/qplaybook.py doctor
    29	python3 scripts/qplaybook.py smoke --profile code --no-llm
    30	```
    31	
    32	The wrapper resolves `codex` from PATH and rejects:
    33	
    34	`/Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/codex`
    35	
    36	This avoids the macOS-quarantined Codex.app binary path.
    37	
    38	The stable wrapper config is `docs/runbooks/qplaybook_config.json`. The wrapper
    39	must not depend on a generated `quality/qplaybook_config.json` copy because
    40	Quality Playbook phase runs may archive `quality/` artifacts before writing a
    41	fresh run.
    42	
    43	The installed bundle must include the v1.5.7 Phase 1 support modules:
    44	`bin/reference_docs_ingest.py` and `bin/benchmark_lib.py`. It must also include
    45	the externalized phase guide references added in v1.5.7. Missing bundle members
    46	turn the doctor/smoke checks into failures.
    47	
    48	The installed quality gate is also run through the wrapper. The wrapper treats
    49	gate timeouts as failures instead of waiting indefinitely; the timeout is set by
    50	`quality_gate_timeout_seconds` in `docs/runbooks/qplaybook_config.json`.
    51	
    52	## Running Quality Playbook Phases
    53	
    54	This install is tooling-only. Do not generate V16 `quality/` workbook artifacts
    55	unless that work is explicitly requested.
    56	
    57	When a run is approved, use the wrapper with an explicit artifact-generation
    58	flag:
    59	
    60	```bash
    61	python3 scripts/qplaybook.py run --profile code --allow-quality-artifacts -- --phase 1
    62	```
    63	
    64	The wrapper validates the upstream checkout, installed bundles, and Codex CLI
    65	before handing execution to the upstream runner.
    66	
    67	## V16 Boundaries
    68	
    69	- Quality Playbook does not advance or bypass any V16 migration phase gate.
    70	- It must not run model training.
    71	- It must not run Supabase migrations, `db push`, or local Supabase.
    72	- Any future generated playbook content must be V16-specific and grounded in
    73	  the canonical migration plan plus current repo reality.
    74	
    75	## Completion Status
    76	
    77	Quality Playbook output is not sufficient by itself to claim task completion.
    78	Apply `docs/agent-safety-gates.md`:
    79	
    80	- Any `NOT RUN` TDD/test evidence means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    81	- Any `WARN` in the required quality gate means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    82	- Any aborted Quality Playbook finalizer means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    83	- Any source/config/tooling edit after artifact generation means quality
    84	  artifacts are stale until regenerated and re-verified.
    85	
    86	Run:
    87	
    88	```bash
    89	npm run guard:completion
    90	```
    91	
    92	before any completion claim.

     1	# Session Startup Runbook
     2	
     3	Run this before implementation work.
     4	
     5	## 1. Read Authority Docs
     6	
     7	Read in order:
     8	
     9	1. `AGENTS.md`
    10	2. `docs/INDEX.md`
    11	3. `docs/MASTER_PLAN.md`
    12	4. `docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`
    13	5. `docs/agent-safety-gates.md`
    14	6. Relevant runbook or contract for the task
    15	
    16	## 2. Inspect Workspace State
    17	
    18	Run:
    19	
    20	```bash
    21	git status --short
    22	git branch --show-current
    23	git log --oneline -10
    24	```
    25	
    26	Classify existing changes before editing:
    27	
    28	- user/source edits to preserve
    29	- generated `quality/` artifacts
    30	- unrelated changes outside the requested scope
    31	
    32	## 3. Identify Active Phase
    33	
    34	Use the canonical migration plan phase table. Do not advance a phase unless its
    35	documented gate evidence exists and is current.
    36	
    37	## 4. Declare Write Scope
    38	
    39	Before editing, state which files or directories will be touched. Do not add
    40	unrelated refactors.
    41	
    42	## 5. Apply Fail-Closed Completion
    43	
    44	Use:
    45	
    46	```bash
    47	npm run guard:pre-commit
    48	```
    49	
    50	before preparing a commit, and:
    51	
    52	```bash
    53	npm run guard:completion
    54	```
    55	
    56	before claiming completion.
    57	
    58	If any check is `FAIL` or `NOT RUN`, report `STATUS: INCOMPLETE` with blockers.

     1	# V16 Data Contracts (Weekly Batch Pivot - 2026-05-07)
     2	
     3	## Core Market Contracts (High-Frequency Path Retained)
     4	- `mkt.price_1h`: hourly OHLCV feed for chart freshness.
     5	- `mkt.price_1d`: daily OHLCV rollup for daily-bar path.
