     8	
     9	## Weekly Batch Contracts (Reduced Source Surface)
    10	- Non-price market, macro, supply, and alternative source ingestion runs in weekly batch windows.
    11	- Active source surface is reduced to approximately 25% of prior volume.
    12	- Batch output must be traceable by run metadata and ingest timestamps.
    13	
    14	## Forecast/Training Cadence Contract
    15	- Weekly retraining is the default cadence.
    16	- Weekly forecast publication follows successful weekly retrain.
    17	- Training and publication must remain explicit and auditable in `training.*` and `forecasts.*` tables.
    18	
    19	## Sentiment and Legislation Contracts
    20	- Sentiment and Legislation page content is GPT-driven from approved news/policy sources.
    21	- Generated summaries/scores must remain attributable to source records and run timestamps.
    22	
    23	## Dashboard AI Card Instruction Contract
    24	- `/api/dashboard/risk-factors` must provide per-card `strategicSpecialInstructions` for each driver card and the AI Market Intelligence card.
    25	- Instructions must be topic-specific, quant-research oriented, and include:
    26	  - strategic objective
    27	  - neural connection thesis
    28	  - quant research protocol
    29	  - inference constraints
    30	  - output requirements
    31	- Generic instruction text is not allowed for AI card instruction payloads.
    32	- AI narrative content is sourced from `app/config/dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json` (no OpenAI API key path).
    33	- Snapshot metadata must include `model`, `reasoningEffort`, `source`, `generatedAt`, and daily `refreshScheduleEt`.
    34	- Current locked runtime target: `gpt-5.5-fast` with `high-think`, refreshed daily at `07:00 America/New_York`.
    35	
    36	## AI Daily Card Pull Contract (All Pages)
    37	- Daily AI pull is the default source for card content on all primary pages.
    38	- Snapshot files are committed in-repo under `app/config/` and must be refreshed daily:
    39	  - `dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json`
    40	  - `strategy-posture-ai.json`
    41	  - `sentiment-overview-ai.json`
    42	  - `legislation-feed-ai.json`
    43	  - `vegas-intel-ai.json`
    44	- API routes must read their page snapshot and expose AI-derived card payloads, while preserving controlled DB fallback behavior.
    45	- AI card payloads should include market prices, reasoning, risk framing, and math-oriented interpretation wherever applicable.
    46	- Every AI-powered card on every page must include:
    47	  - `strategicSpecialInstructions` (topic-specific, quant-research, non-generic)
    48	  - `provenance` with table/source feeds and record hints
    49	- Card-level provenance must identify the source tables/feeds used for the AI narrative and include `asOf` + `generatedAt`.
    50	
    51	## Operational Contracts
    52	- `ops.ingest_run`: mandatory ingest lifecycle logging for batch and price paths.
    53	- `ops.pipeline_alerts`: freshness and pipeline alerting.
    54	- `ops.source_registry`: source ownership, cadence, and enablement state.
    55	
    56	## Freshness Expectations
    57	- Hourly contracts: update hourly for active market sessions.
    58	- Daily rollup contract: refresh from hourly path on daily schedule.
    59	- Weekly batch contracts: update on weekly schedule.
    60	- Forecast contracts: refresh after successful weekly retrain.

     2	
     3	## Access Tiers

     6

    10	- Protected API routes enforce explicit server auth guards (`requireAuthenticatedApiRequest`).
    11	- Protected pages enforce explicit server auth guards via route-group layout (`requireAuthenticatedPageSession`).

     1	BEGIN;
     2	
     3	DO $$
     4	DECLARE
     5	  rec RECORD;
     6	  policy_name TEXT;
     7	BEGIN
     8	  FOR rec IN
     9	    SELECT schemaname, tablename
    10	    FROM pg_tables
    11	    WHERE schemaname IN ('mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'vegas')
    12	  LOOP
    13	    EXECUTE format('ALTER TABLE %I.%I ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    14	
    15	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS authenticated_read ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    16	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY authenticated_read ON %I.%I FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    17	
    18	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_insert ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    19	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_insert ON %I.%I FOR INSERT TO service_role WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    20	
    21	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_update ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    22	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_update ON %I.%I FOR UPDATE TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    23	
    24	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_delete ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    25	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_delete ON %I.%I FOR DELETE TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    26	  END LOOP;
    27	
    28	  FOR rec IN
    29	    SELECT schemaname, tablename
    30	    FROM pg_tables
    31	    WHERE schemaname = 'ops'
    32	  LOOP
    33	    EXECUTE format('ALTER TABLE %I.%I ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    34	
    35	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_select ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    36	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_select ON %I.%I FOR SELECT TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    37	
    38	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_insert ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    39	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_insert ON %I.%I FOR INSERT TO service_role WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    40	
    41	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_update ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    42	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_update ON %I.%I FOR UPDATE TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    43	
    44	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_delete ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    45	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_delete ON %I.%I FOR DELETE TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    46	  END LOOP;
    47	END $$;
    48	
    49	DO $$
    50	DECLARE
    51	  schema_name TEXT;
    52	BEGIN
    53	  FOR schema_name IN
    54	    SELECT unnest(ARRAY['mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'vegas'])
    55	  LOOP
    56	    EXECUTE format('GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA %I TO authenticated, service_role', schema_name);
    57	    EXECUTE format('GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA %I TO authenticated', schema_name);
    58	    EXECUTE format('GRANT INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA %I TO service_role', schema_name);
    59	    EXECUTE format('GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA %I TO service_role', schema_name);
    60	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT SELECT ON TABLES TO authenticated', schema_name);
    61	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO service_role', schema_name);
    62	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON SEQUENCES TO service_role', schema_name);
    63	  END LOOP;
    64	
    65	  EXECUTE 'GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    66	  EXECUTE 'GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    67	  EXECUTE 'GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    68	  EXECUTE 'ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA ops GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO service_role';
    69	  EXECUTE 'ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA ops GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON SEQUENCES TO service_role';
    70	END $$;
    71	
    72	DO $$
    73	DECLARE
    74	  rec RECORD;
    75	  idx_name TEXT;
    76	BEGIN
    77	  FOR rec IN
    78	    SELECT table_schema, table_name, column_name
    79	    FROM information_schema.columns
    80	    WHERE table_schema IN ('mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'ops', 'vegas')
    81	      AND column_name IN ('bucket_ts', 'observation_date', 'trade_date', 'event_date', 'forecast_date', 'ingested_at', 'created_at')
    82	  LOOP
    83	    idx_name := format('idx_%s_%s_%s', rec.table_schema, rec.table_name, rec.column_name);
    84	    IF length(idx_name) > 60 THEN
    85	      idx_name := 'idx_' || substr(md5(idx_name), 1, 24);
    86	    END IF;
    87	
    88	    EXECUTE format(
    89	      'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS %I ON %I.%I (%I)',
    90	      idx_name,
    91	      rec.table_schema,
    92	      rec.table_name,
    93	      rec.column_name
    94	    );
    95	  END LOOP;
    96	END $$;
    97	
    98	COMMIT;

   131	@dataclass
   132	class YahooSeries:
   133	    value: float | None

   135	    change_5d: float | None

   138	
   139	def load_local_env(path: Path) -> None:
   140	    if not path.exists():
   141	        return
   142	    for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
   143	        line = line.strip()
   144	        if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
   145	            continue
   146	        key, value = line.split("=", 1)
   147	        key = key.strip()
   148	        value = value.strip().strip("'").strip('"')
   149	        os.environ.setdefault(key, value)
   150	
   151	
   152	def ensure_db_url() -> str:
   153	    env_path = ROOT / ".env.local"
   154	    load_local_env(env_path)
   155	    for key in ("POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING", "DATABASE_URL", "SUPABASE_DB_URL"):
   156	        value = os.getenv(key)
   157	        if value:
   158	            return value
   159	    raise RuntimeError("Missing database URL in POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING/DATABASE_URL/SUPABASE_DB_URL")
   160	
   161	
   162	def request_text(url: str, *, timeout: int = 30) -> str:
   163	    last_error: Exception | None = None
   164	    for attempt in range(3):
   165	        try:
   166	            res = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=timeout)
   167	            res.raise_for_status()
   168	            return res.text
   169	        except Exception as err:  # noqa: BLE001
   170	            last_error = err
   171	            time.sleep(0.75 * (attempt + 1))
   172	    try:
   173	        # Some providers intermittently reset TLS streams for requests.
   174	        # Fallback to curl transport before failing the entire run.
   175	        proc = subprocess.run(
   176	            ["curl", "-fsSL", "--max-time", str(timeout), url],
   177	            check=True,
   178	            capture_output=True,
   179	            text=True,
   180	        )
   181	        if proc.stdout:
   182	            return proc.stdout
   183	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   184	        pass
   185	    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed request: {url} :: {last_error}")
   186	
   187	
   188	def request_json(url: str, *, timeout: int = 30) -> dict[str, Any] | list[Any]:
   189	    text = request_text(url, timeout=timeout)
   190	    return json.loads(text)
   191	
   192	
   193	def safe_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
   194	    try:
   195	        if value is None:
   196	            return None
   197	        n = float(value)
   198	        if math.isfinite(n):
   199	            return n
   200	        return None
   201	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   202	        return None
   203	
   204	
   205	def to_iso_z(dt: datetime | None) -> str | None:
   206	    if dt is None:
   207	        return None
   208	    return dt.astimezone(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   209	
   210	
   211	def dt_from_epoch(epoch_value: int | float | None) -> datetime | None:
   212	    if epoch_value is None:
   213	        return None
   214	    try:
   215	        return datetime.fromtimestamp(float(epoch_value), tz=timezone.utc)
   216	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   217	        return None
   218	
   219	
   220	def fetch_yahoo_series(symbol: str) -> YahooSeries:
   221	    query = urlencode({"interval": "1d", "range": "3mo"})
   222	    url = f"https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/{symbol}?{query}"
   223	    payload = request_json(url)
   224	    result = (
   225	        payload.get("chart", {})
   226	        .get("result", [{}])[0]
   227	    )
   228	    timestamps = result.get("timestamp", []) or []
   229	    close_values_raw = (
   230	        result.get("indicators", {})
   231	        .get("quote", [{}])[0]
   232	        .get("close", [])
   233	        or []
   234	    )
   235	    points: list[tuple[datetime, float]] = []
   236	    for ts, close in zip(timestamps, close_values_raw, strict=False):
   237	        close_val = safe_float(close)
   238	        dt = dt_from_epoch(ts)
   239	        if dt is None or close_val is None:
   240	            continue
   241	        points.append((dt, close_val))
   242	
   243	    if not points:
   244	        return YahooSeries(value=None, as_of=None, change_5d=None, close_values=[])
   245	
   246	    latest_dt, latest_close = points[-1]
   247	    close_values = [p[1] for p in points]
   248	    anchor_close: float | None = None
   249	    if len(close_values) >= 6:
   250	        anchor_close = close_values[-6]
   251	    change_5d = None
   252	    if anchor_close and anchor_close != 0:
   253	        change_5d = (latest_close - anchor_close) / anchor_close
   254	
   255	    return YahooSeries(
   256	        value=latest_close,
   257	        as_of=latest_dt,
   258	        change_5d=change_5d,
   259	        close_values=close_values,
   260	    )
   261	
   262	
   263	def fetch_fred_latest(series_id: str) -> tuple[float | None, datetime | None, list[tuple[date, float]]]:
   264	    url = f"https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id={series_id}"
   265	    text = request_text(url)
   266	    rows: list[tuple[date, float]] = []
   267	    for line in text.strip().splitlines()[1:]:
   268	        parts = line.split(",", 1)
   269	        if len(parts) != 2:
   270	            continue
   271	        d, v = parts[0].strip(), parts[1].strip()
   272	        if not d or not v or v == ".":
   273	            continue
   274	        try:
   275	            parsed_date = datetime.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
   276	            parsed_value = float(v)
   277	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
   278	            continue
   279	        rows.append((parsed_date, parsed_value))
   280	    if not rows:
   281	        return None, None, []
   282	    latest_date, latest_value = rows[-1]
   283	    latest_dt = datetime.combine(latest_date, datetime.min.time(), tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   284	    return latest_value, latest_dt, rows
   285	
   286	
   287	def fetch_cftc_soybean_oil() -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   288	    params = {
   289	        "$where": "commodity_name='SOYBEAN OIL'",
   290	        "$order": "report_date_as_yyyy_mm_dd DESC",
   291	        "$limit": "6",
   292	    }
   293	    url = f"https://publicreporting.cftc.gov/resource/6dca-aqww.json?{urlencode(params)}"
   294	    payload = request_json(url)
   295	    if not isinstance(payload, list) or not payload:
   296	        return None
   297	    latest = payload[0]
   298	    obs_raw = latest.get("report_date_as_yyyy_mm_dd")
   299	    observation_date = None
   300	    if isinstance(obs_raw, str):
   301	        try:
   302	            observation_date = datetime.fromisoformat(obs_raw.replace("Z", "+00:00")).date()
   303	        except ValueError:
   304	            pass
   305	
   306	    noncomm_long = safe_float(latest.get("noncomm_positions_long_all")) or 0.0
   307	    noncomm_short = safe_float(latest.get("noncomm_positions_short_all")) or 0.0
   308	    open_interest = safe_float(latest.get("open_interest_all")) or 0.0
   309	    net = noncomm_long - noncomm_short
   310	    ratio = (net / open_interest) if open_interest > 0 else 0.0
   311	    if ratio >= 0.08:
   312	        bias = "bullish"
   313	    elif ratio <= -0.08:
   314	        bias = "bearish"
   315	    else:
   316	        bias = "neutral"
   317	
   318	    return {
   319	        "symbol": "ZL",
   320	        "observation_date": observation_date,
   321	        "bias": bias,
   322	        "managed_money_net": net,
   323	        "managed_money_ratio": ratio,
   324	        "open_interest": open_interest,
   325	        "payload": latest,
   326	    }
   327	
   328	
   329	def classify_tags(text: str) -> list[str]:
   330	    t = text.lower()
   331	    tags: set[str] = set()
   332	    if any(k in t for k in ("vix", "ovx", "volatility")):
   333	        tags.add("volatility")
   334	    if any(k in t for k in ("crush", "meal", "soybean", "soy oil", "soybean oil")):
   335	        tags.add("crush")
   336	    if "china" in t or "cny" in t:
   337	        tags.add("china")
   338	    if any(k in t for k in ("tariff", "trade policy", "trade", "duties")):
   339	        tags.add("tariff")
   340	    if any(k in t for k in ("crude", "oil", "energy", "diesel", "petroleum", "lng")):
   341	        tags.add("energy")
   342	    if any(k in t for k in ("biofuel", "renewable fuel", "rfs", "biodiesel", "saf")):
   343	        tags.add("biofuel")
   344	    if any(k in t for k in ("palm", "cpo")):
   345	        tags.add("palm")
   346	    if any(k in t for k in ("federal reserve", "fomc", "rates", "monetary")):
   347	        tags.add("fed")
   348	    if any(k in t for k in ("executive", "congress", "bill", "regulation", "epa", "usda")):
   349	        tags.add("policy")
   350	    if not tags:
   351	        tags.add("macro")
   352	    return sorted(tags)
   353

from pathlib import Path
import re,subprocess
root=Path('.')
files=subprocess.check_output(['git','ls-files'],text=True).splitlines()
tracked=set(files)
missing=[]
for f in files:
    if f.endswith('SKILL.md'):
        text=(root/f).read_text(encoding='utf-8',errors='ignore')
        for i,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(),start=1):
            for m in re.finditer(r'\\((["'^)]+scripts/[''^)]+)'"\\)',line):
                rel=m.group(1)
                rel=rel.split('#',1)[0]
                p=(root/f).parent/rel
                if not p.exists():
                    missing.append((f,i,rel))

print('missing',len(missing))
for f,i,rel in missing[:50]:
    print(f'{f}:{i} -> {rel}')

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from __future__ import annotations
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
import os

root = Path('.')
files = subprocess.check_output(['git','ls-files'], text=True).splitlines()

skill_tool_refs = {
    '.kilo/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.kilo/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
    '.github/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.github/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
}

config_exact = {
    '.env.example',
    '.gitignore',
    '.vercelignore',
    '.kilo/kilo.json',
    'components.json',
    'eslint.config.mjs',
    'next.config.ts',
    'package-lock.json',
    'package.json',
    'postcss.config.mjs',
    'supabase/config.toml',
    'tailwind.config.ts',
    'tsconfig.json',
    'vercel.json',
}

# Files that are runtime snapshots/config payloads consumed by app code.
runtime_config_prefixes = (
    'app/config/',
)

# Statically served assets are classified as code because they are part of runtime behavior/UI contract.
asset_exts = {'.svg', '.png', '.ico'}


def classify(path: str) -> tuple[str, str, str | None]:
    p = Path(path)
    suffix = p.suffix.lower()

    # Skill-tool first (strict explicit reference)
    if path in skill_tool_refs:
        rationale = (
            'This script is explicitly named in the associated SKILL prose as an execution step for the skill workflow.'
        )
        return 'skill-tool', rationale, skill_tool_refs[path]

    # Skill prose / declarative agent instructions
    if path == 'AGENTS.md' or path == 'CLAUDE.md':
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'Repository-level agent instructions define mandatory behavior, constraints, and execution protocol for automated work.',
            None,
        )

    if path == '.github/copilot-instructions.md':
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'This is declarative agent instruction prose that configures assistant behavior rather than product runtime logic.',
            None,
        )

    if path.startswith('.github/agents/'):
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'Agent prompt content defines procedural behavior and guardrails for task execution sessions.',
            None,
        )

    if path.startswith('.claude/commands/') or path.startswith('.kilo/commands/'):
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'Command markdown files encode reusable operator/agent procedures and invocation semantics.',
            None,
        )

    if path.startswith('.kilo/rules/') or path.startswith('.kilo/workflows/'):
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'Rules/workflow documents are declarative guidance artifacts that direct agent decisions and sequencing.',
            None,
        )

    if path.endswith('/SKILL.md'):
        return (
            'skill-prose',
            'SKILL.md defines the workflow contract, checkpoints, and hard rules for a named skill.',
            None,
        )

    # Skill references
    if '/skills/' in path and '/references/' in path:
        return (
            'skill-reference',
            'This reference document is consumed by skill prose as supporting context and detailed procedure material.',
            None,
        )

    # Tests
    if (
        path.startswith('python/tests/')
        or path.startswith('scripts/tests/')
        or path.endswith('_test.py')
        or path.startswith('test/')
        or '/test_' in path
    ):
        return (
            'test',
            'The file is a test module/harness that asserts behavior contracts and guards against regressions.',
            None,
        )

    # Docs
    if path == 'README.md' or path.startswith('docs/'):
        return (
            'docs',
            'Documentation artifact describing architecture, plans, contracts, or operational procedures for the project.',
            None,
        )

    # Config
    if path in config_exact:
        return (
            'config',
            'Configuration file controlling tooling, build/runtime settings, dependency metadata, or environment contracts.',
            None,
        )

    if path.startswith(runtime_config_prefixes):
        return (
            'config',
            'Runtime configuration/snapshot payload consumed by API handlers to provide AI-first card content and metadata.',
            None,
        )

    if suffix in {'.toml', '.yaml', '.yml'}:
        return (
            'config',
            'Configuration-format file used to define environment, tooling, or deployment behavior.',
            None,
        )

    if suffix in asset_exts:
        return (
            'code',
            'Static asset participates in the shipped application surface and is loaded directly by runtime UI routes/components.',
            None,
        )

    # Fixtures (test data/examples)
    if 'fixture' in path.lower() or 'fixtures/' in path:
        return (
            'fixture',
            'Fixture/example data file used to support repeatable tests or deterministic validation scenarios.',
            None,
        )

    # Default to code (independent executable behavior)
    return (
        'code',
        'Independent implementation artifact (application, library, migration, or script) that carries runtime behavior contracts.',
        None,
    )

entries = []
for path in files:
    role, rationale, prose_ref = classify(path)
    size = (root / path).stat().st_size
    item = {
        'path': path,
        'role': role,
        'size_bytes': int(size),
        'rationale': rationale,
    }
    if role == 'skill-tool' and prose_ref:
        item['skill_prose_reference'] = prose_ref
    entries.append(item)

# Deterministic ordering by path
entries.sort(key=lambda x: x['path'])

payload = {
    'schema_version': '1.0',
    'timestamp_start': datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace('+00:00', 'Z'),
    'provenance': 'git-ls-files',
    'files': entries,
}

quality_dir = root / 'quality'
quality_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
out_path = quality_dir / 'exploration_role_map.json'
out_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')
print(f'wrote {out_path} with {len(entries)} files')

# quick sanity check for disallowed prefixes in map
bad_prefixes = ['.git/', '.venv/', 'venv/', 'node_modules/', '__pycache__/', '.pytest_cache/', '.mypy_cache/', '.ruff_cache/', '.tox/']
bad = [e['path'] for e in entries if any(e['path'].startswith(pref) for pref in bad_prefixes) or any(part.endswith('.egg-info') or part.endswith('.dist-info') for part in e['path'].split('/'))]
print('disallowed_paths', len(bad))

codex
I hit a tracked-path edge case while generating the role map (`app/dashboard/page.tsx` is tracked but absent in the working tree). I’m regenerating with a git-object size fallback so the map still covers the canonical `git ls-files` set without skipping entries.

from __future__ import annotations
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

root = Path('.')
files = subprocess.check_output(['git','ls-files'], text=True).splitlines()

skill_tool_refs = {
    '.kilo/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.kilo/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
    '.github/skills/data-review/scripts/freshness_report.sql': '.github/skills/data-review/SKILL.md:13',
}

config_exact = {
    '.env.example',
    '.gitignore',
    '.vercelignore',
    '.kilo/kilo.json',
    'components.json',
    'eslint.config.mjs',
    'next.config.ts',
    'package-lock.json',
    'package.json',
    'postcss.config.mjs',
    'supabase/config.toml',
    'tailwind.config.ts',
    'tsconfig.json',
    'vercel.json',
}

runtime_config_prefixes = (
    'app/config/',
)

def classify(path: str):
    p = Path(path)
    suffix = p.suffix.lower()

    if path in skill_tool_refs:
        return 'skill-tool', 'This script is explicitly named in skill prose as an execution step for the documented workflow.', skill_tool_refs[path]

    if path in {'AGENTS.md', 'CLAUDE.md'}:
        return 'skill-prose', 'Repository-level agent instruction prose defines mandatory operating rules, sequencing, and safety constraints.', None

    if path == '.github/copilot-instructions.md':
        return 'skill-prose', 'Declarative assistant instruction content that governs agent behavior rather than product runtime logic.', None

    if path.startswith('.github/agents/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Agent prompt content that prescribes task behavior and operational constraints.', None

    if path.startswith('.claude/commands/') or path.startswith('.kilo/commands/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Command prose encodes reusable agent procedures and invocation guidance.', None

    if path.startswith('.kilo/rules/') or path.startswith('.kilo/workflows/'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Rules/workflow prose defines decision policy and process invariants for guided agent work.', None

    if path.endswith('/SKILL.md'):
        return 'skill-prose', 'Skill definition prose describing loops, gates, and hard constraints for a named skill.', None

    if '/skills/' in path and '/references/' in path:
        return 'skill-reference', 'Reference material consumed by skill prose for deeper procedures, examples, or query packs.', None

    if (
        path.startswith('python/tests/')
        or path.startswith('scripts/tests/')
        or path.endswith('_test.py')
        or '/tests/' in path
        or path.endswith('test_connection.py')
    ):
        return 'test', 'Automated test/harness file used to assert behavior contracts and regression guards.', None

    if path == 'README.md' or path.startswith('docs/'):
        return 'docs', 'Documentation artifact describing project intent, plans, contracts, operations, or audits.', None

    if path in config_exact:
        return 'config', 'Configuration file controlling tooling, build/runtime settings, dependencies, or environment contracts.', None

    if path.startswith(runtime_config_prefixes):
        return 'config', 'Runtime snapshot/config payload read by API routes to serve AI-first card content and provenance metadata.', None

    if suffix in {'.toml', '.yaml', '.yml'}:
        return 'config', 'Configuration-format file defining environment, deployment, or tool behavior.', None

    if suffix in asset_exts:
        return 'code', 'Static runtime asset that is part of shipped application behavior and UI rendering.', None

    if 'fixture' in path.lower() or 'fixtures/' in path:
        return 'fixture', 'Fixture/example data artifact used for deterministic testing or validation.', None

    return 'code', 'Independent implementation artifact (application, library, migration, or script) with runtime behavior contract.', None


def file_size_bytes(path: str) -> int:
    fp = root / path
    if fp.exists():
        return fp.stat().st_size
    # fallback for tracked paths missing from working tree
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'cat-file', '-s', f'HEAD:{path}'], text=True).strip()
        return int(out)
    except Exception:
        return 0

entries = []
missing_worktree = []
for path in files:
    role, rationale, ref = classify(path)
    size = file_size_bytes(path)
    if not (root / path).exists():
        missing_worktree.append(path)
        rationale = rationale + ' File is tracked in git but absent from current working tree; size sourced from git object metadata.'
    item = {
        'path': path,
        'role': role,
        'size_bytes': int(size),
        'rationale': rationale,
    }
    if role == 'skill-tool' and ref:
        item['skill_prose_reference'] = ref
    entries.append(item)

entries.sort(key=lambda x: x['path'])

payload = {
    'schema_version': '1.0',
    'timestamp_start': datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace('+00:00', 'Z'),
    'provenance': 'git-ls-files',
    'files': entries,
}

quality_dir = root / 'quality'
quality_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
out_path = quality_dir / 'exploration_role_map.json'
out_path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding='utf-8')

print('disallowed_paths', len(bad))

     1	---
     2	name: quality-playbook
     3	description: "Run a complete quality engineering audit on any codebase. Derives behavioral requirements from the code, generates spec-traced functional tests, runs a three-pass code review with regression tests, executes a multi-model spec audit (Council of Three), and produces a consolidated bug report with TDD-verified patches. Finds the 35% of real defects that structural code review alone cannot catch. Works with any language. Trigger on 'quality playbook', 'spec audit', 'Council of Three', 'fitness-to-purpose', or 'coverage theater'."
     4	license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt
     5	metadata:
     6	  version: 1.5.7
     7	  # NOTE: Inline occurrences of the skill version exist throughout this file (frontmatter,
     8	  # banner, version stamp template, sidecar JSON examples, run metadata, recheck template).
     9	  # When bumping the version, update ALL occurrences — search for the old version string
    10	  # globally. One historical reference to v1.4.6 edgequake benchmarking is intentionally
    11	  # preserved in the challenge-gate section and must NOT be bumped.
    12	  author: Andrew Stellman
    13	  github: https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook
    14	---
    15	
    16	# Quality Playbook Generator
    17	
    18	## Plan Overview — read this first, then explain it to the user
    19	
    20	Before reading any other section of this skill, understand the plan and its dependencies. Each phase produces artifacts that the next phase depends on. Skipping or rushing a phase means every downstream phase works from incomplete information.
    21	
    22	**Phase 0 (Prior Run Analysis):** If previous quality runs exist, load their findings as seed data. This is automatic and only applies to re-runs.
    23	
    24	**Phase 1 (Explore):** Run the v1.5.3 documentation intake first (`python -m bin.reference_docs_ingest <target>` to walk `reference_docs/` — `cite/` files produce `quality/formal_docs_manifest.json` records; top-level files are loaded as Tier 4 context via `reference_docs_ingest.load_tier4_context(<target>)`). Then explore the codebase in three stages: open exploration driven by domain knowledge, domain-knowledge risk analysis, and selected structured exploration patterns. Write all findings to `quality/EXPLORATION.md`. This file is the foundation — Phase 2 reads it as its primary input.
    25	
    26	**Phase 2 (Generate):** Read EXPLORATION.md and produce the quality artifacts: requirements, constitution, functional tests, code review protocol, integration tests, spec audit protocol, TDD protocol. (`AGENTS.md` at the target's repo root is generated by the orchestrator AFTER Phase 6, not by you in Phase 2 — see "File 6" below for the contract.)
    27	
    28	**Phase 3 (Code Review):** Run the three-pass code review against HEAD. Write regression tests for every confirmed bug. Generate patches.
    29	
    30	**Phase 4 (Spec Audit):** Three independent AI auditors review the code against requirements. Triage with verification probes. After triage, the same Council runs the v1.5.3 Layer-2 semantic citation check — one prompt per reviewer, structured per-REQ verdicts for every Tier 1/2 citation, output to `quality/citation_semantic_check.json`. Write regression tests for net-new findings.
    31	
    32	**Phase 5 (Reconciliation):** Close the loop — every bug from code review and spec audit is tracked, regression-tested or explicitly exempted. Run TDD red-green cycle. Finalize the completeness report.
    33	
    34	**Phase 6 (Verify):** Run self-check benchmarks against all generated artifacts. Check for internal consistency, version stamp correctness, and convergence.
    35	
    36	**Phase 7 (Present, Explore, Improve):** Present results to the user with a scannable summary table, offer drill-down on any artifact, and provide a menu of improvement paths (iteration strategies, requirement refinement, integration test tuning). This is the interactive phase where the user takes ownership of the quality system.
    37	
    38	Every bug found traces back to a requirement, and every requirement traces back to an exploration finding.
    39	
    40	**The critical dependency chain:** Exploration findings → EXPLORATION.md → Requirements → Code review + Spec audit → Bug discovery. A shallow exploration produces abstract requirements. Abstract requirements miss bugs. The exploration phase is where bugs are won or lost.
    41	
    42	**MANDATORY FIRST ACTION:** After reading and understanding the plan above, print the following message to the user, then explain the plan in your own words — what you'll do, what each phase produces, and why the exploration phase matters most. Emphasize that exploration starts with open-ended domain-driven investigation, followed by domain-knowledge risk analysis that reasons about what goes wrong in systems like this, then supplemented by selected structured patterns. Do not copy the plan verbatim; paraphrase it to demonstrate understanding.
    43	
    44	> Quality Playbook v1.5.7 — by Andrew Stellman
    45	> https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook
    46	
    47	Generate a complete quality system tailored to a specific codebase. Unlike test stub generators that work mechanically from source code, this skill explores the project first — understanding its domain, architecture, specifications, and failure history — then produces a quality playbook grounded in what it finds.
    48	
    49	## How to run this — v1.5.4 self-encoded invocation contract
    50	
    51	If the operator hands you this skill (or points you at any QPB-installed target) and says **"Run the Quality Playbook"** — possibly with a hint like "this is a bootstrap run" or "run on itself" or "self-audit" — this section tells you exactly what to do. The operator should not need to provide additional instructions; the canonical invocation, the defaults, the guardrails, and the output contract all live here.
    52	
    53	### Pick your execution mode
    54	
    55	QPB ships in two execution shapes. Pick the one that matches your runtime — the wrong choice produces the codex-on-codex indirection pathology surfaced by the 2026-04-30 bootstrap test.
    56	
    57	| Mode | When this is you | What you do |
    58	|------|------------------|-------------|
    59	| **A. Skill-direct (UI-context)** | You are a coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex desktop, etc.) handed this skill in your own chat. Your runtime IS the reasoning loop — you read files, you write files, you decide. | Walk through Phase 1 → Phase 6 yourself using the externalized phase prompts in `phase_prompts/`. Write artifacts into the target's `quality/` directory directly. No subprocess, no runner. |
    60	| **B. Runner-driven (CLI-automation)** | The operator is invoking `python3 -m bin.run_playbook` deliberately — to batch across multiple targets, drive a headless CI run, or fan out per-phase work to a different model than the one reading this prose. | The orchestrator spawns a CLI agent (`claude`, `copilot`, `codex`, or `cursor`) per phase. You (or whoever is reading this) are the operator-side control loop, not the per-phase reasoner. |
    61	
    62	**Both modes use the same phase prompt content** — the `phase_prompts/*.md` files at the repo root are the single source of truth, loaded by `bin/run_playbook.py::_load_phase_prompt` and read directly by Mode A walkthroughs. The only thing the two modes differ on is WHO drives — you (Mode A) or the orchestrator subprocess-spawning a CLI agent (Mode B).
    63	
    64	**When in doubt, default to Mode A.** If the operator wanted runner-driven invocation they would have run the runner themselves; if they pasted "Run the Quality Playbook" into your chat, they want you to drive. The Mode B section below tells you what to do *if* the operator explicitly invokes the runner.
    65	
    66	### Mode A — skill-direct walkthrough (UI-context)
    67	
    68	The operator's prompt is just **"Run the Quality Playbook"** (or "run on itself", "self-audit", etc.). You drive every phase inline.
    69	
    70	For each phase 1..6, in order:
    71	
    72	1. **Load the phase prompt.** Read `phase_prompts/phaseN.md` (resolve via the same install-location fallback list documented for `references/` below). For `phase1.md`, substitute `{seed_instruction}` (the prelude that says "skip Phase 0/0b" — empty string when seeds are allowed) and `{role_taxonomy}` (the taxonomy block rendered from the role taxonomy below). For `phase2.md` through `phase6.md`, the file is pure-literal — read it verbatim.
    73	2. **Execute the phase per the prompt.** Read the inputs the prompt names, do the analysis, write outputs into the target's `quality/` directory.
    74	3. **STOP at the end-of-phase boundary.** Every phase prompt ends with an "IMPORTANT: Do NOT proceed to Phase N+1" instruction. Honor it. The operator advances to the next phase by saying so.
    75	
    76	You are responsible — without the orchestrator's structural backstop — for the same source-unchanged invariant the runner enforces: **do NOT modify any file outside the target's `quality/` directory**. In Mode B the gate would catch this; in Mode A you are the gate. The 2026-04-30 bootstrap test specifically failed on a Phase 2 LLM modifying the target's root `AGENTS.md` — the same failure mode applies in Mode A.
    77	
    78	For the bootstrap-run (self-audit) variant of Mode A, see "Bootstrap mode" below — the only delta is that the target IS the QPB repo, so cite the same `phase_prompts/` files you read from.
    79	
    80	#### Mode A scope — what's covered, what's Mode-B-only

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba components/dashboard/ProbabilitySurface.tsx | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"use client"
     2	
     3	import React, { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react"
     4	
     5	interface ForecastTarget {
     6	  id: string
     7	  horizonDays: number
     8	  horizonLabel: string
     9	  priceLow: number | null
    10	  priceHigh: number | null
    11	  oofPrice: number | null
    12	  coveragePct: number | null
    13	  isMissing?: boolean
    14	}
    15	
    16	const BIN_COUNT = 11
    17	const AG_HORIZON_DAYS = [30, 90, 180] as const
    18	
    19	function horizonLabel(days: number): string {
    20	  if (days === 30) return "1M"
    21	  if (days === 90) return "3M"
    22	  if (days === 180) return "6M"
    23	  return `${days}d`
    24	}
    25	
    26	function clamp(v: number, min: number, max: number): number {
    27	  return Math.min(max, Math.max(min, v))
    28	}
    29	
    30	function cellAlpha(price: number, t: ForecastTarget): number {
    31	  if (t.priceLow === null || t.priceHigh === null || t.oofPrice === null) {
    32	    return 0.08
    33	  }
    34	
    35	  const low = Math.min(t.priceLow, t.priceHigh)
    36	  const high = Math.max(t.priceLow, t.priceHigh)
    37	  const halfWidth = Math.max((high - low) / 2, 0.1)
    38	  const centerDist = Math.abs(price - t.oofPrice) / halfWidth
    39	  const insideZone = price >= low && price <= high
    40	
    41	  if (insideZone) {
    42	    return clamp(1 - centerDist * 0.75, 0.25, 1)
    43	  }
    44	
    45	  const outsideDist = Math.abs(price - t.oofPrice) / (halfWidth * 2.5)
    46	  return clamp(0.18 - outsideDist * 0.12, 0.04, 0.18)
    47	}
    48	
    49	export function ProbabilitySurface() {
    50	  const [targets, setTargets] = useState<ForecastTarget[]>([])
    51	  const [asOfDate, setAsOfDate] = useState<string | null>(null)
    52	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    53	  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    54	
    55	  useEffect(() => {
    56	    let cancelled = false
    57	
    58	    async function loadTargets() {
    59	      setLoading(true)
    60	      setError(null)
    61	      try {
    62	        const res = await fetch("/api/zl/forecast-targets", { cache: "no-store" })
    63	        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    64	        const json = await res.json()
    65	        if (cancelled) return
    66	        setTargets(
    67	          (json.targets ?? [])
    68	            .filter(
    69	              (t: ForecastTarget) =>
    70	                AG_HORIZON_DAYS.includes(t?.horizonDays as (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number]) &&
    71	                typeof t?.horizonDays === "number" &&
    72	                typeof t?.priceLow === "number" &&
    73	                typeof t?.priceHigh === "number" &&
    74	                typeof t?.oofPrice === "number",
    75	            )
    76	            .map((t: ForecastTarget) => ({
    77	              ...t,
    78	              horizonLabel: horizonLabel(t.horizonDays),
    79	            }))
    80	            .sort((a: ForecastTarget, b: ForecastTarget) => a.horizonDays - b.horizonDays),
    81	        )
    82	        setAsOfDate(json.asOfDate ?? null)
    83	      } catch (e) {
    84	        if (cancelled) return
    85	        setError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Failed to load target zones")
    86	      } finally {
    87	        if (!cancelled) setLoading(false)
    88	      }
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    loadTargets()
    92	    return () => { cancelled = true }
    93	  }, [])
    94	
    95	  const priceBins = useMemo(() => {
    96	    const resolvedTargets = targets.filter(
    97	      (t) => t.priceLow !== null && t.priceHigh !== null && t.oofPrice !== null,
    98	    )
    99	    if (resolvedTargets.length === 0) return [] as number[]
   100	
   101	    const low = Math.min(...resolvedTargets.map((t) => Math.min(t.priceLow!, t.priceHigh!)))
   102	    const high = Math.max(...resolvedTargets.map((t) => Math.max(t.priceLow!, t.priceHigh!)))
   103	    if (!Number.isFinite(low) || !Number.isFinite(high)) return [] as number[]
   104	
   105	    const span = Math.max(high - low, 1)
   106	    const min = low - span * 0.05
   107	    const max = high + span * 0.05
   108	    const step = (max - min) / (BIN_COUNT - 1)
   109	
   110	    return Array.from(
   111	      { length: BIN_COUNT },
   112	      (_, idx) => Number((max - idx * step).toFixed(2)),
   113	    )
   114	  }, [targets])
   115	
   116	  const displayTargets = useMemo(() => {
   117	    const byHorizon = new Map<number, ForecastTarget>()
   118	    for (const t of targets) byHorizon.set(t.horizonDays, t)
   119	
   120	    return AG_HORIZON_DAYS.map((horizonDays) => {
   121	      const resolved = byHorizon.get(horizonDays)
   122	      if (resolved) return resolved
   123	
   124	      return {
   125	        id: `missing-${horizonDays}`,
   126	        horizonDays,
   127	        horizonLabel: horizonLabel(horizonDays),
   128	        priceLow: null,
   129	        priceHigh: null,
   130	        oofPrice: null,
   131	        coveragePct: null,
   132	        isMissing: true,
   133	      } satisfies ForecastTarget
   134	    })
   135	  }, [targets])
   136	
   137	  const gridTemplateColumns = `56px repeat(${Math.max(displayTargets.length, 1)}, minmax(0,1fr))`
   138	
   139	  return (
   140	    <div className="w-full bg-[#0a0a0a] border border-white/5 rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm overflow-hidden">
   141	      <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2">
   142	        <div>
   143	          <h3 className="text-sm font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wider">
   144	            L3 Probability Surface
   145	          </h3>
   146	          <p className="text-xs text-slate-500">
   147	            Real target zone density from latest AG trading-model forecast (1M/3M/6M)
   148	            {asOfDate ? ` (as of ${asOfDate})` : ""}
   149	          </p>
   150	        </div>
   151	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 text-[10px] text-slate-500">
   152	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
   153	            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-[1px] bg-cyan-400/90" />
   154	            High
   155	          </div>
   156	          <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
   157	            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-[1px] bg-cyan-400/20" />
   158	            Low
   159	          </div>
   160	        </div>
   161	      </div>
   162	
   163	      {loading && (
   164	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-slate-400">Loading forecast density...</div>
   165	      )}
   166	
   167	      {!loading && error && (
   168	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-red-400">Failed to load probability data: {error}</div>
   169	      )}
   170	
   171	      {!loading && !error && targets.length === 0 && (
   172	        <div className="mt-6 text-sm text-slate-400">
   173	          No production target zones available yet. This view populates automatically once forecast rows are present.
   174	        </div>
   175	      )}
   176	
   177	      {!loading && !error && targets.length > 0 && (
   178	        <div className="mt-6 space-y-2">
   179	          <div className="grid gap-1" style={{ gridTemplateColumns }}>
   180	            <div />
   181	            {displayTargets.map((t) => (
   182	              <div key={`h-${t.id}`} className="text-center text-[10px] text-slate-500 font-mono">
   183	                <div>{t.horizonLabel}</div>
   184	                {t.isMissing && (
   185	                  <div className="text-[9px] text-slate-600">pending</div>
   186	                )}
   187	              </div>
   188	            ))}
   189	
   190	            {priceBins.map((price) => (
   191	              <React.Fragment key={`row-${price}`}>
   192	                <div className="text-[10px] text-slate-600 font-mono pr-2 text-right">
   193	                  ${price.toFixed(2)}
   194	                </div>
   195	                {displayTargets.map((t) => {
   196	                  const alpha = cellAlpha(price, t)
   197	                  const title = t.isMissing
   198	                    ? `${t.horizonLabel} | awaiting forecast rows`
   199	                    : `${t.horizonLabel} | zone $${t.priceLow!.toFixed(2)}-$${t.priceHigh!.toFixed(2)} | median $${t.oofPrice!.toFixed(2)}`
   200	                  return (
   201	                    <div
   202	                      key={`${t.id}-${price}`}
   203	                      className="h-5 rounded-[2px] border border-white/5"
   204	                      style={{
   205	                        backgroundColor: `rgba(34, 211, 238, ${alpha})`,
   206	                        boxShadow: alpha > 0.75 ? "0 0 10px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.35)" : "none",
   207	                      }}
   208	                      title={title}
   209	                    />
   210	                  )
   211	                })}
   212	              </React.Fragment>
   213	            ))}
   214	          </div>
   215	
   216	          <div className="grid gap-1 pt-2 border-t border-white/5" style={{ gridTemplateColumns }}>
   217	            <div className="text-[10px] text-slate-600 font-mono pr-2 text-right">Coverage</div>

   219	              <div key={`c-${t.id}`} className="text-center text-[10px] font-mono text-cyan-300/80">
   220	                {t.coveragePct !== null ? `${t.coveragePct}%` : "--"}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba app/api/zl/target-zones/route.ts | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     4	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, TargetZone } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	const AG_HORIZON_DAYS = [30, 90, 180] as const;
     8	const LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP: Record<number, (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number]> = {
     9	  7: 30,
    10	  14: 90,
    11	  30: 180,
    12	};
    13	
    14	function normalizeHorizon(row: {
    15	  horizon_days: number;
    16	  model_version: string | null;
    17	}): number | null {
    18	  if (AG_HORIZON_DAYS.includes(row.horizon_days as (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number])) {
    19	    return row.horizon_days;
    20	  }
    21	  if (row.model_version?.startsWith("trusted-fill-v1")) {
    22	    return LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP[row.horizon_days] ?? null;
    23	  }
    24	  return null;
    25	}
    26	
    27	export async function GET() {
    28	  try {
    29	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    30	    if (unauthorized) {
    31	      return unauthorized;
    32	    }
    33	
    34	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    35	
    36	    // Get the latest target zones (most recent forecast_date, all horizons)
    37	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    38	      .schema("forecasts")
    39	      .from("target_zones")
    40	      .select("horizon_days, p30, p50, p70, model_version, generated_at, forecast_date")
    41	      .order("forecast_date", { ascending: false })
    42	      .limit(20);
    43	
    44	    if (error) {
    45	      return NextResponse.json(
    46	        { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    47	        { status: 500 },
    48	      );
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
    52	      const envelope: ApiEnvelope<TargetZone[]> = {
    53	        ok: true,
    54	        data: [],
    55	        asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    56	        source: "forecasts.target_zones",
    57	      };
    58	      return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    59	    }
    60	
    61	    // Get the latest forecast_date and filter to only that date's zones
    62	    const latestDate = rows[0].forecast_date;
    63	    const latestZones = rows.filter((r) => r.forecast_date === latestDate);
    64	
    65	    const deduped = new Map<number, TargetZone>();
    66	    for (const row of latestZones) {
    67	      const horizonDays = normalizeHorizon(row);
    68	      if (!horizonDays) continue;
    69	      if (deduped.has(horizonDays)) continue;
    70	      deduped.set(horizonDays, {
    71	        horizonDays,
    72	        p30: Number(row.p30),
    73	        p50: Number(row.p50),
    74	        p70: Number(row.p70),
    75	        generatedAt: row.generated_at,
    76	      });
    77	    }
    78	    const zones = [...deduped.values()].sort((a, b) => a.horizonDays - b.horizonDays);
    79	
    80	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<TargetZone[]> = {
    81	      ok: true,
    82	      data: zones,
    83	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    84	      source: "forecasts.target_zones",
    85	    };
    86	
    87	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    88	  } catch (err) {
    89	    return NextResponse.json(
    90	      { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    91	      { status: 500 },
    92	    );
    93	  }
    94	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     4	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     5	
     6	type ForecastTarget = {
     7	  id: string;
     8	  horizonDays: number;
     9	  horizonLabel: string;
    10	  priceLow: number;
    11	  priceHigh: number;
    12	  oofPrice: number;
    13	  coveragePct: number | null;
    14	};
    15	
    16	const AG_HORIZON_DAYS = [30, 90, 180] as const;
    17	const LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP: Record<number, (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number]> = {
    18	  7: 30,
    19	  14: 90,
    20	  30: 180,
    21	};
    22	
    23	function normalizeHorizon(row: {
    24	  horizon_days: number;
    25	  model_version: string | null;
    26	}): number | null {
    27	  if (AG_HORIZON_DAYS.includes(row.horizon_days as (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number])) {
    28	    return row.horizon_days;
    29	  }
    30	  if (row.model_version?.startsWith("trusted-fill-v1")) {
    31	    return LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP[row.horizon_days] ?? null;
    32	  }
    33	  return null;
    34	}
    35	
    36	function horizonLabel(days: number): string {
    37	  if (days === 30) return "1M";
    38	  if (days === 90) return "3M";
    39	  if (days === 180) return "6M";
    40	  return `${days}d`;
    41	}
    42	
    43	export async function GET() {
    44	  try {
    45	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    46	    if (unauthorized) {
    47	      return unauthorized;
    48	    }
    49	
    50	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    51	
    52	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    53	      .schema("forecasts")
    54	      .from("target_zones")
    55	      .select("forecast_date, horizon_days, p30, p50, p70, model_version")
    56	      .order("forecast_date", { ascending: false })
    57	      .limit(60);
    58	
    59	    if (error) {
    60	      return NextResponse.json(
    61	        { error: error.message, asOfDate: null, targets: [] as ForecastTarget[] },
    62	        { status: 500 },
    63	      );
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
    67	      return NextResponse.json({
    68	        asOfDate: null,
    69	        targets: [] as ForecastTarget[],
    70	      });
    71	    }
    72	
    73	    const asOfDate = rows[0].forecast_date;
    74	    const latestRows = rows.filter((r) => r.forecast_date === asOfDate);
    75	    const deduped = new Map<number, ForecastTarget>();
    76	    for (const row of latestRows) {
    77	      const horizonDays = normalizeHorizon(row);
    78	      if (!horizonDays) continue;
    79	      if (deduped.has(horizonDays)) continue;
    80	      deduped.set(horizonDays, {
    81	        id: `${row.forecast_date}-${horizonDays}`,
    82	        horizonDays,
    83	        horizonLabel: horizonLabel(horizonDays),
    84	        priceLow: Number(row.p30),
    85	        priceHigh: Number(row.p70),
    86	        oofPrice: Number(row.p50),
    87	        coveragePct: null,
    88	      });
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    const targets: ForecastTarget[] = [...deduped.values()]
    92	      .sort((a, b) => a.horizonDays - b.horizonDays)
    93	      .map((r) => ({
    94	        id: r.id,
    95	        horizonDays: r.horizonDays,
    96	        horizonLabel: r.horizonLabel,
    97	        priceLow: r.priceLow,
    98	        priceHigh: r.priceHigh,
    99	        oofPrice: r.oofPrice,
   100	        coveragePct: r.coveragePct,
   101	      }));
   102	
   103	    return NextResponse.json({
   104	      asOfDate,
   105	      targets,
   106	    });
   107	  } catch (err) {
   108	    return NextResponse.json(
   109	      { error: String(err), asOfDate: null, targets: [] as ForecastTarget[] },
   110	      { status: 500 },
   111	    );
   112	  }
   113	}

    45	function toChartDay(dateStr: string): Time {
    46	  return dateStr.slice(0, 10) as unknown as Time;
    47	}
    48	
    49	function computeVolatility(bars: ZlPriceBar[]): string {
    50	  const recent = bars.slice(-20);
    51	  if (recent.length < 2) return "--";
    52	  const returns: number[] = [];
    53	  for (let i = 1; i < recent.length; i++) {
    54	    returns.push(Math.log(recent[i].close / recent[i - 1].close));
    55	  }
    56	  const mean = returns.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / returns.length;
    57	  const variance =
    58	    returns.reduce((a, b) => a + (b - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;
    59	  return (Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100).toFixed(1) + "%";
    60	}
    61	
    62	function computeSmaData(candles: CandlestickData<Time>[], period: number): { time: Time; value: number }[] {
    63	  if (candles.length < period) return [];
    64	
    65	  const result: { time: Time; value: number }[] = [];
    66	  let rollingSum = 0;
    67	
    68	  for (let i = 0; i < candles.length; i += 1) {
    69	    rollingSum += candles[i].close;
    70	    if (i >= period) {
    71	      rollingSum -= candles[i - period].close;
    72	    }
    73	    if (i >= period - 1) {
    74	      result.push({
    75	        time: candles[i].time,
    76	        value: rollingSum / period,
    77	      });
    78	    }
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  return result;
    82	}

    84	export function ZlCandlestickChart({
    85	  height = "70vh",
    86	  targetZones = [],
    87	}: {
    88	  height?: string | number;
    89	  targetZones?: TargetZone[];
    90	}) {
    91	  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    92	  const chartRef = useRef<IChartApi | null>(null);
    93	  const seriesRef = useRef<ISeriesApi<"Candlestick"> | null>(null);
    94	  const fitCalledRef = useRef(false);
    95	  const fibLockedRef = useRef<FibResult | null>(null);
    96	
    97	  const [bars, setBars] = useState<ZlPriceBar[]>([]);
    98	  const [lastPrice, setLastPrice] = useState<number | null>(null);
    99	  const [priceChange, setPriceChange] = useState(0);
   100	  const [highPrice, setHighPrice] = useState<number | null>(null);
   101	  const [lowPrice, setLowPrice] = useState<number | null>(null);
   102	  const [volatility, setVolatility] = useState("--");
   103	
   104	  // Fetch daily bars
   105	  useEffect(() => {
   106	    async function fetchBars() {
   107	      try {
   108	        const res = await fetch("/api/zl/price-1d", { cache: "no-store" });
   109	        if (!res.ok) return;
   110	        const json = await res.json();
   111	        if (!json.data || json.data.length === 0) return;
   112	
   113	        const parsed: ZlPriceBar[] = json.data.map(
   114	          (d: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
   115	            symbol: String(d.symbol),
   116	            tradeDate: String(d.tradeDate),
   117	            open: Number(d.open),
   118	            high: Number(d.high),
   119	            low: Number(d.low),
   120	            close: Number(d.close),
   121	            volume: Number(d.volume),
   122	          }),
   123	        );
   124	
   125	        setBars(parsed);
   126	
   127	        const latest = parsed[parsed.length - 1];
   128	        const prev = parsed[parsed.length - 2];
   129	        setLastPrice(latest.close);
   130	        setHighPrice(latest.high);
   131	        setLowPrice(latest.low);
   132	        setVolatility(computeVolatility(parsed));
   133	
   134	        if (prev) {
   135	          setPriceChange(((latest.close - prev.close) / prev.close) * 100);
   136	        }
   137	      } catch {
   138	        /* fail silently — chart stays empty */
   139	      }
   140	    }
   141	
   142	    fetchBars();
   143	    const id = setInterval(fetchBars, REFRESH_MS);
   144	    return () => clearInterval(id);
   145	  }, []);
   146	
   147	  // Create and manage chart
   148	  useEffect(() => {
   149	    if (!containerRef.current || bars.length === 0) return;
   150	
   151	    // Clean up previous
   152	    if (chartRef.current) {
   153	      try {
   154	        chartRef.current.remove();
   155	      } catch {
   156	        /* already disposed */
   157	      }
   158	      chartRef.current = null;
   159	      seriesRef.current = null;
   160	      fitCalledRef.current = false;
   161	    }
   162	
   163	    const chart = createChart(containerRef.current, {
   164	      layout: {
   165	        background: { type: ColorType.Solid, color: "transparent" },
   166	        textColor: TEXT_COLOR,
   167	        fontFamily: "Inter, sans-serif",
   168	        fontSize: 11,
   169	        attributionLogo: false,
   170	      },
   171	      grid: {
   172	        vertLines: { color: GRID_COLOR },
   173	        horzLines: { color: GRID_COLOR },
   174	      },
   175	      crosshair: {
   176	        vertLine: {
   177	          color: CROSSHAIR_COLOR,
   178	          width: 1,
   179	          style: LineStyle.Solid,
   180	          labelBackgroundColor: LABEL_BG,
   181	        },
   182	        horzLine: {
   183	          color: CROSSHAIR_COLOR,
   184	          width: 1,
   185	          style: LineStyle.Solid,
   186	          labelBackgroundColor: LABEL_BG,
   187	        },
   188	      },
   189	      rightPriceScale: {
   190	        borderColor: "transparent",
   191	        autoScale: true,
   192	        scaleMargins: { top: 0.05, bottom: 0.05 },
   193	      },
   194	      timeScale: {
   195	        borderColor: "transparent",
   196	        timeVisible: false,
   197	        fixLeftEdge: false,
   198	        fixRightEdge: false,
   199	        rightOffset: RIGHT_PADDING_BARS,
   200	        barSpacing: BAR_SPACING,
   201	        minBarSpacing: MIN_BAR_SPACING,
   202	        lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize: true,
   203	      },
   204	      handleScroll: {
   205	        mouseWheel: false,
   206	        pressedMouseMove: true,
   207	        horzTouchDrag: true,
   208	        vertTouchDrag: false,
   209	      },
   210	      handleScale: {
   211	        mouseWheel: false,
   212	        pinch: true,
   213	        axisPressedMouseMove: { time: true, price: true },
   214	        axisDoubleClickReset: { time: true, price: true },
   215	      },
   216	    });
   217	
   218	    chartRef.current = chart;
   219	
   220	    // Build candlestick data sorted chronologically
   221	    const candleData: CandlestickData<Time>[] = bars
   222	      .map((b) => ({
   223	        time: toChartDay(b.tradeDate),
   224	        open: b.open,
   225	        high: b.high,
   226	        low: b.low,
   227	        close: b.close,
   228	      }))
   229	      .sort((a, b) => String(a.time).localeCompare(String(b.time)));
   230	
   231	    const series = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries, {
   232	      upColor: CANDLE_THEME.upColor,
   233	      downColor: CANDLE_THEME.downColor,
   234	      borderUpColor: CANDLE_THEME.borderUpColor,
   235	      borderDownColor: CANDLE_THEME.borderDownColor,
   236	      wickUpColor: CANDLE_THEME.wickUpColor,
   237	      wickDownColor: CANDLE_THEME.wickDownColor,
   238	      priceLineVisible: true,
   239	    });
   240	
   241	    series.setData(candleData);
   242	    seriesRef.current = series;
   243	
   244	    const smaData = computeSmaData(candleData, SMA_PERIOD);
   245	    if (smaData.length > 0) {
   246	      const smaSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, {
   247	        color: SMA_COLOR,
   248	        lineWidth: SMA_WIDTH,
   249	        lineStyle: LineStyle.Solid,
   250	        priceLineVisible: false,
   251	        lastValueVisible: false,
   252	        crosshairMarkerVisible: false,
   253	      });
   254	      smaSeries.setData(smaData);
   255	    }
   256	
   257	    const pivotPrimitive = new PivotLinesPrimitive();
   258	    const forecastTargetsPrimitive = new ForecastTargetsPrimitive();
   259	    series.attachPrimitive(pivotPrimitive);
   260	    if (SHOW_FORECAST_TARGET_OVERLAY) {
   261	      series.attachPrimitive(forecastTargetsPrimitive);
   262	    }
   263	
   264	    // Forecast target zones should be projected near the most recent bars, not full-width.
   265	    if (SHOW_FORECAST_TARGET_OVERLAY) {
   266	      const targetStartIndex = Math.max(0, candleData.length - 16);
   267	      const targetStartTime = candleData[targetStartIndex]?.time;
   268	      forecastTargetsPrimitive.setTargetZones(targetZones, targetStartTime);
   269	    }
   270	
   271	    // Fib rendering: anchored start, pivot zone fill, and structural-break lock.
   272	    const fibCandles: CandleData[] = bars.map((b, idx) => ({
   273	      time: idx,
   274	      open: b.open,
   275	      high: b.high,
   276	      low: b.low,
   277	      close: b.close,
   278	      volume: b.volume,
   279	    }));
   280	
   281	    const currentPrice = bars[bars.length - 1]?.close;
   282	    let fibResult = fibLockedRef.current;
   283	    if (
   284	      !fibResult ||
   285	      currentPrice == null ||
   286	      currentPrice > fibResult.anchorHigh ||
   287	      currentPrice < fibResult.anchorLow
   288	    ) {
   289	      fibResult = calculateFibonacciMultiPeriod(fibCandles);
   290	      fibLockedRef.current = fibResult;
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    if (fibResult) {
   294	      const anchorIdx = Math.min(
   295	        fibResult.anchorHighBarIndex,
   296	        fibResult.anchorLowBarIndex,
   297	      );
   298	      const anchorStartTime = candleData[anchorIdx]?.time;
   299	      pivotPrimitive.setFibResult(fibResult, anchorStartTime);
   300	    } else {
   301	      pivotPrimitive.setFibResult(null);
   302	    }
   303	
   304	    // Set initial visible range: last N bars + right padding
   305	    if (!fitCalledRef.current && candleData.length > 0) {
   306	      const total = candleData.length;
   307	      const visible = Math.min(INITIAL_VISIBLE_BARS, total);
   308	      chart.timeScale().setVisibleLogicalRange({
   309	        from: Math.max(0, total - visible),
   310	        to: total - 1 + RIGHT_PADDING_BARS,
   311	      });
   312	      fitCalledRef.current = true;
   313	    }
   314	
   315	    // Responsive resize
   316	    let disposed = false;
   317	    const observer = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
   318	      if (disposed || !entries[0]) return;
   319	      const { width, height: h } = entries[0].contentRect;
   320	      try {
   321	        chart.applyOptions({ width, height: h });
   322	      } catch {
   323	        /* disposed */
   324	      }
   325	    });
   326	    observer.observe(containerRef.current);
   327	
   328	    return () => {
   329	      disposed = true;
   330	      observer.disconnect();
   331	      try {
   332	        if (SHOW_FORECAST_TARGET_OVERLAY) {
   333	          series.detachPrimitive(forecastTargetsPrimitive);
   334	        }
   335	        series.detachPrimitive(pivotPrimitive);
   336	        chart.remove();
   337	      } catch {
   338	        /* disposed */
   339	      }
   340	    };
   341	  }, [bars, targetZones]);
   342	
   343	  const changeColor = priceChange >= 0 ? "#26C6DA" : "#EC0000";
   344	
   345	  return (
   346	    <div
   347	      className="relative w-full rounded-xl overflow-hidden border border-white/5 flex flex-col"
   348	      style={{
   349	        background: "linear-gradient(180deg, #131722 0%, #0d1117 100%)",
   350	        height: typeof height === "number" ? `${height}px` : height,
   351	      }}
   352	    >
   353	      {/* Header */}
   354	      <div className="flex-shrink-0 flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-2 border-b border-white/5">
   355	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   356	          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   357	            <div className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-cyan-400 shadow-lg shadow-cyan-400/50" />
   358	            <span className="text-sm font-semibold text-white tracking-tight">
   359	              ZL1!
   360	            </span>

    39	    }));

    44	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    45	      source: "mkt.price_15m",

    52	      { status: 500 },

     1	import { redirect } from "next/navigation";

     4	import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
     5	import { hasEnvVars } from "@/lib/utils";
     6	
     7	export async function requireAuthenticatedApiRequest() {
     8	  if (!hasEnvVars) {
     9	    return NextResponse.json(
    10	      { ok: false, error: "Supabase auth env vars are not configured" },
    11	      { status: 503 },
    12	    );
    13	  }
    14	
    15	  const supabase = await createClient();
    16	  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.getClaims();
    17	
    18	  if (error || !data?.claims) {
    19	    return NextResponse.json(
    20	      { ok: false, error: "Authentication required" },
    21	      { status: 401 },
    22	    );
    23	  }
    24	
    25	  return null;
    26	}
    27	
    28	export async function requireAuthenticatedPageSession() {
    29	  if (!hasEnvVars) {
    30	    redirect("/auth/login");
    31	  }
    32	
    33	  const supabase = await createClient();
    34	  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.getClaims();
    35	
    36	  if (error || !data?.claims) {
    37	    redirect("/auth/login");
    38	  }
    39	}

     4	export type AiSnapshotMeta = {
     5	  generatedAt: string;
     6	  model: string;
     7	  reasoningEffort: string;
     8	  source?: string;
     9	  refreshScheduleEt?: string;
    10	};
    11	
    12	export type AiEnvelopeMeta = {
    13	  enabled: boolean;
    14	  source: string;
    15	  model: string | null;
    16	  reasoningEffort: string | null;
    17	  generatedAt: string | null;
    18	  refreshScheduleEt: string | null;
    19	};
    20	
    21	const TRUSTED_SNAPSHOT_SOURCES = new Set([
    22	  "trusted-live-pull",
    23	  "trusted-authority-pull",
    24	  "ai-daily-refresh",
    25	  "chatgpt-pro-subscription-refresh",
    26	]);
    27	
    28	const SNAPSHOT_LOADERS: Record<string, () => Promise<unknown>> = {
    29	  "app/config/dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json": async () =>
    30	    (await import("@/app/config/dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json")).default,
    31	  "app/config/strategy-posture-ai.json": async () =>
    32	    (await import("@/app/config/strategy-posture-ai.json")).default,
    33	  "app/config/sentiment-overview-ai.json": async () =>
    34	    (await import("@/app/config/sentiment-overview-ai.json")).default,
    35	  "app/config/legislation-feed-ai.json": async () =>
    36	    (await import("@/app/config/legislation-feed-ai.json")).default,
    37	  "app/config/vegas-intel-ai.json": async () =>
    38	    (await import("@/app/config/vegas-intel-ai.json")).default,
    39	};
    40	
    41	function coerceSnapshot<T extends object>(parsed: unknown): (T & AiSnapshotMeta) | null {
    42	  if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== "object") return null;
    43	  const candidate = parsed as T & Partial<AiSnapshotMeta>;
    44	  if (typeof candidate.generatedAt !== "string" || candidate.generatedAt.trim().length === 0) return null;
    45	  if (typeof candidate.model !== "string" || candidate.model.trim().length === 0) return null;
    46	  if (typeof candidate.reasoningEffort !== "string" || candidate.reasoningEffort.trim().length === 0) return null;
    47	  const snapshotSource =
    48	    typeof candidate.source === "string" ? candidate.source.trim().toLowerCase() : "";
    49	  if (!TRUSTED_SNAPSHOT_SOURCES.has(snapshotSource)) return null;
    50	  return candidate as T & AiSnapshotMeta;
    51	}
    52	
    53	export async function readAiSnapshot<T extends object>(
    54	  relativePathFromRepoRoot: string,
    55	): Promise<(T & AiSnapshotMeta) | null> {
    56	  try {
    57	    const loader = SNAPSHOT_LOADERS[relativePathFromRepoRoot];
    58	    if (loader) {
    59	      const imported = await loader();
    60	      const validated = coerceSnapshot<T>(imported);
    61	      if (validated) return validated;
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    const fullPath = path.join(
    65	      /* turbopackIgnore: true */ process.cwd(),
    66	      relativePathFromRepoRoot,
    67	    );
    68	    const raw = await readFile(fullPath, "utf8");
    69	    return coerceSnapshot<T>(JSON.parse(raw));
    70	  } catch {
    71	    return null;
    72	  }
    73	}
    74	
    75	export function toAiEnvelopeMeta(snapshot: AiSnapshotMeta | null): AiEnvelopeMeta {
    76	  return {
    77	    enabled: Boolean(snapshot),
    78	    source: snapshot?.source ?? "none",
    79	    model: snapshot?.model ?? null,
    80	    reasoningEffort: snapshot?.reasoningEffort ?? null,
    81	    generatedAt: snapshot?.generatedAt ?? null,
    82	    refreshScheduleEt: snapshot?.refreshScheduleEt ?? null,
    83	  };
    84	}

    45	function toDateIso(ts: number | null | undefined): string | null {
    46	  if (typeof ts !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(ts)) return null;
    47	  return new Date(ts * 1000).toISOString();
    48	}
    49	
    50	async function fetchJson<T>(url: string): Promise<T | null> {
    51	  try {
    52	    const res = await fetch(url, {
    53	      headers: {
    54	        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Codex Trusted Source Pull)",
    55	      },
    56	      cache: "no-store",
    57	    });
    58	    if (!res.ok) return null;
    59	    return (await res.json()) as T;
    60	  } catch {
    61	    return null;
    62	  }
    63	}
    64	
    65	async function fetchYahooSeries(symbol: string): Promise<TrustedSeriesPoint & { change5d: number | null }> {
    66	  const url = `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodeURIComponent(
    67	    symbol,
    68	  )}?interval=1d&range=1mo`;
    69	  const payload = await fetchJson<YahooChartResponse>(url);
    70	  const result = payload?.chart?.result?.[0];
    71	  const timestamps = result?.timestamp ?? [];
    72	  const closes = result?.indicators?.quote?.[0]?.close ?? [];
    73	
    74	  let latestClose: number | null = null;
    75	  let latestTs: number | null = null;
    76	  for (let i = closes.length - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1) {
    77	    const close = closes[i];
    78	    if (typeof close === "number" && Number.isFinite(close)) {
    79	      latestClose = close;
    80	      latestTs = timestamps[i] ?? null;
    81	      break;
    82	    }
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  let anchorClose: number | null = null;
    86	  let validSeen = 0;
    87	  for (let i = closes.length - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1) {
    88	    const close = closes[i];
    89	    if (typeof close !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(close)) continue;
    90	    validSeen += 1;
    91	    if (validSeen >= 6) {
    92	      anchorClose = close;
    93	      break;
    94	    }
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  const change5d =
    98	    latestClose !== null && anchorClose !== null && anchorClose !== 0
    99	      ? (latestClose - anchorClose) / anchorClose
   100	      : null;
   101	
   102	  return {
   103	    value: latestClose,
   104	    asOf: toDateIso(latestTs ?? result?.meta?.regularMarketTime ?? null),
   105	    source: "Yahoo Finance",
   106	    url,
   107	    change5d,
   108	  };
   109	}
   110	
   111	async function fetchFredLatest(seriesId: string): Promise<TrustedSeriesPoint> {
   112	  const url = `https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=${encodeURIComponent(seriesId)}`;
   113	  try {
   114	    const res = await fetch(url, { cache: "no-store" });
   115	    if (!res.ok) {
   116	      return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   117	    }
   118	    const csv = await res.text();
   119	    const lines = csv.trim().split("\n");
   120	    for (let i = lines.length - 1; i >= 1; i -= 1) {
   121	      const row = lines[i];
   122	      const [date, value] = row.split(",", 2);
   123	      if (!date || !value || value === ".") continue;
   124	      const n = Number(value);
   125	      if (!Number.isFinite(n)) continue;
   126	      return {
   127	        value: n,
   128	        asOf: new Date(`${date}T00:00:00Z`).toISOString(),
   129	        source: "FRED",
   130	        url,
   131	      };
   132	    }
   133	  } catch {
   134	    return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   135	  }
   136	  return { value: null, asOf: null, source: "FRED", url };
   137	}
   138	
   139	export async function fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot(): Promise<TrustedMarketSnapshot> {
   140	  const [zl, cl, cny, vix, ovx] = await Promise.all([
   141	    fetchYahooSeries("ZL=F"),
   142	    fetchYahooSeries("CL=F"),
   143	    fetchYahooSeries("CNY=X"),
   144	    fetchFredLatest("VIXCLS"),
   145	    fetchFredLatest("OVXCLS"),
   146	  ]);
   147	
   148	  return {
   149	    fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   150	    zl,
   151	    cl,
   152	    cny,
   153	    vix,
   154	    ovx,
   155	  };
   156	}
   157	
   158	export function uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(snapshot: TrustedMarketSnapshot): string[] {
   159	  return [snapshot.zl.url, snapshot.cl.url, snapshot.cny.url, snapshot.vix.url, snapshot.ovx.url]
   160	    .filter((u, idx, arr) => typeof u === "string" && arr.indexOf(u) === idx);
   161	}
