    97	function toNumber(value: unknown): number | null {
    98	  if (value === null || value === undefined) return null;
    99	  const n = Number(value);
   100	  return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : null;
   101	}
   102	
   103	function normalizeMetricKey(key: string): string {
   104	  return key.trim().toLowerCase();
   105	}
   106	
   107	function coerceScore(value: number | null): number | null {
   108	  if (value === null) return null;
   109	  const abs = Math.abs(value);
   110	  if (abs <= 1) return Math.round(abs * 1000) / 10;
   111	  if (abs <= 100) return Math.round(abs * 10) / 10;
   112	  return 100;
   113	}
   114	
   115	function mapFactorToDriver(factor: string): DriverKey | null {
   116	  const f = factor.toLowerCase();
   117	  if (f.includes("vix") || f.includes("volatility")) return "vix_stress";
   118	  if (f.includes("crush")) return "crush_pressure";
   119	  if (f.includes("china") || f.includes("cny")) return "china_tension";
   120	  if (f.includes("tariff") || f.includes("policy") || f.includes("tpu") || f.includes("emv")) return "tariff_threat";
   121	  if (f.includes("energy") || f.includes("crude") || f.includes("oil") || f.includes("cl")) return "energy_stress";
   122	  return null;
   123	}
   124	
   125	function levelFor(driver: DriverKey, score: number | null): string {
   126	  if (score === null) return "No Data";
   127	
   128	  if (driver === "vix_stress") {
   129	    if (score >= 85) return "Gap Risk";
   130	    if (score >= 65) return "Fund Exit";
   131	    if (score >= 45) return "Elevated";
   132	    return "Calm";
   133	  }
   134	  if (driver === "crush_pressure") {
   135	    if (score >= 85) return "Plant Idling";
   136	    if (score >= 65) return "Margin Squeeze";
   137	    if (score >= 45) return "Breakeven Risk";
   138	    return "Strong";
   139	  }
   140	  if (driver === "china_tension") {
   141	    if (score >= 80) return "Active Conflict";
   142	    if (score >= 60) return "Trade Diversion";
   143	    if (score >= 40) return "Monitor Flows";
   144	    return "Brazil Favored";
   145	  }
   146	  if (driver === "tariff_threat") {
   147	    if (score >= 85) return "Systemic Shock";
   148	    if (score >= 65) return "Elevated Risk";
   149	    if (score >= 45) return "Watch";
   150	    return "Contained";
   151	  }
   152	  if (score >= 85) return "Crisis";
   153	  if (score >= 65) return "Supply Shock";
   154	  if (score >= 45) return "Elevated";
   155	  return "Low Risk";
   156	}
   157	
   158	function regimeFor(score: number | null): string {
   159	  if (score === null) return "NO_DATA";
   160	  if (score >= 70) return "PRESSURE";
   161	  if (score >= 45) return "WATCH";
   162	  return "CALM";
   163	}
   164	
   165	function outlookFromScore(score: number): "BULLISH" | "NEUTRAL" | "CAUTIOUS" | "BEARISH" {
   166	  if (score >= 70) return "BEARISH";
   167	  if (score >= 55) return "CAUTIOUS";
   168	  if (score >= 35) return "NEUTRAL";
   169	  return "BULLISH";
   170	}
   171	
   172	function colorFromScore(score: number): string {
   173	  if (score >= 70) return "#EF4444";
   174	  if (score >= 55) return "#EF7300";
   175	  if (score >= 35) return "#EAB308";
   176	  return "#22C55E";
   177	}
   178	
   179	function headlineFor(driverName: string, score: number | null): string {
   180	  if (score === null) return `Hard stop: ${driverName} lacks verified promoted data`;
   181	  if (score >= 70) return `${driverName}: elevated pressure`;
   182	  if (score >= 45) return `${driverName}: moderate risk`;
   183	  return `${driverName}: stable`;
   184	}
   185	
   186	function getMetric(metrics: Map<string, number | null>, keys: string[]): number | null {
   187	  for (const key of keys) {
   188	    const value = metrics.get(normalizeMetricKey(key));
   189	    if (value !== undefined) return value;
   190	  }
   191	  return null;
   192	}

    60	    ],
    61	    inferenceConstraints: [
    62	      "Do not collapse all drivers into a single undifferentiated summary.",
    63	      "Do not hide conflict between top-ranked channels.",
    64	      "Do not assign confidence without data freshness context.",
    65	    ],
    66	    outputRequirements: [
    67	      "Provide ordered driver stack with concise rationale.",
    68	      "Call out interaction risk explicitly.",
    69	      "Translate ranking into procurement monitoring priorities.",
    70	    ],
    71	  },
    72	  riskMetrics: {
    73	    cardTopic: "Buyer Risk Math and Tail Exposure",
    74	    strategicObjective:
    75	      "Express near-term procurement risk using explicit asymmetry, latency risk, and exposure math so decision timing is grounded in quantified downside.",
    76	    neuralConnectionThesis:
    77	      "For buyers, decision latency under elevated risk networks is often more expensive than controlled over-hedging; quantified asymmetry clarifies when faster action is warranted.",
    78	    quantResearchProtocol: [
    79	      "Assess near-term tail asymmetry from active driver network state.",
    80	      "Measure decision-latency risk versus controlled execution-risk tradeoff.",
    81	      "Classify exposure state as contained, elevated, or escalation-prone.",
    82	      "Tie risk statements to buyer-side cost outcomes and cadence impact.",

    88	    ],

    91	      "Explain latency risk versus hedge risk comparison.",
    92	      "Provide specific cadence/monitoring recommendation.",
    93	    ],
    94	  },

    96	
    97	function buildProvenance(
    98	  generatedAt: string,
    99	  tradeDate: string | null,
   100	  cardKey: keyof StrategyCards,
   101	  trustedUrls: string[],
   102	): AiCardProvenance {
   103	  return {
   104	    asOf: tradeDate ?? generatedAt,
   105	    generatedAt,
   106	    method: "verified-db-and-trusted-market-pull",
   107	    sourceFeeds: [
   108	      "analytics.market_posture",
   109	      "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   110	      "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   111	      ...trustedUrls,
   112	    ],
   113	    sourceRecords: [
   114	      {
   115	        source: "analytics.market_posture",
   116	        table: "analytics.market_posture",
   117	        recordHint: tradeDate ? `trade_date=${tradeDate}` : "latest row",
   118	        observedAt: tradeDate ?? undefined,
   119	      },
   120	      {
   121	        source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   122	        table: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   123	        recordHint: `card=${cardKey}`,
   124	        observedAt: tradeDate ?? generatedAt,
   125	      },
   126	    ],
   127	    notes: [
   128	      "Buyer posture is interpreted from verified table and market-source pulls.",
   129	      "Hard stop is applied when required driver or market evidence is missing.",
   130	    ],
   131	  };
   132	}
   133	
   134	export async function GET() {
   135	  try {
   136	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
   137	    if (unauthorized) {
   138	      return unauthorized;
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   142	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<StrategyAiSnapshot>(
   143	      "app/config/strategy-posture-ai.json",
   144	    );
   145	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   146	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   147	
   148	    const [{ data: row, error }, { data: attributionRows }, { data: metricRows }] = await Promise.all([
   149	      supabase
   150	        .schema("analytics")
   151	        .from("market_posture")
   152	        .select("posture, rationale, trade_date")
   153	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   154	        .limit(1)
   155	        .maybeSingle(),
   156	      supabase
   157	        .schema("analytics")
   158	        .from("driver_attribution_1d")
   159	        .select("trade_date, rank, factor, contribution")
   160	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   161	        .order("rank", { ascending: true })
   162	        .limit(40),
   163	      supabase
   164	        .schema("analytics")
   165	        .from("dashboard_metrics")
   166	        .select("trade_date, metric_key, metric_value")
   167	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   168	        .limit(300),
   169	    ]);
   170	
   171	    if (error) {
   172	      return NextResponse.json(
   173	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
   174	        { status: 500 },
   175	      );
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    const latestAttrDate = attributionRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   179	    const latestAttr = (attributionRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestAttrDate);
   180	    const topFactors = latestAttr
   181	      .slice(0, 4)
   182	      .map((r) => `${String(r.factor)} (${Number(r.contribution).toFixed(2)})`);
   183	
   184	    const latestMetricDate = metricRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   185	    const latestMetrics = (metricRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestMetricDate);
   186	    const metricMap = new Map<string, number>();
   187	    for (const rowMetric of latestMetrics) {
   188	      const n = Number(rowMetric.metric_value);
   189	      if (Number.isFinite(n)) {
   190	        metricMap.set(String(rowMetric.metric_key).toLowerCase(), n);
   191	      }
   192	    }
   193	
   194	    const dbVix = metricMap.get("vix_value") ?? null;
   195	    const dbOvx = metricMap.get("ovx_value") ?? null;
   196	    const dbCl5d = metricMap.get("cl_change_5d") ?? metricMap.get("crude_oil_change_5d") ?? null;
   197	    const liveVix = trustedMarket.vix.value ?? dbVix;
   198	    const liveOvx = trustedMarket.ovx.value ?? dbOvx;
   199	    const liveCl5d = trustedMarket.cl.change5d ?? dbCl5d;
   200	
   201	    const dbPosture: StrategyPosture | null = row
   202	      ? {
   203	          posture: row.posture as StrategyPosture["posture"],
   204	          rationale: row.rationale ?? "",
   205	          updatedAt: row.trade_date ?? new Date().toISOString(),
   206	        }
   207	      : null;
   208	
   209	    const posture: StrategyPosture | null = aiSnapshot?.posture ?? dbPosture;
   210	    const generatedAt = aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   211	    const tradeDate = row?.trade_date ?? posture?.updatedAt ?? null;
   212	    const stagedExecutionBias =
   213	      posture?.posture === "ACCUMULATE"
   214	        ? "Staged accumulation remains favored."
   215	        : posture?.posture === "WAIT"
   216	          ? "Hold execution optionality and avoid urgency."
   217	          : "Defer new exposure until risk compression is visible.";
   218	    const volatilityLine =
   219	      liveVix !== null && liveOvx !== null
   220	        ? `VIX ${liveVix.toFixed(2)} and OVX ${liveOvx.toFixed(2)} remain active volatility constraints.`
   221	        : "Hard stop: verified VIX/OVX volatility reads are unavailable.";
   222	    const crudeLine =
   223	      liveCl5d !== null
   224	        ? `CL 5-day change is ${(liveCl5d * 100).toFixed(2)}%, informing near-term pass-through risk.`
   225	        : "Hard stop: verified CL 5-day change is unavailable.";
   226	    const topFactorLine =
   227	      topFactors.length > 0
   228	        ? `Latest verified attribution stack: ${topFactors.join(", ")}.`
   229	        : "Hard stop: no verified attribution rows available in analytics.driver_attribution_1d.";
   230	    const noTrustedFeeds =
   231	      liveVix === null && liveOvx === null && liveCl5d === null;
   232	
   233	    const fallbackCards: StrategyCards = {
   234	      contractImpactCalculator: {
   235	        title: "Contract Impact Calculator",
   236	        body: noTrustedFeeds
   237	          ? "Hard stop: trusted market evidence from Yahoo/FRED is unavailable, so contract-impact guidance is blocked."
   238	          : `${stagedExecutionBias} ${volatilityLine} ${crudeLine}`,
   239	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.contractImpactCalculator,
   240	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "contractImpactCalculator", trustedUrls),
   241	      },
   242	      factorWaterfall: {
   243	        title: "Factor Waterfall",
   244	        body: topFactorLine,
   245	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.factorWaterfall,
   246	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "factorWaterfall", trustedUrls),
   247	      },
   248	      riskMetrics: {
   249	        title: "Risk Metrics",
   250	        body: noTrustedFeeds
   251	          ? "Hard stop: trusted volatility and crude metrics are unavailable; buyer risk-math output is blocked until verified feeds recover."
   252	          : `${volatilityLine} ${crudeLine} Decision-latency risk should be prioritized over single-window execution risk when these channels remain elevated.`,
   253	        strategicSpecialInstructions: STRATEGY_INSTRUCTIONS.riskMetrics,
   254	        provenance: buildProvenance(generatedAt, tradeDate, "riskMetrics", trustedUrls),
   255	      },
   256	    };
   257	
   258	    const rawCards = aiSnapshot?.cards ?? fallbackCards;
   259	    const cards: StrategyCards = {
   260	      contractImpactCalculator: {

   143	function buildProvenance(
   144	  generatedAt: string,
   145	  updatedAt: string,
   146	  cardKey: string,
   147	  trustedUrls: string[],
   148	): AiCardProvenance {
   149	  return {
   150	    asOf: updatedAt,
   151	    generatedAt,
   152	    method: "verified-db-and-trusted-market-pull",
   153	    sourceFeeds: ["alt.news_events", "mkt.cftc_1w", ...trustedUrls],
   154	    sourceRecords: [
   155	      {
   156	        source: "alt.news_events",
   157	        table: "alt.news_events",
   158	        recordHint: "latest 7-day sentiment rows",
   159	        observedAt: updatedAt,
   160	      },
   161	      {
   162	        source: "mkt.cftc_1w",
   163	        table: "mkt.cftc_1w",
   164	        recordHint: "symbol=ZL latest observation",
   165	        observedAt: updatedAt,
   166	      },
   167	      {
   168	        source: "trusted-market-pull",
   169	        recordHint: `card=${cardKey}`,
   170	        observedAt: generatedAt,
   171	      },
   172	    ],
   173	    notes: [
   174	      "Narrative output is buyer-facing procurement interpretation from verified rows.",
   175	      "Hard stop language is required when market or flow evidence is missing.",
   176	    ],
   177	  };
   178	}

   180	export async function GET() {
   181	  try {
   182	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
   183	    if (unauthorized) {
   184	      return unauthorized;
   185	    }
   186	
   187	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   188	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<SentimentAiSnapshot>(
   189	      "app/config/sentiment-overview-ai.json",
   190	    );
   191	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   192	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   193	
   194	    const sevenDaysAgo = new Date();
   195	    sevenDaysAgo.setDate(sevenDaysAgo.getDate() - 7);
   196	    const oneDayAgo = new Date();
   197	    oneDayAgo.setDate(oneDayAgo.getDate() - 1);
   198	
   199	    const [{ data: newsRows, error: newsError }, { data: cftcRow }] = await Promise.all([
   200	      supabase
   201	        .schema("alt")
   202	        .from("news_events")
   203	        .select("source, published_at, specialist_tags")
   204	        .gte("published_at", sevenDaysAgo.toISOString())
   205	        .order("published_at", { ascending: false })
   206	        .limit(300),
   207	      supabase
   208	        .schema("mkt")
   209	        .from("cftc_1w")
   210	        .select("observation_date, payload")
   211	        .eq("symbol", "ZL")
   212	        .order("observation_date", { ascending: false })
   213	        .limit(1)
   214	        .maybeSingle(),
   215	    ]);
   216	
   217	    if (newsError) {
   218	      return NextResponse.json(
   219	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: newsError.message },
   220	        { status: 500 },
   221	      );
   222	    }
   223	
   224	    const rows = newsRows ?? [];
   225	    const headlineCount = rows.length;
   226	    const last24h = rows.filter((row) => new Date(row.published_at).getTime() >= oneDayAgo.getTime()).length;
   227	    const pulseRatio = headlineCount > 0 ? last24h / Math.max(1, headlineCount / 7) : 0;
   228	    const flowState = pulseRatio >= 1.5 ? "elevated" : pulseRatio >= 1 ? "balanced" : "subdued";
   229	
   230	    const sourceSet = new Set<string>();
   231	    const tagCounts = new Map<string, number>();
   232	    for (const row of rows) {
   233	      if (row.source) sourceSet.add(row.source);
   234	      const tags = Array.isArray(row.specialist_tags) ? row.specialist_tags : [];
   235	      for (const tag of tags) {
   236	        const normalized = String(tag).trim();
   237	        if (!normalized) continue;
   238	        tagCounts.set(normalized, (tagCounts.get(normalized) ?? 0) + 1);
   239	      }
   240	    }
   241	    const topTags = Array.from(tagCounts.entries())
   242	      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
   243	      .slice(0, 3)
   244	      .map(([tag]) => tag);
   245	    const tagPhrase = topTags.length > 0 ? topTags.join(", ") : "no dominant specialist cluster";
   246	
   247	    const cotPayload = cftcRow?.payload as Record<string, unknown> | null;
   248	    const cotBias = (cotPayload?.bias as string) ?? "neutral";
   249	    const cotBiasNorm = cotBias.trim().toLowerCase();
   250	    const cotBiasLabel =
   251	      cotBiasNorm.includes("bull") ? "bullish bias" : cotBiasNorm.includes("bear") ? "bearish bias" : "neutral bias";
   252	    const cotBiasScore = cotBiasNorm.includes("bull") ? 20 : cotBiasNorm.includes("bear") ? -20 : 0;
   253	
   254	    const vix = trustedMarket.vix.value;
   255	    const ovx = trustedMarket.ovx.value;

   259	    const macroTrustedMissing = vix === null && ovx === null && cl5d === null;
   260	    const volatilityPenalty =

    77	  "antidumping",
    78	  "rule",
    79	  "program",
    80	  "appropriation",
    81	  "act",
    82	];
    83	
    84	const AG_SOY_TAG_HINTS = new Set(["crush", "biofuel", "palm", "tariff", "china", "energy"]);
    85	
    86	function includesAnyTerm(text: string, terms: readonly string[]): boolean {
    87	  return terms.some((term) => {
    88	    const normalized = term.trim().toLowerCase();
    89	    if (!normalized) return false;
    90	    const escaped = normalized
    91	      .replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
    92	      .replace(/\s+/g, "\\s+");
    93	    const pattern = new RegExp(`(^|[^a-z0-9])${escaped}([^a-z0-9]|$)`, "i");
    94	    return pattern.test(text);
    95	  });
    96	}
    97	
    98	function normalizeTags(raw: unknown): string[] {
    99	  if (!Array.isArray(raw)) return [];
   100	  return raw
   101	    .map((tag) => String(tag).trim())
   102	    .filter((tag) => tag.length > 0);
   103	}
   104	
   105	function isAgSoyPolicyRelevant(params: {
   106	  title: string;
   107	  summary?: string | null;
   108	  tags?: string[];
   109	}): boolean {
   110	  const textBlob = `${params.title} ${params.summary ?? ""}`.toLowerCase();
   111	  const primaryMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_PRIMARY_TERMS);
   112	  const contextMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_CONTEXT_TERMS);
   113	  const policyMatch = includesAnyTerm(textBlob, AG_SOY_POLICY_TERMS);
   114	  const tagSet = new Set((params.tags ?? []).map((tag) => tag.toLowerCase()));
   115	  const tagHint = Array.from(tagSet).some((tag) => AG_SOY_TAG_HINTS.has(tag));
   116	
   117	  if (primaryMatch) return true;
   118	  if (contextMatch && (policyMatch || tagHint)) return true;
   119	  if (tagHint && includesAnyTerm(textBlob, ["usda", "epa", "farm", "agricult", "renewable", "biofuel"])) {
   120	    return true;
   121	  }
   122	  return false;
   123	}
   124	
   125	function dedupeLegislationItems(items: LegislationItem[]): LegislationItem[] {
   126	  const seen = new Set<string>();
   127	  const deduped: LegislationItem[] = [];
   128	  for (const item of items) {
   129	    const key = `${item.source}::${item.title}`;
   130	    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
   131	    seen.add(key);
   132	    deduped.push(item);
   133	  }
   134	  return deduped;
   135	}
   136

   245	        source: "trusted-market-pull",

   247	        observedAt: generatedAt,
   248	      },

   250	  };
   251	}

   161	export async function GET() {
   162	  try {
   163	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
   164	    if (unauthorized) {
   165	      return unauthorized;
   166	    }
   167	
   168	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   169	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiSnapshot<VegasIntelAiSnapshot>(
   170	      "app/config/vegas-intel-ai.json",
   171	    );
   172	    const trustedMarket = await fetchTrustedMarketSnapshot();
   173	    const trustedUrls = uniqueTrustedMarketUrls(trustedMarket);
   174	
   175	    const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
   176	    const in14 = new Date();
   177	    in14.setDate(in14.getDate() + 14);
   178	    const in30 = new Date();
   179	    in30.setDate(in30.getDate() + 30);
   180	
   181	    const [
   182	      { data: eventRows, error: evtError },
   183	      { data: restaurantRows, error: restError },
   184	      { data: fryerRows },
   185	      { data: scoreRows },
   186	      { data: impactRows },
   187	    ] = await Promise.all([
   188	      supabase
   189	        .schema("vegas")
   190	        .from("events")
   191	        .select("id, event_name, event_date")
   192	        .gte("event_date", today)
   193	        .order("event_date", { ascending: true })
   194	        .limit(200),
   195	      supabase
   196	        .schema("vegas")
   197	        .from("restaurants")
   198	        .select("id, restaurant_name, account_status")
   199	        .eq("account_status", "active")
   200	        .limit(500),
   201	      supabase
   202	        .schema("vegas")
   203	        .from("fryers")
   204	        .select("restaurant_id, fryer_count")
   205	        .limit(500),
   206	      supabase
   207	        .schema("vegas")
   208	        .from("customer_scores")
   209	        .select("restaurant_id, score_date, score")
   210	        .order("score_date", { ascending: false })
   211	        .limit(500),
   212	      supabase
   213	        .schema("vegas")
   214	        .from("event_impact")
   215	        .select("event_id, restaurant_id, impact_score")
   216	        .order("impact_score", { ascending: false })
   217	        .limit(500),
   218	    ]);
   219	
   220	    if (evtError) {
   221	      return NextResponse.json(
   222	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: evtError.message },
   223	        { status: 500 },
   224	      );
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    if (restError) {
   228	      return NextResponse.json(
   229	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: restError.message },
   230	        { status: 500 },
   231	      );
   232	    }
   233	
   234	    const events = eventRows ?? [];
   235	    const activeRestaurants = restaurantRows ?? [];
   236	    const fryers = fryerRows ?? [];
   237	    const customerScores = scoreRows ?? [];
   238	    const eventImpacts = impactRows ?? [];
   239	
   240	    const activeEvents = events.length;
   241	    const highPriority = activeRestaurants.length;
   242	    const events14d = events.filter((e) => new Date(e.event_date).getTime() <= in14.getTime()).length;
   243	    const events30d = events.filter((e) => new Date(e.event_date).getTime() <= in30.getTime()).length;
   244	    const nextEvent = events[0] ?? null;
   245	
   246	    const knownFryerCounts = fryers
   247	      .map((row) => Number(row.fryer_count))
   248	      .filter((count) => Number.isFinite(count) && count >= 0);
   249	    const totalFryers = knownFryerCounts.reduce((acc, count) => acc + count, 0);
   250	    const lowRedundancySites = knownFryerCounts.filter((count) => count <= 1).length;
   251	    const unknownFryerSites = Math.max(0, fryers.length - knownFryerCounts.length);
   252	
   253	    const latestScoreDate = customerScores[0]?.score_date ?? null;
   254	    const latestScores = customerScores.filter((row) => row.score_date === latestScoreDate);
   255	    const scoredAccounts = latestScores
   256	      .map((row) => Number(row.score))
   257	      .filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value));
   258	    const avgScore =
   259	      scoredAccounts.length > 0
   260	        ? scoredAccounts.reduce((acc, value) => acc + value, 0) / scoredAccounts.length

exec

   117	def _parse_csv(value: str | None, default: tuple[str, ...]) -> tuple[str, ...]:
   118	    if not value:
   119	        return default
   120	    parsed = tuple(item.strip().upper() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip())
   121	    return parsed or default
   122	
   123	
   124	def _parse_float(value: str | None, default: float) -> float:
   125	    if not value:
   126	        return default
   127	    return float(value)
   128	
   129	
   130	def _as_utc_datetime(value: Any) -> datetime | None:
   131	    if value is None:
   132	        return None
   133	    if isinstance(value, datetime):
   134	        return value.astimezone(timezone.utc) if value.tzinfo else value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   135	    if isinstance(value, date):
   136	        return datetime(value.year, value.month, value.day, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   137	    if isinstance(value, str):
   138	        parsed = pd.to_datetime(value, errors="coerce", utc=True)
   139	        if pd.isna(parsed):
   140	            return None
   141	        return parsed.to_pydatetime()
   142	    return None
   143	
   144	
   145	def _age_hours(value: Any, now_utc: datetime) -> float | None:
   146	    dt = _as_utc_datetime(value)
   147	    if dt is None:
   148	        return None
   149	    return (now_utc - dt).total_seconds() / 3600.0
   150	
   151	
   152	def _age_days(value: Any, now_utc: datetime) -> float | None:
   153	    hours = _age_hours(value, now_utc)
   154	    if hours is None:
   155	        return None
   156	    return hours / 24.0
   157	
   158	
   159	def _ratio(numerator: int, denominator: int) -> float:
   160	    if denominator <= 0:
   161	        return 0.0
   162	    return float(numerator) / float(denominator)
   163	
   164	
   165	def _format_issues(issues: list[str]) -> str:
   166	    if len(issues) <= _ISSUE_PREVIEW_LIMIT:
   167	        return "; ".join(issues)
   168	    shown = "; ".join(issues[:_ISSUE_PREVIEW_LIMIT])
   169	    return f"{shown}; ... {len(issues) - _ISSUE_PREVIEW_LIMIT} more"
   170	
   171	
   172	def _table_exists(cur: Any, *, schema: str, table: str) -> bool:
   173	    cur.execute(
   174	        """
   175	        SELECT EXISTS (
   176	          SELECT 1
   177	          FROM information_schema.tables
   178	          WHERE table_schema = %s AND table_name = %s
   179	        )
   180	        """,
   181	        (schema, table),
   182	    )
   183	    return bool(cur.fetchone()[0])
   184	
   185	
   186	def _column_exists(cur: Any, *, schema: str, table: str, column: str) -> bool:
   187	    cur.execute(
   188	        """
   189	        SELECT EXISTS (
   190	          SELECT 1
   191	          FROM information_schema.columns
   192	          WHERE table_schema = %s AND table_name = %s AND column_name = %s
   193	        )
   194	        """,
   195	        (schema, table, column),
   196	    )
   197	    return bool(cur.fetchone()[0])
   198

     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import argparse
     4	from typing import Callable
     5	
     6	from . import build_matrix
     7	from . import generate_forward_forecasts
     8	from . import generate_specialist_features
     9	from . import generate_specialist_signals
    10	from . import generate_target_zones
    11	from . import promote_to_cloud
    12	from . import run_garch
    13	from . import run_monte_carlo
    14	from . import training_readiness_gate
    15	from . import train_models
    16	
    17	PhaseFn = Callable[..., dict[str, object]]
    18	
    19	PHASE_MAP: dict[str, PhaseFn] = {
    20	    "matrix": build_matrix.run,
    21	    "specialists": generate_specialist_features.run,
    22	    "signals": generate_specialist_signals.run,
    23	    "train-readiness": training_readiness_gate.run,
    24	    "train": train_models.run,
    25	    "forecast": generate_forward_forecasts.run,
    26	    "monte-carlo": run_monte_carlo.run,
    27	    "garch": run_garch.run,
    28	    "target-zones": generate_target_zones.run,
    29	    "promote": promote_to_cloud.run,
    30	}
    31	
    32	PIPELINE_ORDER = [
    33	    "matrix",
    34	    "specialists",
    35	    "signals",
    36	    "train-readiness",
    37	    "train",
    38	    "forecast",
    39	    "garch",
    40	    "monte-carlo",
    41	    "target-zones",
    42	    "promote",
    43	]
    44	
    45	
    46	def run_phase(
    47	    name: str,
    48	    *,
    49	    dry_run: bool,
    50	    approve_training: bool,
    51	    approve_promotion: bool,
    52	) -> dict[str, object]:
    53	    fn = PHASE_MAP[name]
    54	    if name == "train":
    55	        return fn(dry_run=dry_run, approved=approve_training)
    56	    if name == "promote":
    57	        return fn(dry_run=dry_run or not approve_promotion, approved=approve_promotion)
    58	    return fn(dry_run=dry_run)
    59	
    60	
    61	def main() -> None:
    62	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ZINC Fusion v16 pipeline runner")
    63	    parser.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Run all phases in pipeline order")
    64	    parser.add_argument("--phase", choices=PIPELINE_ORDER, help="Run one phase")
    65	    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Show intent only, no writes")
    66	    parser.add_argument(
    67	        "--approve-training",
    68	        action="store_true",
    69	        help="Explicit training approval gate for non-dry-run train phase",
    70	    )
    71	    parser.add_argument(
    72	        "--approve-promotion",
    73	        action="store_true",
    74	        help="Explicit cloud promotion approval gate for non-dry-run promote phase",
    75	    )
    76	    args = parser.parse_args()
    77	
    78	    selected: list[str]
    79	    if args.all:
    80	        selected = PIPELINE_ORDER
    81	    elif args.phase:
    82	        selected = [args.phase]
    83	    else:
    84	        parser.error("Select --all or --phase")
    85	
    86	    for phase in selected:
    87	        result = run_phase(
    88	            phase,
    89	            dry_run=args.dry_run,
    90	            approve_training=args.approve_training,
    91	            approve_promotion=args.approve_promotion,
    92	        )
    93	        print(result)
    94	
    95	
    96	if __name__ == "__main__":
    97	    main()

     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import json
     4	import os
     5	import subprocess
     6	import sys
     7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
     8	from urllib.parse import urlparse
     9	from uuid import uuid4
    10

    14	from .artifacts import (
    15	    feature_columns,
    16	    matrix_path,
    17	    model_artifact_dir,
    18	    read_parquet,
    19	    target_columns,
    20	    training_runs_path,
    21	    write_parquet,
    22	)

    25	from .training_readiness_gate import run as run_training_readiness

    27	
    28	class TrainingApprovalError(RuntimeError):
    29	    pass
    30	
    31	
    32	class TrainingContractError(RuntimeError):
    33	    pass
    34	
    35	
    36	LOCKED_PRESET = "best_quality"
    37	LOCKED_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS = 3600
    38	LOCKED_NUM_BAG_FOLDS = 5
    39	LOCKED_NUM_STACK_LEVELS = 1
    40	LOCKED_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE = "full_zoo"
    41	LOCKED_TRAINING_SOURCE = "local_postgres"
    42	LOCKED_MIN_DIRECTIONAL_ACCURACY = 0.70
    43	MAX_DEFAULT_NUM_CPUS = 8
    44	MODEL_SELECTION_MODES = {"full_zoo", "include_only", "exclude_only"}
    45	TRAINING_SOURCE_MODES = {"local_postgres", "parquet_artifacts"}
    46	INTERNAL_MODEL_TYPE_EXCLUSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    47	    "DUMMY",
    48	    "ENS_WEIGHTED",
    49	    "SIMPLE_ENS_WEIGHTED",
    50	    "GBM_PREP",
    51	    "AG_IMAGE_NN",
    52	    "AG_TEXT_NN",
    53	    "FASTTEXT",
    54	)

    56	
    57	def _parse_int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
    58	    value = os.getenv(name)
    59	    if value is None or not value.strip():
    60	        return default
    61	    return int(value)
    62	
    63	
    64	def _parse_float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
    65	    value = os.getenv(name)
    66	    if value is None or not value.strip():
    67	        return default
    68	    return float(value)
    69	
    70	
    71	def _parse_csv_env(name: str, default: str = "") -> list[str]:
    72	    raw = os.getenv(name, default)
    73	    return [item.strip() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]
    74	
    75	
    76	def _normalize_model_types(values: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    77	    normalized: list[str] = []
    78	    seen: set[str] = set()
    79	    for item in values:
    80	        key = item.strip().upper()
    81	        if not key or key in seen:
    82	            continue
    83	        seen.add(key)
    84	        normalized.append(key)
    85	    return normalized
    86	
    87	
    88	def _resolve_num_cpus() -> int:
    89	    requested = _parse_int_env("AUTOGLUON_NUM_CPUS", 0)
    90	    if requested > 0:
    91	        return requested
    92	    cpu_total = os.cpu_count() or 1
    93	    return max(1, min(cpu_total, MAX_DEFAULT_NUM_CPUS))
    94	
    95	
    96	def _apply_runtime_thread_guard(*, num_cpus: int) -> dict[str, int]:
    97	    thread_budget = max(1, min(num_cpus, MAX_DEFAULT_NUM_CPUS))
    98	    runtime_vars = {
    99	        "OMP_NUM_THREADS": thread_budget,
   100	        "OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS": thread_budget,
   101	        "MKL_NUM_THREADS": thread_budget,
   102	        "VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS": thread_budget,
   103	        "NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS": thread_budget,
   104	    }
   105	    for key, value in runtime_vars.items():
   106	        os.environ[key] = str(value)
   107	    return runtime_vars
   108	
   109	
   110	def _resolve_runtime_guard(*, dry_run: bool) -> dict[str, object]:
   111	    allow_unsafe = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_UNSAFE_RUNTIME", "0").strip() == "1"
   112	    requires_guard = sys.version_info[:2] >= (3, 12)
   113	    runtime = {
   114	        "python_version": f"{sys.version_info.major}.{sys.version_info.minor}.{sys.version_info.micro}",
   115	        "python_executable": sys.executable,
   116	        "allow_unsafe_runtime": allow_unsafe,
   117	        "requires_safe_runtime": requires_guard,
   118	    }
   119	    if requires_guard and not allow_unsafe and not dry_run:
   120	        raise TrainingContractError(
   121	            "Unsafe AutoGluon runtime detected on Python 3.12+. "
   122	            "Use the project's .venv-ag311 environment for approved training runs, "
   123	            "or set AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_UNSAFE_RUNTIME=1 only for explicit crash-risk override runs."
   124	        )
   125	    return runtime
   126	
   127	
   128	def _resolve_openmp_runtime() -> dict[str, object]:
   129	    try:
   130	        import lightgbm  # noqa: F401
   131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
   132	        return {"detected": False, "error": f"lightgbm import failed: {exc}", "openmp_libraries": []}
   133	    try:
   134	        import sklearn  # noqa: F401
   135	    except Exception:
   136	        # sklearn import is optional for this guard; proceed with whatever is loaded.
   137	        pass
   138	
   139	    try:
   140	        from threadpoolctl import threadpool_info
   141	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
   142	        return {"detected": False, "error": f"threadpoolctl unavailable: {exc}", "openmp_libraries": []}
   143	
   144	    info = threadpool_info()
   145	    openmp_libraries = sorted(
   146	        {
   147	            str(entry.get("filepath"))
   148	            for entry in info
   149	            if entry.get("user_api") == "openmp" and entry.get("filepath")
   150	        }
   151	    )
   152	    return {
   153	        "detected": True,
   154	        "openmp_libraries": openmp_libraries,
   155	        "openmp_library_count": len(openmp_libraries),
   156	    }
   157	
   158	
   159	def _discover_full_zoo_model_types() -> list[str]:
   160	    from autogluon.tabular.trainer.model_presets import presets
   161	
   162	    registry_keys = getattr(presets.ag_model_registry, "keys", [])
   163	    if not isinstance(registry_keys, list) or not registry_keys:
   164	        raise TrainingContractError("unable to discover AutoGluon model registry keys for full-zoo selection")
   165	
   166	    internal = set(INTERNAL_MODEL_TYPE_EXCLUSIONS)
   167	    selected: list[str] = []
   168	    for raw_key in registry_keys:
   169	        key = str(raw_key).strip().upper()
   170	        if not key:
   171	            continue
   172	        if key in internal:
   173	            continue
   174	        if key.startswith("IM_"):
   175	            continue
   176	        selected.append(key)
   177	    selected = _normalize_model_types(selected)
   178	    if not selected:
   179	        raise TrainingContractError("full-zoo selection resolved to 0 model types")
   180	    return selected
   181	
   182	
   183	def _resolve_model_selection(*, allow_override: bool) -> tuple[str, list[str], list[str], list[str], bool]:
   184	    selection_mode = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE", LOCKED_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE).strip().lower()
   185	    if selection_mode not in MODEL_SELECTION_MODES:
   186	        raise TrainingContractError(
   187	            f"unsupported AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE={selection_mode!r}; "
   188	            f"valid={sorted(MODEL_SELECTION_MODES)}"
   189	        )
   190	
   191	    requested_included = _normalize_model_types(_parse_csv_env("AUTOGLUON_INCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES"))
   192	    requested_excluded = _normalize_model_types(_parse_csv_env("AUTOGLUON_EXCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES"))
   193	    available_full_zoo = _discover_full_zoo_model_types()
   194	    available_set = set(available_full_zoo)
   195	
   196	    unknown_included = sorted([value for value in requested_included if value not in available_set])
   197	    unknown_excluded = sorted([value for value in requested_excluded if value not in available_set])
   198	    if unknown_included or unknown_excluded:
   199	        raise TrainingContractError(
   200	            "unknown model types in selection request: "
   201	            + f"included_unknown={unknown_included}, excluded_unknown={unknown_excluded}"
   202	        )
   203	
   204	    if selection_mode == "full_zoo":
   205	        if not allow_override and (requested_included or requested_excluded):
   206	            raise TrainingContractError(
   207	                "AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE=full_zoo does not accept "
   208	                "AUTOGLUON_INCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES or AUTOGLUON_EXCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES unless "
   209	                "AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_CONTRACT_OVERRIDE=1."
   210	            )
   211	        included_model_types = available_full_zoo
   212	        excluded_model_types: list[str] = []
   213	    elif selection_mode == "include_only":
   214	        if not requested_included:
   215	            raise TrainingContractError(
   216	                "AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE=include_only requires AUTOGLUON_INCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES"
   217	            )
   218	        if requested_excluded:
   219	            raise TrainingContractError(
   220	                "AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE=include_only cannot be combined with "
   221	                "AUTOGLUON_EXCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES"
   222	            )
   223	        included_model_types = requested_included
   224	        excluded_model_types = []
   225	    else:
   226	        if requested_included:
   227	            raise TrainingContractError(
   228	                "AUTOGLUON_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE=exclude_only cannot be combined with "
   229	                "AUTOGLUON_INCLUDED_MODEL_TYPES"
   230	            )
   231	        included_model_types = []
   232	        excluded_model_types = requested_excluded
   233	
   234	    return selection_mode, included_model_types, excluded_model_types, available_full_zoo, selection_mode == LOCKED_MODEL_SELECTION_MODE
   235	
   236	
   237	def _resolve_training_source(*, allow_override: bool) -> str:
   238	    source = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_TRAINING_SOURCE", LOCKED_TRAINING_SOURCE).strip().lower() or LOCKED_TRAINING_SOURCE
   239	    if source not in TRAINING_SOURCE_MODES:
   240	        raise TrainingContractError(
   241	            f"unsupported AUTOGLUON_TRAINING_SOURCE={source!r}; valid={sorted(TRAINING_SOURCE_MODES)}"
   242	        )
   243	    if not allow_override and source != LOCKED_TRAINING_SOURCE:
   244	        raise TrainingContractError(
   245	            "Hard lock violation on AG training source: "
   246	            + f"AUTOGLUON_TRAINING_SOURCE={source!r} (locked={LOCKED_TRAINING_SOURCE!r}). "
   247	            + "Set AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_CONTRACT_OVERRIDE=1 only for explicit, approved override runs."
   248	        )
   249	    return source
   250	
   251	
   252	def _resolve_training_contract() -> tuple[int, str, int, int, str, list[str], list[str], list[str], str, bool, bool]:
   253	    allow_override = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_CONTRACT_OVERRIDE", "0").strip() == "1"
   254	    requested_time_limit = _parse_int_env("AUTOGLUON_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS", LOCKED_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS)
   255	    requested_presets = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_PRESETS", LOCKED_PRESET)
   256	    requested_num_bag_folds = _parse_int_env("AUTOGLUON_NUM_BAG_FOLDS", LOCKED_NUM_BAG_FOLDS)

   147	
   148	def run(*, dry_run: bool = True, approved: bool = False) -> dict[str, object]:
   149	    matrix = _validate_matrix()
   150	    specialists = {specialist: _validate_specialist(specialist) for specialist in SPECIALISTS}
   151	    signals = _validate_signals()
   152	
   153	    payload: dict[str, object] = {
   154	        "phase": "promote",
   155	        "dry_run": dry_run,
   156	        "approved": approved,
   157	        "reads": [
   158	            str(matrix_path()),
   159	            str(signals_path()),
   160	            *[str(specialist_features_path(specialist)) for specialist in SPECIALISTS],
   161	        ],
   162	        "writes": ["training.matrix_1d", "training.specialist_signals_1d", *[f"training.specialist_features_{s}" for s in SPECIALISTS]],
   163	        "target_columns_stripped_from_matrix_payload": target_columns(matrix.columns),
   164	        "matrix_rows": int(len(matrix)),
   165	        "signal_rows": int(len(signals)),
   166	        "specialist_rows": {specialist: int(len(frame)) for specialist, frame in specialists.items()},
   167	        "status": "dry-run" if dry_run else "pending",
   168	    }
   169	
   170	    if dry_run:
   171	        return payload
   172	    if not approved:
   173	        raise PromotionApprovalError("Cloud promotion requires explicit approval. Pass approved=True or --execute.")
   174	
   175	    db_url = resolve_cloud_db_url()
   176	    if not db_url:
   177	        raise RuntimeError(
   178	            "Cloud DB URL is not set. Configure DATABASE_URL, SUPABASE_DB_URL, POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING, or SUPABASE_POOLER_URL."
   179	        )
   180	
   181	    with psycopg2.connect(db_url, connect_timeout=10, application_name="fusion_promote_to_cloud") as conn:
   182	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
   183	            promoted = {
   184	                "training.matrix_1d": _promote_matrix(cur, matrix),
   185	                "training.specialist_signals_1d": _promote_signals(cur, signals),
   186	            }
   187	            for specialist, frame in specialists.items():
   188	                promoted[f"training.specialist_features_{specialist}"] = _promote_specialist(cur, specialist, frame)
   189	
   190	    payload["status"] = "ok"
   191	    payload["promoted_rows"] = promoted
   192	    payload["promoted_at"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
   193	    return payload
   194	
   195	
   196	def main() -> None:
   197	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate and promote local V16 training artifacts to cloud Supabase")
   198	    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", default=True, help="Validate local artifacts without cloud writes")
   199	    parser.add_argument("--execute", action="store_true", help="Perform approved cloud promotion")
   200	    args = parser.parse_args()
   201	
   202	    result = run(dry_run=not args.execute, approved=args.execute)
   203	    print(result)
   204	
   205	
   206	if __name__ == "__main__":
   207	    main()

   674	def run(*, dry_run: bool = False, approved: bool = False) -> dict[str, object]:
   675	    readiness = run_training_readiness(dry_run=dry_run)
   676	    cfg = load_config()
   677	    target_mode = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_TARGET_MODE", "price").strip().lower() or "price"
   678	    min_directional_accuracy = _parse_float_env("AUTOGLUON_MIN_DIRECTIONAL_ACCURACY", LOCKED_MIN_DIRECTIONAL_ACCURACY)
   679	    labels = [TARGET_TEMPLATE.format(h=horizon) for horizon in HORIZONS]
   680	    run_id = f"{cfg.model_version}-{datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ')}-{uuid4().hex[:8]}"
   681	    runtime_guard = _resolve_runtime_guard(dry_run=dry_run)
   682	    num_cpus = _resolve_num_cpus()
   683	    runtime_thread_guard = _apply_runtime_thread_guard(num_cpus=num_cpus)
   684	    openmp_runtime = _resolve_openmp_runtime()
   685	    allow_multiple_openmp = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_MULTIPLE_OPENMP", "0").strip() == "1"
   686	    strict_openmp_guard = os.getenv("AUTOGLUON_STRICT_OPENMP_GUARD", "0").strip() == "1"
   687	    (
   688	        time_limit,
   689	        presets,
   690	        num_bag_folds,
   691	        num_stack_levels,
   692	        model_selection_mode,
   693	        included_model_types,
   694	        excluded_model_types,
   695	        available_full_zoo,
   696	        training_source,
   697	        contract_lock_enforced,
   698	        model_selection_lock_enforced,
   699	    ) = _resolve_training_contract()
   700	    if num_stack_levels > 0 and num_bag_folds < 2:
   701	        raise TrainingContractError("AUTOGLUON_NUM_STACK_LEVELS requires AUTOGLUON_NUM_BAG_FOLDS >= 2")
   702	    if target_mode not in {"price", "returns"}:
   703	        raise TrainingContractError("AUTOGLUON_TARGET_MODE must be 'price' or 'returns'")
   704	    if min_directional_accuracy <= 0 or min_directional_accuracy > 1:
   705	        raise TrainingContractError("AUTOGLUON_MIN_DIRECTIONAL_ACCURACY must be in (0, 1]")
   706	    if (
   707	        not dry_run
   708	        and openmp_runtime.get("detected", False)
   709	        and int(openmp_runtime.get("openmp_library_count", 0)) > 1
   710	        and (
   711	            bool(runtime_guard.get("requires_safe_runtime", False))
   712	            or strict_openmp_guard
   713	        )
   714	        and not allow_multiple_openmp
   715	    ):
   716	        raise TrainingContractError(
   717	            "Multiple OpenMP runtimes detected in process. "
   718	            + f"libraries={openmp_runtime.get('openmp_libraries', [])}. "
   719	            + "This is a known crash risk for full-zoo runs. Align libraries to one OpenMP runtime "
   720	            + "or set AUTOGLUON_ALLOW_MULTIPLE_OPENMP=1 for explicit risk override."
   721	        )
   722	
   723	    if dry_run:
   724	        frame_status: dict[str, object]
   725	        try:
   726	            frame, label_map = _load_training_frame(target_mode=target_mode, training_source=training_source)
   727	            frame_status = {
   728	                "rows": int(len(frame)),
   729	                "feature_columns": len(feature_columns(frame.columns)),
   730	                "target_columns": list(label_map.values()),
   731	                "trade_date_min": str(frame["trade_date"].min()),
   732	                "trade_date_max": str(frame["trade_date"].max()),
   733	            }
   734	        except Exception as exc:
   735	            frame_status = {"training_source_check": "blocked", "error": str(exc)}
   736	
   737	        return {
   738	            "phase": "train",
   739	            "dry_run": True,
   740	            "approved": approved,
   741	            "readiness": readiness,
   742	            "training_frame": frame_status,
   743	            "labels": labels,
   744	            "target_mode": target_mode,
   745	            "model_run_id_example": run_id,
   746	            "presets": presets,
   747	            "time_limit_seconds": time_limit,
   748	            "num_cpus": num_cpus,
   749	            "num_bag_folds": num_bag_folds,
   750	            "num_stack_levels": num_stack_levels,
   751	            "model_selection_mode": model_selection_mode,
   752	            "training_source": training_source,
   753	            "included_model_types": included_model_types,
   754	            "excluded_model_types": excluded_model_types,
   755	            "available_full_zoo_model_types": available_full_zoo,
   756	            "contract_lock_enforced": contract_lock_enforced,
   757	            "model_selection_lock_enforced": model_selection_lock_enforced,
   758	            "min_directional_accuracy": min_directional_accuracy,
   759	            "runtime_guard": runtime_guard,
   760	            "runtime_thread_guard": runtime_thread_guard,
   761	            "openmp_runtime": openmp_runtime,
   762	            "allow_multiple_openmp": allow_multiple_openmp,
   763	            "strict_openmp_guard": strict_openmp_guard,
   764	            "writes": [str(model_artifact_dir(run_id)), str(training_runs_path())],
   765	            "cloud_writes": [],
   766	            "single_matrix_training_contract": True,
   767	            "status": "dry-run",
   768	        }
   769	
   770	    if not approved:
   771	        raise TrainingApprovalError(
   772	            "Training requires explicit approval. Use dry-run or pass approved=True."
   773	        )
   774	    if not readiness.get("ready", False):
   775	        blockers = readiness.get("blockers", [])
   776	        lines = "\n".join(f"- {item}" for item in blockers)
   777	        raise TrainingReadinessError(
   778	            "Training readiness gate failed. Resolve blockers before running train:\n" + lines
   779	        )
   780	    if time_limit <= 0:
   781	        raise TrainingContractError("AUTOGLUON_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS must be > 0")
   782	
   783	    frame, label_map = _load_training_frame(target_mode=target_mode, training_source=training_source)
   784	    run_dir = model_artifact_dir(run_id)
   785	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=False)
   786	    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
   787	
   788	    summaries = [
   789	        _train_one_horizon(
   790	            frame,
   791	            horizon=horizon,
   792	            label=label_map[horizon],
   793	            target_mode=target_mode,
   794	            run_id=run_id,
   795	            presets=presets,
   796	            time_limit=time_limit,
   797	            num_cpus=num_cpus,
   798	            min_directional_accuracy=min_directional_accuracy,
   799	            model_selection_mode=model_selection_mode,
   800	            included_model_types=included_model_types,
   801	            excluded_model_types=excluded_model_types,
   802	            num_bag_folds=num_bag_folds,
   803	            num_stack_levels=num_stack_levels,
   804	        )
   805	        for horizon in HORIZONS
   806	    ]
   807	    failed_accuracy = [item for item in summaries if not item.get("passes_accuracy_threshold", False)]
   808	    accuracy_gate_passed = len(failed_accuracy) == 0
   809	    run_status = "ok" if accuracy_gate_passed else "accuracy-blocked"
   810	
   811	    run_record = {
   812	        "run_id": run_id,
   813	        "model_version": cfg.model_version,
   814	        "started_at": started_at,
   815	        "finished_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   816	        "git_sha": _git_sha(),
   817	        "matrix_path": str(matrix_path()) if training_source == "parquet_artifacts" else "local_postgres.training.matrix_1d",
   818	        "signals_path": "not_used_single_matrix_contract",
   819	        "model_artifact_dir": str(run_dir),
   820	        "presets": presets,
   821	        "time_limit_seconds": time_limit,
   822	        "num_cpus": num_cpus,
   823	        "target_mode": target_mode,
   824	        "num_bag_folds": num_bag_folds,
   825	        "num_stack_levels": num_stack_levels,

   830	        "contract_lock_enforced": contract_lock_enforced,
   831	        "model_selection_lock_enforced": model_selection_lock_enforced,
   832	        "min_directional_accuracy": min_directional_accuracy,
   833	        "accuracy_gate_passed": accuracy_gate_passed,
   834	        "failed_accuracy_horizons": [item["horizon_days"] for item in failed_accuracy],

   837	        "openmp_runtime": openmp_runtime,

    13	_MATRIX_SQL = """
    14	WITH
    15	zl AS (
    16	  SELECT
    17	    bucket_ts::date AS trade_date,
    18	    open::numeric AS open,
    19	    high::numeric AS high,
    20	    low::numeric AS low,
    21	    close::numeric AS close,
    22	    volume::numeric AS volume
    23	  FROM mkt.price_1d
    24	  WHERE symbol = 'ZL'
    25	),
    26	zs AS (
    27	  SELECT bucket_ts::date AS trade_date, close::numeric AS close
    28	  FROM mkt.price_1d
    29	  WHERE symbol = 'ZS'
    30	),
    31	zm AS (
    32	  SELECT bucket_ts::date AS trade_date, close::numeric AS close
    33	  FROM mkt.price_1d
    34	  WHERE symbol = 'ZM'
    35	),
    36	cl AS (
    37	  SELECT bucket_ts::date AS trade_date, close::numeric AS close
    38	  FROM mkt.price_1d
    39	  WHERE symbol = 'CL'
    40	),
    41	joined AS (
    42	  SELECT
    43	    z.trade_date,
    44	    z.open,
    45	    z.high,
    46	    z.low,
    47	    z.close,
    48	    z.volume,
    49	    zs.close AS zs_close,
    50	    zm.close AS zm_close,
    51	    cl.close AS cl_close
    52	  FROM zl z
    53	  LEFT JOIN zs USING (trade_date)
    54	  LEFT JOIN zm USING (trade_date)
    55	  LEFT JOIN cl USING (trade_date)
    56	),
    57	features AS (
    58	  SELECT
    59	    trade_date,
    60	    open,
    61	    high,
    62	    low,
    63	    close,
    64	    volume,
    65	    zs_close,
    66	    zm_close,
    67	    cl_close,
    68	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 1) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_1d,
    69	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 5) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_5d,
    70	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 10) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_10d,
    71	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 20) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_20d,
    72	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 30) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_30d,
    73	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 60) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_60d,
    74	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 90) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_90d,
    75	    (close / NULLIF(LAG(close, 180) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS ret_180d,
    76	    (volume / NULLIF(LAG(volume, 1) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS vol_chg_1d,
    77	    (volume / NULLIF(LAG(volume, 5) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date), 0)) - 1 AS vol_chg_5d,
    78	    AVG(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 49 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS ma_50,
    79	    AVG(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 99 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS ma_100,
    80	    AVG(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 199 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS ma_200,
    81	    STDDEV_SAMP(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 19 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS std_20,
    82	    STDDEV_SAMP(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 59 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS std_60,
    83	    close - AVG(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 19 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS mean_revert_20,
    84	    close - AVG(close) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date ROWS BETWEEN 49 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS mean_revert_50,
    85	    close - COALESCE(zs_close, close) AS spread_zl_zs,
    86	    close - COALESCE(zm_close, close) AS spread_zl_zm,
    87	    close - COALESCE(cl_close, close) AS spread_zl_cl,
    88	    close / NULLIF(zs_close, 0) AS ratio_zl_zs,
    89	    close / NULLIF(zm_close, 0) AS ratio_zl_zm,
    90	    close / NULLIF(cl_close, 0) AS ratio_zl_cl,
    91	    CASE
    92	      WHEN zs_close IS NULL OR zm_close IS NULL THEN NULL
    93	      ELSE (0.022 * zm_close) + (11.0 * (close / 100.0)) - (zs_close / 100.0)
    94	    END AS crush_margin_board,
    95	    CASE
    96	      WHEN zs_close IS NULL OR zm_close IS NULL THEN NULL
    97	      ELSE (
    98	        (
    99	          (0.022 * zm_close) + (11.0 * (close / 100.0)) - (zs_close / 100.0)
   100	        ) - LAG((0.022 * zm_close) + (11.0 * (close / 100.0)) - (zs_close / 100.0), 20) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date)
   101	      )
   102	    END AS crush_margin_chg_20d,
   103	    CASE
   104	      WHEN zs_close IS NULL OR zm_close IS NULL THEN NULL
   105	      ELSE ((11.0 * (close / 100.0)) / NULLIF((0.022 * zm_close) + (11.0 * (close / 100.0)), 0)) * 100.0
   106	    END AS crush_oil_share_pct,
   107	    LEAD(close, 30) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date) AS target_price_30d,
   108	    LEAD(close, 90) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date) AS target_price_90d,
   109	    LEAD(close, 180) OVER (ORDER BY trade_date) AS target_price_180d
   110	  FROM joined
   111	)
   112	SELECT *
   113	FROM features
   114	WHERE ret_1d IS NOT NULL
   115	  AND ret_5d IS NOT NULL
   116	  AND ret_10d IS NOT NULL
   117	  AND ret_20d IS NOT NULL
   118	  AND ret_30d IS NOT NULL
   119	  AND ret_60d IS NOT NULL
   120	  AND ret_90d IS NOT NULL
   121	  AND ret_180d IS NOT NULL
   122	  AND ma_50 IS NOT NULL
   123	  AND ma_100 IS NOT NULL
   124	  AND ma_200 IS NOT NULL
   125	  AND std_20 IS NOT NULL
   126	  AND std_60 IS NOT NULL
   127	ORDER BY trade_date;
   128	"""
   129	
   130	
   131	def _to_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
   132	    if value is None:
   133	        return None
   134	    if isinstance(value, Decimal):
   135	        return float(value)
   136	    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
   137	        return float(value)
   138	    return float(value)
   139	
   140	
   141	def _normalize_matrix(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
   142	    if df.empty:
   143	        raise RuntimeError("matrix build returned 0 rows")
   144	
   145	    df = df.copy()
   146	    df["trade_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["trade_date"]).dt.date
   147	
   148	    for column in df.columns:
   149	        if column == "trade_date":
   150	            continue
   151	        df[column] = df[column].map(_to_float).astype("float64")
   152	
   153	    expected_targets = [TARGET_TEMPLATE.format(h=h) for h in HORIZONS]
   154	    missing_targets = [column for column in expected_targets if column not in df.columns]
   155	    if missing_targets:
   156	        raise RuntimeError(f"matrix is missing target columns: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
   157	
   158	    feature_cols = feature_columns(df.columns)
   159	    label_cols = target_columns(df.columns)
   160	    if not feature_cols:
   161	        raise RuntimeError("matrix has no non-target feature columns")
   162	    return df[["trade_date", *feature_cols, *label_cols]]
   163	
   164	
   165	def run(*, dry_run: bool = False) -> dict[str, object]:
   166	    output_path = matrix_path()
   167	    if dry_run:
   168	        return {
   169	            "phase": "matrix",
   170	            "dry_run": True,
   171	            "reads": ["mkt.price_1d"],
   172	            "writes": [str(output_path)],
   173	            "cloud_writes": [],
   174	            "status": "dry-run",
   175	        }
   176	
   177	    db_url = resolve_cloud_db_url()
   178	    if not db_url:
   179	        raise RuntimeError(
   180	            "Cloud DB URL is not set. Configure DATABASE_URL, SUPABASE_DB_URL, POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING, or SUPABASE_POOLER_URL."
   181	        )
   182	
   183	    with psycopg2.connect(db_url, connect_timeout=10, application_name="fusion_build_matrix") as conn:
   184	        df = pd.read_sql_query(_MATRIX_SQL, conn)
   185	
   186	    matrix = _normalize_matrix(df)
   187	    write_parquet(matrix, output_path)
   188	
   189	    return {
   190	        "phase": "matrix",
   191	        "dry_run": False,
   192	        "reads": ["mkt.price_1d"],
   193	        "writes": [str(output_path)],
   194	        "cloud_writes": [],
   195	        "status": "ok",
   196	        "rows_written": int(len(matrix)),
   197	        "feature_columns": len(feature_columns(matrix.columns)),
   198	        "target_columns": target_columns(matrix.columns),
   199	        "target_non_null_counts": {
   200	            column: int(matrix[column].notna().sum()) for column in target_columns(matrix.columns)
   201	        },
   202	        "target_null_counts": {
   203	            column: int(matrix[column].isna().sum()) for column in target_columns(matrix.columns)
   204	        },
   205	        "trade_date_min": str(matrix["trade_date"].min()),
   206	        "trade_date_max": str(matrix["trade_date"].max()),
   207	    }

    27	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    28	class PipelineConfig:
    29	    supabase_db_url: str | None
    30	    supabase_pooler_url: str | None
    31	    local_training_db_url: str
    32	    model_version: str
    33	    local_data_dir: Path
    34	    model_artifact_dir: Path
    35	
    36	
    37	def _first_non_empty(*values: str | None) -> str | None:
    38	    for value in values:
    39	        if value:
    40	            return value
    41	    return None
    42	
    43	
    44	def load_config() -> PipelineConfig:
    45	    import os
    46	
    47	    local_data_dir = Path(os.getenv("FUSION_LOCAL_DATA_DIR", "data/fusion")).expanduser()
    48	    model_artifact_dir = Path(os.getenv("FUSION_MODEL_ARTIFACT_DIR", "models/fusion")).expanduser()
    49	
    50	    return PipelineConfig(
    51	        # DATABASE_URL is the canonical cloud DB contract for V16.
    52	        # SUPABASE_DB_URL remains a compatibility alias for existing scripts.
    53	        supabase_db_url=_first_non_empty(
    54	            os.getenv("DATABASE_URL"),
    55	            os.getenv("SUPABASE_DB_URL"),
    56	            # Vercel env pull exposes direct Postgres URL under this key.
    57	            os.getenv("POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING"),
    58	        ),
    59	        # Keep compatibility with environments that expose POSTGRES_URL.
    60	        supabase_pooler_url=_first_non_empty(
    61	            os.getenv("SUPABASE_POOLER_URL"),
    62	            os.getenv("POSTGRES_URL"),
    63	        ),
    64	        local_training_db_url=_first_non_empty(
    65	            os.getenv("FUSION_LOCAL_TRAINING_DB_URL"),
    66	            os.getenv("LOCAL_TRAINING_DB_URL"),
    67	            DEFAULT_LOCAL_TRAINING_DB_URL,
    68	        )
    69	        or DEFAULT_LOCAL_TRAINING_DB_URL,
    70	        model_version=os.getenv("MODEL_VERSION", "v16-scaffold"),
    71	        local_data_dir=local_data_dir,
    72	        model_artifact_dir=model_artifact_dir,
    73	    )
    74	
    75	
    76	def resolve_cloud_db_url() -> str | None:
    77	    cfg = load_config()
    78	    return cfg.supabase_db_url or cfg.supabase_pooler_url
    79	
    80	
    81	def resolve_local_training_db_url() -> str:
    82	    return load_config().local_training_db_url

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Fail-closed guard runner for ZINC Fusion V16.
     3	
     4	The guard writes an audit log on every run and reports one final status:
     5	PASS only when every required check passes; INCOMPLETE when any check fails,
     6	is unavailable, times out, or is not run.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import argparse
    12	import json
    13	import subprocess
    14	import sys
    15	from dataclasses import dataclass
    16	from datetime import datetime, timezone
    17	from pathlib import Path
    18	
    19	
    20	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    21	LOG_ROOT = ROOT / "logs" / "fusion-guard"
    22	
    23	PASS = "PASS"
    24	FAIL = "FAIL"
    25	NOT_RUN = "NOT RUN"
    26	
    27	AUTHORITY_DOCS = (
    28	    "AGENTS.md",
    29	    "docs/INDEX.md",
    30	    "docs/MASTER_PLAN.md",
    31	    "docs/agent-safety-gates.md",
    32	    "docs/runbooks/session-startup.md",
    33	    "docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md",
    34	)
    35	
    36	PRODUCT_PREFIXES = (
    37	    "app/",
    38	    "components/",
    39	    "lib/",
    40	    "python/",
    41	    "scripts/",
    42	    "supabase/",
    43	    "package.json",
    44	    "package-lock.json",
    45	    "eslint.config.mjs",
    46	)
    47	
    48	DOC_PREFIXES = (
    49	    "AGENTS.md",
    50	    "docs/",
    51	    ".github/",
    52	    ".kilo/",
    53	)
    54

    73	
    74	@dataclass
    75	class Check:
    76	    name: str
    77	    status: str
    78	    detail: str
    79	
    80	    def line(self) -> str:
    81	        return f"{self.status}: {self.name} - {self.detail}"
    82	
    83	
    84	def utc_stamp() -> str:
    85	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
    86	
    87	
    88	def rel(path: Path) -> str:
    89	    try:
    90	        return str(path.relative_to(ROOT))
    91	    except ValueError:
    92	        return str(path)
    93	
    94	
    95	def run_cmd(argv: list[str], *, timeout: int) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
    96	    try:
    97	        result = subprocess.run(
    98	            argv,
    99	            cwd=ROOT,
   100	            text=True,
   101	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   102	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   103	            timeout=timeout,
   104	            check=False,
   105	        )
   106	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
   107	        return None, str(exc)
   108	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
   109	        output = exc.stdout or ""
   110	        if isinstance(output, bytes):
   111	            output = output.decode(errors="replace")
   112	        return -124, f"timeout after {timeout}s\n{output}"
   113	    return result.returncode, result.stdout
   114	
   115	
   116	def git_lines(args: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   117	    code, output = run_cmd(["git", *args], timeout=30)
   118	    if code != 0:
   119	        return []
   120	    return [line.strip() for line in output.splitlines() if line.strip()]
   121	
   122	
   123	def untracked_files() -> list[str]:
   124	    return git_lines(["ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"])
   125	
   126	
   127	def changed_files(mode: str) -> tuple[list[str], Check | None]:
   128	    if mode == "pre-commit":
   129	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   130	        return files, None
   131	
   132	    if mode == "pre-push":
   133	        upstream = git_lines(["rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "--symbolic-full-name", "@{upstream}"])
   134	        if not upstream:
   135	            return [], Check("upstream range", NOT_RUN, "no upstream configured; cannot check full push range")
   136	        files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR", f"{upstream[0]}...HEAD"])
   137	        return files, Check("upstream range", PASS, upstream[0])
   138	
   139	    files = git_lines(["diff", "--name-only", "--diff-filter=ACMR"])
   140	    files.extend(untracked_files())
   141	    return sorted(set(files)), None
   142	
   143	
   144	def has_prefix(path: str, prefixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   145	    return path in prefixes or any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in prefixes)
   146	
   147	
   148	def check_authority_docs() -> list[Check]:
   149	    checks: list[Check] = []
   150	    missing = [path for path in AUTHORITY_DOCS if not (ROOT / path).is_file()]
   151	    if missing:
   152	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", FAIL, "missing: " + ", ".join(missing)))
   153	    else:
   154	        checks.append(Check("authority docs", PASS, ", ".join(AUTHORITY_DOCS)))
   155	    return checks
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_changed_file_contract(files: list[str]) -> list[Check]:
   159	    checks: list[Check] = []
   160	    if not files:
   161	        checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, "no changed files in selected scope"))
   162	        return checks
   163	
   164	    product = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, PRODUCT_PREFIXES)]
   165	    docs = [path for path in files if has_prefix(path, DOC_PREFIXES)]
   166	    quality = [path for path in files if path.startswith(QUALITY_PREFIX)]
   167	
   168	    checks.append(Check("changed-file scope", PASS, f"{len(files)} file(s) inspected"))
   169	
   170	    if product and not docs:
   171	        checks.append(
   172	            Check(
   173	                "contract-sync rule",
   174	                FAIL,
   175	                "product/config/tooling changes require docs/contracts in the same change",
   176	            )
   177	        )
   178	    else:
   179	        checks.append(Check("contract-sync rule", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, docs={len(docs)}"))
   180	
   181	    if product and not quality:
   182	        checks.append(
   183	            Check(
   184	                "quality-artifact sync",
   185	                FAIL,
   186	                "product/config/tooling changes require regenerated quality artifacts or explicit incomplete status",
   187	            )
   188	        )
   189	    else:
   190	        checks.append(Check("quality-artifact sync", PASS, f"product={len(product)}, quality={len(quality)}"))
   191	
   192	    if product and quality:
   193	        product_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in product if (ROOT / path).exists())
   194	        quality_mtime = max((ROOT / path).stat().st_mtime for path in quality if (ROOT / path).exists())
   195	        if product_mtime > quality_mtime:
   196	            checks.append(
   197	                Check(
   198	                    "quality-artifact freshness",
   199	                    FAIL,
   200	                    "source/config/tooling edits are newer than generated quality artifacts",
   201	                )
   202	            )
   203	        else:
   204	            checks.append(Check("quality-artifact freshness", PASS, "generated artifacts are not older than source edits"))
   205	
   206	    return checks
   207	
   208	
   209	def forbidden_terms() -> list[str]:
   210	    # Keep these assembled so the repository does not itself contain the banned tokens.
   211	    base = "dock" + "er"
   212	    return [base, base + "file", base + " compose", base + "-compose"]
   213	
   214	
   215	def check_forbidden_runtime_terms() -> Check:
   216	    matches: list[str] = []
   217	    candidates: list[Path] = []
   218	    for item in SCAN_ROOTS:
   219	        root = ROOT / item
   220	        if root.is_file():
   221	            candidates.append(root)
   222	        elif root.is_dir():
   223	            candidates.extend(path for path in root.rglob("*") if path.is_file())
   224	
   225	    ignored_dirs = {"node_modules", ".next", "Data", "data", "models", "logs", "__pycache__"}
   226	    for path in candidates:
   227	        if not path.is_file():
   228	            continue
   229	        parts = set(path.relative_to(ROOT).parts)
   230	        if parts & ignored_dirs:
   231	            continue
   232	        try:
   233	            text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   234	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
   235	            continue
   236	        lowered = text.lower()
   237	        for term in forbidden_terms():
   238	            if term in lowered:
   239	                matches.append(rel(path))
   240	                break
   241	    if matches:
   242	        return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", FAIL, "matches: " + ", ".join(sorted(set(matches))[:20]))
   243	    return Check("runtime-vocabulary scan", PASS, "no forbidden runtime-stack terms found")
   244	
   245	
   246	def check_quality_finalizer() -> Check:
   247	    progress = ROOT / "quality" / "PROGRESS.md"
   248	    if not progress.is_file():
   249	        return Check("quality finalizer status", NOT_RUN, "quality/PROGRESS.md missing")
   250	    text = progress.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   251	    for phase in range(1, 7):
   252	        marker = f"- [x] Phase {phase}"
   253	        if marker not in text:
   254	            return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, f"missing {marker}")
   255	    if "Gate status: ABORTED" in text:
   256	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "latest workbook finalizer is ABORTED")
   257	    if "NOT_RUN" in text or "NOT RUN" in text:
   258	        return Check("quality finalizer status", FAIL, "workbook contains NOT RUN evidence")
   259	    return Check("quality finalizer status", PASS, "all phases marked and no aborted/not-run markers")
   260	
   261	
   262	def command_check(name: str, argv: list[str], timeout: int, *, clean_gate: bool = False) -> Check:
   263	    code, output = run_cmd(argv, timeout=timeout)
   264	    if code is None:
   265	        return Check(name, NOT_RUN, output)
   266	    if code == -124:
   267	        return Check(name, FAIL, output.splitlines()[0])
   268	    if code != 0:
   269	        tail = "\n".join(output.splitlines()[-8:])
   270	        return Check(name, FAIL, f"exit {code}; {tail}")
   271	    if clean_gate and (" WARN" in output or "WARN:" in output or "WARN " in output):
   272	        return Check(name, FAIL, "warnings present; clean gate requires 0 WARN")
   273	    return Check(name, PASS, f"exit {code}")
   274	
   275	
   276	def pytest_check(timeout: int) -> list[Check]:
   277	    checks: list[Check] = []
   278	    version = command_check("pytest availability", ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "--version"], timeout)
   279	    checks.append(version)
   280	    if version.status != PASS:
   281	        return checks
   282	    checks.append(
   283	        command_check(
   284	            "pytest quality tests",
   285	            ["python3", "-m", "pytest", "quality/test_functional.py", "quality/test_regression.py"],
   286	            timeout,
   287	        )
   288	    )
   289	    return checks
   290	
   291	
   292	def full_gate_checks(args: argparse.Namespace) -> list[Check]:
   293	    checks = [
   294	        command_check("npm lint", ["npm", "run", "lint"], args.command_timeout),
   295	        command_check("npm build", ["npm", "run", "build"], args.build_timeout),
   296	        command_check("qplaybook doctor", ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "doctor"], args.command_timeout),
   297	        command_check(
   298	            "qplaybook smoke no-llm",
   299	            ["python3", "scripts/qplaybook.py", "smoke", "--profile", "code", "--no-llm"],
   300	            args.command_timeout,
   301	        ),
   302	    ]
   303	
   304	    mechanical = ROOT / "quality" / "mechanical" / "verify.sh"
   305	    if mechanical.is_file():
   306	        checks.append(command_check("quality mechanical verify", ["bash", str(mechanical)], args.command_timeout))
   307	    else:
   308	        checks.append(Check("quality mechanical verify", NOT_RUN, "quality/mechanical/verify.sh missing"))
   309	
   310	    gate = ROOT / ".claude" / "skills" / "quality-playbook" / "quality_gate.py"
   311	    if gate.is_file():
   312	        checks.append(command_check("quality gate clean", ["python3", str(gate), "."], args.command_timeout, clean_gate=True))
   313	    else:
   314	        checks.append(Check("quality gate clean", NOT_RUN, "installed quality_gate.py missing"))
   315	
   316	    checks.extend(pytest_check(args.command_timeout))
   317	    return checks
   318	
   319	
   320	def write_audit(mode: str, checks: list[Check], files: list[str]) -> Path:

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""ZINC Fusion V16 Quality Playbook wrapper.
     3	
     4	This wrapper keeps the upstream Quality Playbook checkout as the source of
     5	truth, verifies the repo-local installed bundles, and avoids the quarantined
     6	Codex.app binary path by resolving `codex` from PATH.
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import argparse
    12	import json
    13	import os
    14	import signal
    15	import shutil
    16	import subprocess
    17	import sys
    18	import tempfile
    19	from pathlib import Path
    20	from typing import Any

    25	    ROOT / "docs" / "runbooks" / "qplaybook_config.json",

    27	)

    31	    "SKILL.md",
