    48	def load_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
    49	    for config_path in CONFIG_PATHS:
    50	        if config_path.is_file():
    51	            return json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    52	    searched = ", ".join(str(path.relative_to(ROOT)) for path in CONFIG_PATHS)
    53	    raise SystemExit(f"missing config; searched: {searched}")
    54	
    55	
    56	def run_cmd(
    57	    argv: list[str],
    58	    *,
    59	    cwd: Path = ROOT,
    60	    env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    61	    input_text: str | None = None,
    62	    timeout: int = 120,
    63	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]:
    64	    return subprocess.run(
    65	        argv,
    66	        cwd=str(cwd),
    67	        env=env,
    68	        input=input_text,
    69	        text=True,
    70	        capture_output=True,
    71	        timeout=timeout,
    72	        check=False,
    73	    )
    74	
    75	
    76	def print_result(label: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> bool:
    77	    status = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
    78	    suffix = f" - {detail}" if detail else ""
    79	    print(f"{status} {label}{suffix}")
    80	    return ok
    81	
    82	
    83	def normalized_origin(url: str) -> str:
    84	    value = url.strip()
    85	    if value.endswith(".git"):
    86	        value = value[:-4]
    87	    return value
    88	
    89	
    90	def check_repo_root() -> bool:
    91	    result = run_cmd(["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"])
    92	    top = Path(result.stdout.strip()).resolve() if result.stdout.strip() else None
    93	    return print_result("repo root", result.returncode == 0 and top == ROOT.resolve(), str(top or result.stderr.strip()))
    94	
    95	
    96	def check_source(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    97	    source = Path(config["source_repo"])
    98	    expected_origin = normalized_origin(config["source_origin"])
    99	    expected_commit = config["source_commit"]
   100	
   101	    ok_exists = print_result("QPB source checkout", (source / ".git").is_dir(), str(source))
   102	
   103	    origin = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "remote", "get-url", "origin"])
   104	    actual_origin = normalized_origin(origin.stdout)
   105	    ok_origin = print_result(
   106	        "QPB source origin",
   107	        origin.returncode == 0 and actual_origin == expected_origin,
   108	        actual_origin or origin.stderr.strip(),
   109	    )
   110	
   111	    head = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "rev-parse", "HEAD"])
   112	    actual_head = head.stdout.strip()
   113	    ok_head = print_result(
   114	        "QPB source commit",
   115	        head.returncode == 0 and actual_head == expected_commit,
   116	        actual_head or head.stderr.strip(),
   117	    )
   118	
   119	    status = run_cmd(["git", "-C", str(source), "status", "--short"])
   120	    ok_clean = print_result(
   121	        "QPB source clean",
   122	        status.returncode == 0 and not status.stdout.strip(),
   123	        status.stdout.strip() or "clean",
   124	    )
   125	
   126	    return ok_exists and ok_origin and ok_head and ok_clean
   127	
   128	
   129	def resolve_codex_binary(config: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
   130	    configured = config.get("codex_binary", "codex")
   131	    if os.sep in configured:
   132	        resolved = Path(configured)
   133	    else:
   134	        found = shutil.which(configured)
   135	        if found is None:
   136	            return False, f"{configured} not found on PATH"
   137	        resolved = Path(found)
   138	
   139	    real = resolved.resolve()
   140	    blocked = real == BLOCKED_CODEX_PATH or str(real).startswith("/Applications/Codex.app/")
   141	    if blocked:
   142	        return False, f"blocked quarantined Codex.app path: {real}"
   143	
   144	    version = run_cmd([str(real), "--version"])
   145	    first_line = (version.stdout or version.stderr).strip().splitlines()
   146	    detail = first_line[0] if first_line else str(real)
   147	    ok_version = version.returncode == 0 and "codex-cli" in detail
   148	    if not ok_version:
   149	        return False, f"{real}: {detail}"
   150	    return True, f"{real} ({detail})"
   151	
   152	
   153	def check_codex(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   154	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   155	    return print_result("codex binary", ok, detail)
   156	
   157	
   158	def check_installed_bundle(root: Path, label: str) -> bool:
   159	    missing = [item for item in REQUIRED_BUNDLE_PATHS if not (root / item).is_file()]
   160	    ok_required = print_result(
   161	        f"{label} installed bundle",
   162	        not missing,
   163	        str(root) if not missing else "missing: " + ", ".join(missing),
   164	    )
   165	
   166	    nested_git = [path for path in root.rglob(".git") if path.exists()]
   167	    ok_no_git = print_result(
   168	        f"{label} no nested git",
   169	        not nested_git,
   170	        "none" if not nested_git else ", ".join(str(path) for path in nested_git),
   171	    )
   172	    return ok_required and ok_no_git
   173	
   174	
   175	def check_installs(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   176	    ok_all = True
   177	    for item in config["installed_skill_paths"]:
   178	        ok_all = check_installed_bundle(ROOT / item["path"], item["name"]) and ok_all
   179	    return ok_all
   180	
   181	
   182	def doctor(config: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   183	    checks = [
   184	        check_repo_root(),
   185	        check_source(config),
   186	        check_codex(config),
   187	        check_installs(config),
   188	    ]
   189	    return all(checks)
   190	
   191	
   192	def codex_full_access_args(config: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
   193	    return list(config.get("codex_exec_args", []))
   194	
   195	
   196	def profile_model(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> str:
   197	    try:
   198	        return str(config["profiles"][profile]["model"])
   199	    except KeyError as exc:
   200	        choices = ", ".join(sorted(config["profiles"]))
   201	        raise SystemExit(f"unknown profile {profile!r}; choose one of: {choices}") from exc
   202	
   203	
   204	def smoke_llm(config: dict[str, Any], profile: str) -> bool:
   205	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   206	    if not ok:
   207	        return print_result(f"Codex smoke ({profile})", False, detail)
   208	
   209	    codex_path = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   210	    model = profile_model(config, profile)
   211	    expected = f"zinc-fusion-v16-qpb-{profile}-ok"
   212	    command = [
   213	        codex_path,
   214	        "exec",
   215	        *codex_full_access_args(config),
   216	        "-m",
   217	        model,
   218	        "-",
   219	    ]
   220	    result = run_cmd(
   221	        command,
   222	        input_text=f"Return exactly: {expected}\n",
   223	        timeout=120,
   224	    )
   225	    output = result.stdout.strip()
   226	    ok_smoke = result.returncode == 0 and expected in output
   227	    if not ok_smoke and result.stderr:
   228	        print(result.stderr[-2000:], file=sys.stderr)
   229	    return print_result(
   230	        f"Codex smoke ({profile})",
   231	        ok_smoke,
   232	        f"model={model}, returncode={result.returncode}, stdout={output[-120:]}",
   233	    )
   234	
   235	
   236	def cmd_doctor(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   237	    return 0 if doctor(load_config()) else 1
   238	
   239	
   240	def cmd_smoke(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   241	    config = load_config()
   242	    checks = [doctor(config)]
   243	    if args.no_llm:
   244	        print_result(f"Codex smoke ({args.profile})", True, "skipped by --no-llm")
   245	    else:
   246	        checks.append(smoke_llm(config, args.profile))
   247	    return 0 if all(checks) else 1
   248	
   249	
   250	def write_codex_shim(config: dict[str, Any], shim_dir: Path) -> Path:
   251	    ok, detail = resolve_codex_binary(config)
   252	    if not ok:
   253	        raise SystemExit(detail)
   254	    real_codex = detail.split(" (", 1)[0]
   255	    full_args = codex_full_access_args(config)
   256	    shim_path = shim_dir / "codex"
   257	    shim_path.write_text(
   258	        "\n".join(
   259	            [
   260	                "#!/usr/bin/env python3",

    30	def load_qplaybook_module():
    31	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("qplaybook", QPLAYBOOK_PATH)
    32	    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    33	    assert spec.loader is not None
    34	    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    35	    return module

    38	class QPlaybookWrapperContractTest(unittest.TestCase):

    86	            ".yarn/releases/.gitkeep",
    87	            ".yarn/versions/.gitkeep",
    88	        ):
    89	            with self.subTest(relative_path=relative_path):
    90	                self.assertIn(f"!{relative_path}", gitignore)
    91	
    92	    def test_gate_timeout_returns_failure(self):
    93	        qplaybook = load_qplaybook_module()
    94	
    95	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:
    96	            temp_root = Path(temp_dir)
    97	            config_path = temp_root / "docs/runbooks/qplaybook_config.json"
    98	            gate_path = temp_root / ".claude/skills/quality-playbook/quality_gate.py"
    99	            config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True)
   100	            gate_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True)
   101	            config_path.write_text('{"quality_gate_timeout_seconds": 1}', encoding="utf-8")
   102	            gate_path.write_text(
   103	                "import time\n"
   104	                "time.sleep(30)\n",
   105	                encoding="utf-8",
   106	            )
   107	
   108	            old_root = qplaybook.ROOT
   109	            old_config_paths = qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS
   110	            try:
   111	                qplaybook.ROOT = temp_root
   112	                qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS = (config_path,)
   113	                result = qplaybook.cmd_gate(argparse.Namespace())
   114	            finally:
   115	                qplaybook.ROOT = old_root
   116	                qplaybook.CONFIG_PATHS = old_config_paths
   117	
   118	        self.assertEqual(result, 124)
   119	
   120	
   121	if __name__ == "__main__":
   122	    unittest.main()

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	BEGIN;
     2	
     3	DO $$
     4	DECLARE
     5	  rec RECORD;
     6	  policy_name TEXT;
     7	BEGIN
     8	  FOR rec IN
     9	    SELECT schemaname, tablename
    10	    FROM pg_tables
    11	    WHERE schemaname IN ('mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'vegas')
    12	  LOOP
    13	    EXECUTE format('ALTER TABLE %I.%I ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    14	
    15	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS authenticated_read ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    16	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY authenticated_read ON %I.%I FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    17	
    18	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_insert ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    19	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_insert ON %I.%I FOR INSERT TO service_role WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    20	
    21	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_update ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    22	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_update ON %I.%I FOR UPDATE TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    23	
    24	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_delete ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    25	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_delete ON %I.%I FOR DELETE TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    26	  END LOOP;
    27	
    28	  FOR rec IN
    29	    SELECT schemaname, tablename
    30	    FROM pg_tables
    31	    WHERE schemaname = 'ops'
    32	  LOOP
    33	    EXECUTE format('ALTER TABLE %I.%I ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    34	
    35	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_select ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    36	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_select ON %I.%I FOR SELECT TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    37	
    38	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_insert ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    39	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_insert ON %I.%I FOR INSERT TO service_role WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    40	
    41	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_update ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    42	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_update ON %I.%I FOR UPDATE TO service_role USING (true) WITH CHECK (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    43	
    44	    EXECUTE format('DROP POLICY IF EXISTS service_role_delete ON %I.%I', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    45	    EXECUTE format('CREATE POLICY service_role_delete ON %I.%I FOR DELETE TO service_role USING (true)', rec.schemaname, rec.tablename);
    46	  END LOOP;
    47	END $$;
    48	
    49	DO $$
    50	DECLARE
    51	  schema_name TEXT;
    52	BEGIN
    53	  FOR schema_name IN
    54	    SELECT unnest(ARRAY['mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'vegas'])
    55	  LOOP
    56	    EXECUTE format('GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA %I TO authenticated, service_role', schema_name);
    57	    EXECUTE format('GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA %I TO authenticated', schema_name);
    58	    EXECUTE format('GRANT INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA %I TO service_role', schema_name);
    59	    EXECUTE format('GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA %I TO service_role', schema_name);
    60	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT SELECT ON TABLES TO authenticated', schema_name);
    61	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO service_role', schema_name);
    62	    EXECUTE format('ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA %I GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON SEQUENCES TO service_role', schema_name);
    63	  END LOOP;
    64	
    65	  EXECUTE 'GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    66	  EXECUTE 'GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    67	  EXECUTE 'GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA ops TO service_role';
    68	  EXECUTE 'ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA ops GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLES TO service_role';
    69	  EXECUTE 'ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA ops GRANT USAGE, SELECT ON SEQUENCES TO service_role';
    70	END $$;
    71	
    72	DO $$
    73	DECLARE
    74	  rec RECORD;
    75	  idx_name TEXT;
    76	BEGIN
    77	  FOR rec IN
    78	    SELECT table_schema, table_name, column_name
    79	    FROM information_schema.columns
    80	    WHERE table_schema IN ('mkt', 'econ', 'alt', 'supply', 'training', 'forecasts', 'analytics', 'ops', 'vegas')
    81	      AND column_name IN ('bucket_ts', 'observation_date', 'trade_date', 'event_date', 'forecast_date', 'ingested_at', 'created_at')
    82	  LOOP
    83	    idx_name := format('idx_%s_%s_%s', rec.table_schema, rec.table_name, rec.column_name);
    84	    IF length(idx_name) > 60 THEN
    85	      idx_name := 'idx_' || substr(md5(idx_name), 1, 24);
    86	    END IF;
    87	
    88	    EXECUTE format(
    89	      'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS %I ON %I.%I (%I)',
    90	      idx_name,
    91	      rec.table_schema,
    92	      rec.table_name,
    93	      rec.column_name
    94	    );
    95	  END LOOP;
    96	END $$;
    97

     1	# AGENTS.md — ZINC-FUSION-V16
     2	
     3	## What This Project Is
     4	
     5	Commodity procurement forecasting system for ZL (soybean oil futures). Clean-room rebuild of legacy baseline on Supabase — **no code transferred, everything written from scratch.**
     6	
     7	**Client:** US Oil Solutions (Las Vegas)
     8	
     9	- **Chris** (owner) BUYS raw soybean oil by the trainload for 100+ restaurant kitchens (Caesars, Boyd, Resorts World)
    10	- **Kevin** (sales director) uses Vegas Intel to pitch restaurants and schedule service around events
    11	- Chris is a BUYER. ZL going UP = bad for his costs. Strategy language (ACCUMULATE/WAIT) reflects this.
    12	
    13	**The migration plan is your bible:** [`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md) — 1,235 lines, 14 sections, every table/route/job/phase defined.
    14	
    15	---
    16	
    17	## Tech Stack
    18	
    19	| Layer                  | Technology                                              |
    20	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
    21	| **Database**           | Supabase Postgres — cloud only, no local Supabase       |
    22	| **Schema mgmt**        | Supabase CLI migrations (SQL-first, `db push` to cloud) |
    23	| **Frontend**           | Next.js 14+ on Vercel (frontend hosting ONLY)           |
    24	| **UI System**          | shadcn/ui + Radix primitives + Tailwind CSS             |
    25	| **Data ingestion**     | pg_cron + `http` extension (inside Postgres, $0 cost)   |
    26	| **DB client (TS)**     | Supabase JS client (reads only)                         |
    27	| **DB client (Python)** | psycopg2 to cloud Supabase Postgres; local PostgreSQL only for AG training source |
    28	| **ML**                 | AutoGluon (CPU-only), custom specialist models          |
    29	| **Auth**               | Supabase Auth                                           |
    30	| **API secrets**        | Supabase Vault (accessed via `current_setting()`)       |
    31	| **Package mgr**        | npm (frontend), uv (Python)                             |
    32	| **Env mgmt**           | Vercel <> Supabase integration, `vercel env pull`       |
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	## Hard Rules
    37	
    38	### Architecture Rules
    39	
    40	1. **11 specialists — NEVER say 10.** The Big-11: crush, china, fx, fed, tariff, energy, biofuel, palm, volatility, substitutes, trump_effect.
    41	2. **Target = future PRICE LEVEL** (`close.shift(-horizon)`), columns named `target_price_{h}d`. Never returns.
    42	3. **Target Zones = horizontal lines** at price levels. NEVER say: cones, bands, funnels, confidence intervals.
    43	4. **Probability language:** "ZL has an X% chance of hitting XX.XX by [date]" — derived from Monte Carlo (10k runs) + pinball loss + MAE/accuracy %.
    44	5. **No Inngest. No Vercel Cron.** All scheduling via Supabase pg_cron + `http` extension. Vercel is frontend hosting ONLY.
    45	6. **9 schemas:** mkt, econ, alt, supply, training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas. No others.
    46	7. **ProFarmer is mandatory** ($500/month). Rebuilt as Python Playwright scraper, not Node.js Puppeteer.
    47	8. **Training gate:** NEVER start model training without explicit user approval.
    48	9. **Chart is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — zero modifications to behavior. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    49	10. **Landing page is sacred.** REWRITE from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — preserve the design identity. NEVER copy legacy baseline code.
    50	11. **ZERO mock data.** No placeholders, no temps, no demo/synthetic/random data anywhere, ever. Empty state until real data flows. This is the HARDEST rule.
    51	12. **ZERO code copying.** Every line of V16 is written fresh. legacy baseline is a visual reference only. Clone-and-clean failed catastrophically — never again.
    52	13. **Cloud Supabase only.** Cloud Supabase is canonical. Supabase CLI is for cloud migrations (`db push`) only. No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. Local PostgreSQL is permitted only as the AG training-source staging database, never as canonical warehouse or local Supabase replacement.
    53	14. **No hardcoded port 3000.** Dev server port must be checked for availability first.
    54	15. **Design holdoff exception (locked 2026-05-07).** For page parity work, do not redesign. Reproduce locked source visuals exactly.
    55	16. **Locked page authority map (2026-05-07):** Strategy=V16, Vegas Intel=V16, Dashboard=V15, Legislation=V15, Sentiment=V15.
    56	17. **Global layout lock:** Full-width geometry is mandatory on ALL V16 pages. Do not keep narrow/containerized widths.
    57	18. **Pixel parity lock:** Typography, spacing, shadows, border/radius, gradients, hex colors, and all interaction states must match locked source exactly.
    58	19. **Cadence pivot lock:** Most non-price data moves to weekly batch ingest and weekly retraining; hourly chart freshness remains.
    59	20. **GPT content lock:** Sentiment and Legislation pipelines are GPT-driven from approved source feeds with traceable provenance.
    60	21. **AI-first cards lock (2026-05-08):** Dashboard/strategy/sentiment/legislation/vegas cards are primarily AI snapshot driven; avoid request-time external data pulls in API handlers.
    61	22. **Weekly pull lock (2026-05-08):** Non-price card refresh jobs run weekly cadence by default; AG training runs by manual batch trigger only unless explicitly changed.
    62	23. **Source reduction lock (2026-05-08):** Keep only strict high-impact feeds (FOMC/FED, ProFarmer, FRED aggregate series, constrained weather stations, core energy/FX/volatility signals).
    63	24. **Symbol budget lock (2026-05-08):** Maintain a compact universe (target ~20-30 symbols); do not reopen 50+ symbol spread without explicit approval.
    64	
    65	### Process Rules
    66	
    67	1. **Read the migration plan before touching code.** It defines every table, route, job, and phase.
    68	2. **Follow the phase order.** Phase 0 before Phase 1. Phase 1 before Phase 2. No skipping.
    69	3. **Run evaluation gates.** Each phase has specific checks. Don't declare done until they pass.
    70	4. **One task at a time.** Finish what was asked before touching anything else.
    71	5. **No "while I'm here" refactors.** Stay scoped.
    72	6. **Memory first.** Search memory at session start, store decisions immediately.
    73	7. **Brainstorm before building.** Use the superpowers brainstorming skill for any non-trivial feature.
    74	8. **Verify before claiming done.** Use the superpowers verification skill.
    75	9. **Checkpoint before implementation.** For non-trivial planning work, audit repo reality first, structure the plan as numbered decision checkpoints, run one Ralph Loop per checkpoint, and update docs after each locked decision.
    76	10. **Fail-closed completion.** Read `docs/INDEX.md`, `docs/MASTER_PLAN.md`, and `docs/agent-safety-gates.md` at startup. If any required check is `FAIL`, `NOT RUN`, warning-only, or aborted, report `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    77	11. **Source and generated artifacts are separate lanes.** Source/config/tooling changes require generated `quality/` artifacts to be regenerated and verified before quality can be claimed current.
    78	12. **Docs/contracts update in the same change.** Any behavior, config, schema, gate, or operational-truth change must update the relevant docs/contracts in the same change.
    79	
    80	### Security Rules
    81	
    82	1. No `service_role` key exposed to browser — ever.
    83	2. `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` vars contain only anon key and URL.
    84	3. No manual `.env` copying. Use `vercel env pull` exclusively.
    85	4. ProFarmer credentials stay local — never deployed to Vercel.
    86	5. RLS enabled on every table from day one.
    87	6. API keys for data ingestion stored in Supabase Vault — not env vars, not hardcoded.

   123	
   124	## Key Supabase Patterns
   125	
   126	### Connection Strategy
   127	
   128	```
   129	Frontend (reads):      Supabase JS client with anon key + JWT
   130	Data ingestion:        pg_cron + http extension (runs inside Postgres — no external connection)
   131	Python (reads):        psycopg2 pooled connection to cloud (port 6543)
   132	Python (training src): local PostgreSQL staging DB for AG training only
   133	Python (promotes):     psycopg2 direct connection to cloud (port 5432) — validated outputs only
   134	Python (intermediates): local parquet files — never written to any database
   135	```
   136	
   137	### Migration Pattern
   138	
   139	```bash
   140	# Create a new migration
   141	supabase migration new <name>
   142	
   143	# Push directly to cloud (no local Supabase needed)
   144	supabase db push
   145	
   146	# Diff against cloud
   147	supabase db diff --linked
   148	```
   149	
   150	Never do manual DDL on cloud. Migrations are the single source of truth. No `supabase status`, no `supabase start`, and no local Supabase workflow.
   151	
   152	### Data Ingestion Pattern (pg_cron + http extension)
   153	
   154	All data ingestion runs inside Postgres as plpgsql functions:
   155	
   156	1. pg_cron triggers the function on schedule
   157	2. `http_get()` fetches from external API (synchronous, in-transaction)
   158	3. Parse JSON response in plpgsql
   159	4. UPSERT to target table
   160	5. Log to `ops.ingest_run`
   161	6. API keys from Supabase Vault via `current_setting()`
   162	
   163	No Vercel cron routes. No CRON_SECRET. No serverless functions for ingestion.
   164	
   165	### RLS Pattern
   166	
   167	```sql
   168	-- Enable RLS
   169	ALTER TABLE schema.table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   170	
   171	-- Authenticated users can read
   172	CREATE POLICY "authenticated_read" ON schema.table
   173	  FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
   174	
   175	-- Only service_role can write
   176	CREATE POLICY "service_role_write" ON schema.table
   177	  FOR ALL TO service_role USING (true);
   178	```
   179	
   180	---
   181	
   182	## Tips From Legacy Baseline Experience
   183	
   184	These are hard-won lessons. Don't repeat them.
   185	
   186	### Data Source Gotchas
   187	
   188	- **FRED daily** only fetches `limit=5` (latest obs). Full history needs `refresh_fred_api.py`.
   189	- **EIA API** has been intermittently down since Mar 2026. Build with graceful fallback.
   190	- **MPOB Palm** needs a valid FAS OpenData API key. The legacy baseline key was wrong (FoodData Central, not FAS).
   191	- **Yahoo Finance v8** downsamples to monthly for large date ranges. Use 1-year windows with `period1/period2`.
   192	- **FAS site** (fas.usda.gov) returns HTTP/2 stream errors. Needs retry logic.
   193	- **UCO/Tallow** prices: no free direct API. Use FRED PPI proxies: `WPU06410132` (Tallow PPI) + `PCU3116133116132` (Rendering PPI).
   194	
   195	### AutoGluon Gotchas
   196	
   197	- `TimeSeriesPredictor.load(path, require_version_match=False)` — version check bug fires even when versions match.
   198	- All covariates are OBSERVED, not KNOWN. This limits Chronos2 effectiveness.
   199	- CPU-only on macOS ARM. Deep learning models (DeepAR, TFT, etc.) are disabled.
   200	- Training the 19-model zoo takes significant time. Always `--dry-run` first.
   201	
   202	### Frontend Gotchas
   203	
   204	- The chart uses `lightweight-charts` (TradingView). Settings are precise — don't modify them.
   205	- `ForecastTargetsPrimitive` is a custom series primitive for drawing horizontal Target Zone lines.
   206	- Pivot labels use format `D(P)`, `D(R1)`, `D(S1)`, `W(P)`, `M(P)`, `Y(P)`.
   207	- The NeuralSphere on the landing page uses Three.js + `head.glb`. Resource-heavy but intentional.
   208	
   209	### Supabase-Specific Tips
   210	
   211	- Use `supabase db diff` to check for schema drift between local and cloud.
   212	- Connection pooler (port 6543) has a statement timeout. Use direct (5432) for long writes.
   213	- `pg_cron` jobs run as the `postgres` role. Make sure your functions/procedures are owned by `postgres`.
   214	- Supabase Auth JWTs expire after 1 hour by default. Configure refresh behavior in the client.
   215	
   216	---
   217	
   218	## Vegas Intel — What Makes It Special
   219	
   220	This page is Kevin's primary sales tool. Key features that must survive:
   221	
   222	1. **Events calendar** — CES, SEMA, March Madness, conventions. Links events to oil demand spikes.
   223	2. **Intel buttons** — AI-powered recommendations for each restaurant account.
   224	3. **AI sales strategy** — Generates personalized pitches using real customer data + real oil consumption volumes.
   225	4. **Customer matching** — API pulls real customer records, matches with event impact predictions.
   226	5. **Fryer tracking** — Equipment lifecycle drives service scheduling recommendations.
   227	
   228	This is not a generic dashboard page. It's a sales intelligence tool that directly drives revenue.

   252	3. **Check what phase you're in** — Don't work on Phase 5 stuff if Phase 2 isn't done.

   254	
   255	```
   256	Memory(search) -> Plan -> Execute -> Memory(store) -> Report
   257	```

   263	## Definition of Done (V16)
   264	
   265	V16 is complete when:
   266	
   267	- The chart renders correctly with real ZL data from Supabase
   268	- Target Zones render correctly (P30/P50/P70 horizontal lines)
   269	- The landing page matches legacy baseline's premium design identity (rewritten, not copied)
   270	- All 6 pages are operational with real data
   271	- Only validated routes and jobs exist (no legacy baggage)
   272	- Supabase owns the clean canonical database with RLS enforced — cloud only; local PostgreSQL is AG training-source staging only
   273	- Vercel is frontend hosting ONLY — zero crons, zero ingestion compute
   274	- ~22 pg_cron + http functions keep data fresh inside Supabase ($0 incremental)
   275	- Python pipeline runs end-to-end: reads cloud → local PostgreSQL training source/local files → promotes validated outputs to cloud
   276	- ProFarmer Playwright scraper is working ($500/mo source, 7 sections, 35 runs/week)

   278	- Zero mock data anywhere in the codebase
   279	- legacy baseline can be turned off without losing functionality

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/agent-safety-gates.md | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Agent Safety Gates
     2	
     3	## Status Vocabulary
     4	
     5	Use only these closure states:
     6	
     7	| State | Meaning |
     8	| --- | --- |
     9	| `PASS` | The required command/check ran in this workspace and exited cleanly with no warning state. |
    10	| `FAIL` | The required command/check ran and failed, timed out, or reported an unsafe state. |
    11	| `NOT RUN` | The required check did not run because the runner, environment, credentials, or prerequisite was unavailable. |
    12	| `INCOMPLETE` | Final task status when any required check is `FAIL` or `NOT RUN`. |
    13	
    14	Do not use "complete" for work with `FAIL`, `NOT RUN`, warning-only gates, or
    15	aborted generated-quality finalizers.
    16	
    17	## Required Completion Gate
    18	
    19	`STATUS: PASS` requires all of the following in the same verification window:
    20	
    21	1. `npm run lint` passes.
    22	2. `npm run build` passes.
    23	3. Python test runner availability is proven and required tests run.
    24	4. Quality Playbook doctor and smoke checks pass.
    25	5. `quality/mechanical/verify.sh` passes when quality artifacts exist.
    26	6. Installed Quality Playbook `quality_gate.py` passes with zero warnings.
    27	7. Quality Playbook finalizer is not aborted.
    28	8. Source edits and generated `quality/` artifacts are in sync.
    29	9. Behavior, config, schema, gate, and operational-truth changes update docs or
    30	   contracts in the same change.
    31	
    32	If any item is missing, failed, timed out, unavailable, or warning-only, report:
    33	
    34	`STATUS: INCOMPLETE`
    35	
    36	## Source Lane vs Generated Lane
    37	
    38	Treat these as separate lanes:
    39	
    40	- Source lane: `app/`, `components/`, `lib/`, `python/`, `scripts/`,
    41	  `supabase/`, package/config files, and authority docs.
    42	- Generated lane: `quality/` artifacts and generated quality receipts.
    43	
    44	Rules:
    45	
    46	1. Source edits can invalidate generated quality artifacts.
    47	2. Generated artifacts do not prove source quality unless they were regenerated
    48	   after the source edits they describe.
    49	3. Do not manually clean generated artifacts to make string scans pass. Regenerate
    50	   artifacts or mark the quality lane stale.
    51	4. Guard audit logs are written under `logs/fusion-guard/` and are local runtime
    52	   evidence, not source-of-truth docs.
    53	
    54	## Guard Commands
    55	
    56	Use these commands:
    57	
    58	```bash
    59	npm run guard:pre-commit
    60	npm run guard:pre-push
    61	npm run guard:completion
    62	```
    63	
    64	`guard:pre-commit` checks staged file scope and contract synchronization.
    65	`guard:pre-push` checks the upstream range and full gates.
    66	`guard:completion` checks the current workspace and full gates.
    67	
    68	The guard scripts are intentionally fail-closed. A nonzero guard exit means the
    69	status is incomplete until the blocker is resolved and the guard is rerun.

    10	6. `docs/runbooks/qplaybook.md` — Quality Playbook install and run procedure.
    11	7. `docs/contracts/` — executable API, data, security, and quality contracts.
    12	8. `docs/decisions/` and `docs/ops/` — checkpoint evidence and dated decisions.
    13

    15	generated `quality/` artifacts as fresher than source edits unless the guard

     1	# ZINC Fusion V16 Master Plan
     2	
     3	## Canonical Plan
     4	
     5	The canonical build plan remains:
     6	
     7	`docs/plans/2026-03-17-v16-migration-plan.md`
     8	
     9	This file is the operational router. It does not replace the migration plan.
    10	
    11	## Current Completion Rule
    12	
    13	Completion is fail-closed:
    14	
    15	- Any failed required check means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    16	- Any required check recorded as `NOT RUN` means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    17	- Any aborted generated-quality finalizer means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    18	- Any warning in a required quality gate means `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    19	- A standalone passing sub-gate does not override an incomplete full gate.
    20	
    21	## Current Quality State
    22	
    23	As of 2026-05-16, Quality Playbook artifacts exist and phases 1-6 were run.
    24	The run must still be treated as incomplete until the full completion guard
    25	passes with build, tests, quality gate, mechanical verification, source edit
    26	state, and generated artifact freshness all clean in the same run.
    27	
    28	## Required Work Loop
    29	
    30	1. Read authority docs from `docs/INDEX.md`.
    31	2. Inspect `git status --short`.
    32	3. Identify the active migration phase and gate from the canonical plan.
    33	4. Declare source lane and generated-artifact lane separately.
    34	5. Make the smallest scoped change.
    35	6. Update docs/contracts in the same change when behavior, config, schema, gate,
    36	   or operational truth changes.
    37	7. Regenerate quality artifacts after source/config/tooling changes that affect
    38	   quality evidence.
    39	8. Run the relevant guard:
    40	   - `npm run guard:pre-commit`
    41	   - `npm run guard:pre-push`
    42	   - `npm run guard:completion`
    43	9. Report `PASS`, `FAIL`, or `NOT RUN` per check. If any check is not `PASS`,
    44	   report `STATUS: INCOMPLETE`.
    45	
    46	## Non-Negotiable Boundaries
    47	
    48	- Do not train models without explicit user approval.
    49	- Do not run Supabase migrations or `db push` without explicit approval.
    50	- Do not start local Supabase.
    51	- Do not use synthetic, placeholder, demo, or guessed data.
    52	- Do not copy legacy baseline code into V16.

     1	<a href="https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/">
     2	  <img alt="Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit - the fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase" src="https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/opengraph-image.png">
     3	  <h1 align="center">Next.js and Supabase Starter Kit</h1>
     4	</a>
     5	
     6	<p align="center">
     7	 The fastest way to build apps with Next.js and Supabase
     8	</p>
     9	
    10	<p align="center">
    11	  <a href="#features"><strong>Features</strong></a> ·
    12	  <a href="#demo"><strong>Demo</strong></a> ·
    13	  <a href="#deploy-to-vercel"><strong>Deploy to Vercel</strong></a> ·
    14	  <a href="#clone-and-run-locally"><strong>Clone and run locally</strong></a> ·
    15	  <a href="#feedback-and-issues"><strong>Feedback and issues</strong></a>
    16	  <a href="#more-supabase-examples"><strong>More Examples</strong></a>
    17	</p>
    18	<br/>
    19	
    20	## Features
    21	
    22	- Works across the entire [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) stack
    23	  - App Router
    24	  - Pages Router
    25	  - Proxy
    26	  - Client
    27	  - Server
    28	  - It just works!
    29	- supabase-ssr. A package to configure Supabase Auth to use cookies
    30	- Password-based authentication block installed via the [Supabase UI Library](https://supabase.com/ui/docs/nextjs/password-based-auth)
    31	- Styling with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com)
    32	- Components with [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/)
    33	- Optional deployment with [Supabase Vercel Integration and Vercel deploy](#deploy-your-own)
    34	  - Environment variables automatically assigned to Vercel project
    35	
    36	## Demo
    37	
    38	You can view a fully working demo at [demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app](https://demo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app/).
    39	
    40	## Deploy to Vercel
    41	
    42	Vercel deployment will guide you through creating a Supabase account and project.
    43	
    44	After installation of the Supabase integration, all relevant environment variables will be assigned to the project so the deployment is fully functioning.
    45	
    46	[![Deploy with Vercel](https://vercel.com/button)](https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&project-name=nextjs-with-supabase&repository-name=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-title=nextjs-with-supabase&demo-description=This+starter+configures+Supabase+Auth+to+use+cookies%2C+making+the+user%27s+session+available+throughout+the+entire+Next.js+app+-+Client+Components%2C+Server+Components%2C+Route+Handlers%2C+Server+Actions+and+Middleware.&demo-url=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2F&external-id=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fvercel%2Fnext.js%2Ftree%2Fcanary%2Fexamples%2Fwith-supabase&demo-image=https%3A%2F%2Fdemo-nextjs-with-supabase.vercel.app%2Fopengraph-image.png)
    47	
    48	The above will also clone the Starter kit to your GitHub, you can clone that locally and develop locally.
    49	
    50	If you wish to just develop locally and not deploy to Vercel, [follow the steps below](#clone-and-run-locally).
    51	
    52	## Clone and run locally
    53	
    54	1. You'll first need a Supabase project which can be made [via the Supabase dashboard](https://database.new)
    55	
    56	2. Create a Next.js app using the Supabase Starter template npx command
    57	
    58	   ```bash
    59	   npx create-next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    60	   ```
    61	
    62	   ```bash
    63	   yarn create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    64	   ```
    65	
    66	   ```bash
    67	   pnpm create next-app --example with-supabase with-supabase-app
    68	   ```
    69	
    70	3. Use `cd` to change into the app's directory
    71	
    72	   ```bash
    73	   cd with-supabase-app
    74	   ```
    75	
    76	4. Rename `.env.example` to `.env.local` and update the following:
    77	
    78	  ```env
    79	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT URL]
    80	  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=[INSERT SUPABASE PROJECT API PUBLISHABLE OR ANON KEY]
    81	  ```
    82	  > [!NOTE]
    83	  > This example uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, which refers to Supabase's new **publishable** key format.
    84	  > Both legacy **anon** keys and new **publishable** keys can be used with this variable name during the transition period. Supabase's dashboard may show `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`; its value can be used in this example.
    85	  > See the [full announcement](https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/29260) for more information.
    86	
    87	  Both `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` can be found in [your Supabase project's API settings](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/_?showConnect=true)
    88	
    89	5. You can now run the Next.js local development server:
    90	
    91	   ```bash
    92	   npm run dev
    93	   ```
    94	
    95	   The starter kit should now be running on [localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/).
    96	
    97	6. This template comes with the default shadcn/ui style initialized. If you instead want other ui.shadcn styles, delete `components.json` and [re-install shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/installation/next)
    98	
    99	> Check out [the docs for Local Development](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/local-development) to also run Supabase locally.
   100	
   101	## Feedback and issues
   102	
   103	Please file feedback and issues over on the [Supabase GitHub org](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/new/choose).
   104

   106	
   107	- [Next.js Subscription Payments Starter](https://github.com/vercel/nextjs-subscription-payments)

   109	- [Supabase Auth and the Next.js App Router](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/tree/master/examples/auth/nextjs)

     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     4	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, ZlLivePrice } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	export async function GET() {
     8	  try {
     9	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    10	    if (unauthorized) {
    11	      return unauthorized;
    12	    }
    13	
    14	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    15	
    16	    const { data: row, error } = await supabase
    17	      .schema("mkt")
    18	      .from("latest_price")
    19	      .select("symbol, price, observed_at")
    20	      .eq("symbol", "ZL")
    21	      .maybeSingle();
    22	
    23	    if (error) {
    24	      return NextResponse.json(
    25	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    26	        { status: 500 },
    27	      );
    28	    }
    29	
    30	    const live: ZlLivePrice | null = row
    31	      ? { symbol: row.symbol, price: Number(row.price), observedAt: row.observed_at }
    32	      : null;
    33	
    34	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<ZlLivePrice | null> = {
    35	      ok: true,
    36	      data: live,
    37	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    38	      source: "mkt.latest_price",
    39	    };
    40	
    41	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    42	  } catch (err) {
    43	    return NextResponse.json(
    44	      { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    45	      { status: 500 },
    46	    );
    47	  }
    48	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     4	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, ZlPriceBar } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	type PriceRow = {
     8	  symbol: string;
     9	  bucket_ts: string;
    10	  open: number | string;
    11	  high: number | string;
    12	  low: number | string;
    13	  close: number | string;
    14	  volume: number | string | null;
    15	};
    16	
    17	function dayKey(tsIso: string): string {
    18	  return tsIso.slice(0, 10);
    19	}
    20	
    21	function buildLatestDatabentoDailyFromHourly(rows: PriceRow[]): ZlPriceBar | null {
    22	  if (rows.length === 0) return null;
    23	
    24	  const latestDay = dayKey(rows[0].bucket_ts);
    25	  const sameDayRows = rows.filter((row) => dayKey(row.bucket_ts) === latestDay);
    26	  if (sameDayRows.length === 0) return null;
    27	
    28	  sameDayRows.sort(
    29	    (a, b) => new Date(a.bucket_ts).getTime() - new Date(b.bucket_ts).getTime(),
    30	  );
    31	
    32	  const parsedRows = sameDayRows
    33	    .map((row) => ({
    34	      open: Number(row.open),
    35	      high: Number(row.high),
    36	      low: Number(row.low),
    37	      close: Number(row.close),
    38	      volume: Number(row.volume ?? 0),
    39	    }))
    40	    .filter(
    41	      (row) =>
    42	        Number.isFinite(row.open) &&
    43	        Number.isFinite(row.high) &&
    44	        Number.isFinite(row.low) &&
    45	        Number.isFinite(row.close),
    46	    );
    47	
    48	  if (parsedRows.length === 0) return null;
    49	
    50	  const open = parsedRows[0].open;
    51	  const close = parsedRows[parsedRows.length - 1].close;
    52	  let high = parsedRows[0].high;
    53	  let low = parsedRows[0].low;
    54	  let volume = 0;
    55	
    56	  for (const row of parsedRows) {
    57	    if (row.high > high) high = row.high;
    58	    if (row.low < low) low = row.low;
    59	    if (Number.isFinite(row.volume)) volume += row.volume;
    60	  }
    61	
    62	  return {
    63	    symbol: "ZL",
    64	    tradeDate: `${latestDay}T00:00:00+00:00`,
    65	    open,
    66	    high,
    67	    low,
    68	    close,
    69	    volume,
    70	  };
    71	}
    72	
    73	export async function GET() {
    74	  try {
    75	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    76	    if (unauthorized) {
    77	      return unauthorized;
    78	    }
    79	
    80	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    81	
    82	    // Supabase Data API commonly enforces a max-row cap (default 1000).
    83	    // Pull newest rows first so we always include the current chart window.
    84	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    85	      .schema("mkt")
    86	      .from("price_1d")
    87	      .select("symbol, bucket_ts, open, high, low, close, volume")
    88	      .eq("symbol", "ZL")
    89	      .order("bucket_ts", { ascending: false })
    90	      .limit(1000);
    91	
    92	    if (error) {
    93	      return NextResponse.json(
    94	        { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    95	        { status: 500 },
    96	      );
    97	    }
    98	
    99	    const bars: ZlPriceBar[] = (rows ?? [])
   100	      .map((row) => ({
   101	      symbol: row.symbol,
   102	      tradeDate: row.bucket_ts,
   103	      open: Number(row.open),
   104	      high: Number(row.high),
   105	      low: Number(row.low),
   106	      close: Number(row.close),
   107	      volume: Number(row.volume),
   108	      }))
   109	      .sort(
   110	        (a, b) => new Date(a.tradeDate).getTime() - new Date(b.tradeDate).getTime(),
   111	      );
   112	
   113	    const { data: hourlyRows } = await supabase
   114	      .schema("mkt")
   115	      .from("price_1h")
   116	      .select("symbol, bucket_ts, open, high, low, close, volume")
   117	      .eq("symbol", "ZL")
   118	      .order("bucket_ts", { ascending: false })
   119	      .limit(96);
   120	
   121	    const databentoLatest = buildLatestDatabentoDailyFromHourly(
   122	      (hourlyRows ?? []) as PriceRow[],
   123	    );
   124	
   125	    if (databentoLatest) {
   126	      const lastBar = bars[bars.length - 1] ?? null;
   127	      const lastDay = lastBar ? dayKey(lastBar.tradeDate) : null;
   128	      const databentoDay = dayKey(databentoLatest.tradeDate);
   129	
   130	      if (!lastDay || databentoDay > lastDay) {
   131	        bars.push(databentoLatest);
   132	      } else if (databentoDay === lastDay && lastBar) {
   133	        bars[bars.length - 1] = databentoLatest;
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<ZlPriceBar[]> = {
   138	      ok: true,
   139	      data: bars,
   140	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
   141	      source: databentoLatest
   142	        ? "mkt.price_1d + Databento (mkt.price_1h latest daily bar)"
   143	        : "mkt.price_1d",
   144	    };
   145	
   146	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
   147	  } catch (err) {
   148	    return NextResponse.json(
   149	      { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
   150	      { status: 500 },
   151	    );
   152	  }
   153	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba app/api/zl/forecast/route.ts | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     4	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, ForecastSummary } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	const AG_HORIZON_DAYS = [30, 90, 180] as const;
     8	const LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP: Record<number, (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number]> = {
     9	  7: 30,
    10	  14: 90,
    11	  30: 180,
    12	};
    13	
    14	function normalizeHorizon(row: {
    15	  horizon_days: number;
    16	  model_version: string | null;
    17	}): number | null {
    18	  if (AG_HORIZON_DAYS.includes(row.horizon_days as (typeof AG_HORIZON_DAYS)[number])) {
    19	    return row.horizon_days;
    20	  }
    21	  if (row.model_version?.startsWith("trusted-fill-v1")) {
    22	    return LEGACY_HORIZON_MAP[row.horizon_days] ?? null;
    23	  }
    24	  return null;
    25	}
    26	
    27	export async function GET() {
    28	  try {
    29	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    30	    if (unauthorized) {
    31	      return unauthorized;
    32	    }
    33	
    34	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    35	
    36	    // Get the latest forecast summaries (most recent forecast_date)
    37	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    38	      .schema("forecasts")
    39	      .from("forecast_summary_1d")
    40	      .select("horizon_days, predicted_price, hit_probability, model_version, forecast_date")
    41	      .order("forecast_date", { ascending: false })
    42	      .limit(20);
    43	
    44	    if (error) {
    45	      return NextResponse.json(
    46	        { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    47	        { status: 500 },
    48	      );
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
    52	      const envelope: ApiEnvelope<ForecastSummary[]> = {
    53	        ok: true,
    54	        data: [],
    55	        asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    56	        source: "forecasts.forecast_summary_1d",
    57	      };
    58	      return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    59	    }
    60	
    61	    // Filter to the latest forecast_date only
    62	    const latestDate = rows[0].forecast_date;
    63	    const latestRows = rows.filter((r) => r.forecast_date === latestDate);
    64	
    65	    const deduped = new Map<number, ForecastSummary>();
    66	    for (const row of latestRows) {
    67	      const horizonDays = normalizeHorizon(row);
    68	      if (!horizonDays) continue;
    69	      if (deduped.has(horizonDays)) continue;
    70	      deduped.set(horizonDays, {
    71	        horizonDays,
    72	        predictedPrice: Number(row.predicted_price),
    73	        hitProbability: Number(row.hit_probability ?? 0),
    74	        modelVersion: row.model_version,
    75	      });
    76	    }
    77	    const forecasts = [...deduped.values()].sort((a, b) => a.horizonDays - b.horizonDays);
    78	
    79	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<ForecastSummary[]> = {
    80	      ok: true,
    81	      data: forecasts,
    82	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    83	      source: "forecasts.forecast_summary_1d",
    84	    };
    85	
    86	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    87	  } catch (err) {
    88	    return NextResponse.json(
    89	      { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    90	      { status: 500 },
    91	    );
    92	  }
    93	}

     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     4	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, DriverAttribution } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	export async function GET() {
     8	  try {
     9	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    10	    if (unauthorized) {
    11	      return unauthorized;
    12	    }
    13	
    14	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    15	
    16	    // Get the latest driver attribution (most recent trade_date, top 4 by rank)
    17	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    18	      .schema("analytics")
    19	      .from("driver_attribution_1d")
    20	      .select("factor, contribution, confidence, trade_date, rank")
    21	      .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
    22	      .order("rank", { ascending: true })
    23	      .limit(20);
    24	
    25	    if (error) {
    26	      return NextResponse.json(
    27	        { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    28	        { status: 500 },
    29	      );
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
    33	      const envelope: ApiEnvelope<DriverAttribution[]> = {
    34	        ok: true,
    35	        data: [],
    36	        asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    37	        source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
    38	      };
    39	      return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    40	    }
    41	
    42	    // Filter to latest trade_date only
    43	    const latestDate = rows[0].trade_date;
    44	    const latestRows = rows.filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestDate);
    45	
    46	    const drivers: DriverAttribution[] = latestRows.map((row) => ({
    47	      factor: row.factor,
    48	      contribution: Number(row.contribution),
    49	      confidence: Number(row.confidence),
    50	    }));
    51	
    52	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<DriverAttribution[]> = {
    53	      ok: true,
    54	      data: drivers,
    55	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    56	      source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
    57	    };
    58	
    59	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    60	  } catch (err) {
    61	    return NextResponse.json(
    62	      { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    63	      { status: 500 },
    64	    );
    65	  }
    66	}

     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     4	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	type DashboardMetric = {
     8	  key: string;
     9	  label: string;
    10	  value: number;
    11	};
    12	
    13	export async function GET() {
    14	  try {
    15	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    16	    if (unauthorized) {
    17	      return unauthorized;
    18	    }
    19	
    20	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    21	
    22	    // Get the latest dashboard metrics
    23	    const { data: rows, error } = await supabase
    24	      .schema("analytics")
    25	      .from("dashboard_metrics")
    26	      .select("metric_key, metric_value, trade_date")
    27	      .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
    28	      .limit(20);
    29	
    30	    if (error) {
    31	      return NextResponse.json(
    32	        { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    33	        { status: 500 },
    34	      );
    35	    }
    36	
    37	    if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
    38	      const envelope: ApiEnvelope<DashboardMetric[]> = {
    39	        ok: true,
    40	        data: [],
    41	        asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    42	        source: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
    43	      };
    44	      return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    // Filter to latest trade_date
    48	    const latestDate = rows[0].trade_date;
    49	    const latestRows = rows.filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestDate);
    50	
    51	    const metrics: DashboardMetric[] = latestRows.map((row) => ({
    52	      key: row.metric_key,
    53	      label: row.metric_key.replace(/_/g, " "),
    54	      value: Number(row.metric_value),
    55	    }));
    56	
    57	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<DashboardMetric[]> = {
    58	      ok: true,
    59	      data: metrics,
    60	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    61	      source: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
    62	    };
    63	
    64	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    65	  } catch (err) {
    66	    return NextResponse.json(
    67	      { ok: false, data: [], asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    68	      { status: 500 },
    69	    );
    70	  }
    71	}

     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { createSupabaseAdminClient } from "@/lib/server/supabase-admin";
     4	import { requireAuthenticatedApiRequest } from "@/lib/server/auth-guards";
     5	import type { ApiEnvelope, RegimeState } from "@/lib/contracts/api";
     6	
     7	export async function GET() {
     8	  try {
     9	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
    10	    if (unauthorized) {
    11	      return unauthorized;
    12	    }
    13	
    14	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
    15	
    16	    // Get the latest regime state
    17	    const { data: row, error } = await supabase
    18	      .schema("analytics")
    19	      .from("regime_state_1d")
    20	      .select("regime, confidence, trade_date")
    21	      .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
    22	      .limit(1)
    23	      .maybeSingle();
    24	
    25	    if (error) {
    26	      return NextResponse.json(
    27	        { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: error.message },
    28	        { status: 500 },
    29	      );
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    const regime: RegimeState | null = row
    33	      ? {
    34	          regime: row.regime,
    35	          confidence: Number(row.confidence),
    36	          updatedAt: row.trade_date,
    37	        }
    38	      : null;
    39	
    40	    const envelope: ApiEnvelope<RegimeState | null> = {
    41	      ok: true,
    42	      data: regime,
    43	      asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
    44	      source: "analytics.regime_state_1d",
    45	    };
    46	
    47	    return NextResponse.json(envelope);
    48	  } catch (err) {
    49	    return NextResponse.json(
    50	      { ok: false, data: null, asOf: new Date().toISOString(), error: String(err) },
    51	      { status: 500 },
    52	    );
    53	  }

    40	interface MarketDriversResponse {
    41	  as_of_date: string | null
    42	  as_of_date_min?: string
    43	  as_of_date_max?: string
    44	  mixed_vintage?: boolean
    45	  drivers: {
    46	    vix_stress: DriverData
    47	    crush_pressure: DriverData
    48	    china_tension: DriverData
    49	    tariff_threat: DriverData
    50	    energy_stress?: DriverData
    51	  }
    52	  summary: {
    53	    average_pressure: number
    54	    highest_pressure: { name: string; score: number }
    55	    alert_count: number
    56	  }

    59	
    60	function formatFreshnessLabel(
    61	  payload: Pick<MarketDriversResponse, "as_of_date" | "as_of_date_min" | "as_of_date_max" | "mixed_vintage"> | null | undefined,
    62	): string | null {
    63	  if (!payload) return null
    64	  if (payload.mixed_vintage && payload.as_of_date_min && payload.as_of_date_max) {
    65	    return `Mixed vintage ${payload.as_of_date_min} to ${payload.as_of_date_max}`
    66	  }
    67	  return payload.as_of_date ? `Data as of ${payload.as_of_date}` : null
    68	}
    69	
    70	function formatFreshnessSummary(
    71	  payload: Pick<MarketDriversResponse, "as_of_date" | "as_of_date_min" | "as_of_date_max" | "mixed_vintage"> | null | undefined,
    72	): string {
    73	  if (!payload) return "–"
    74	  if (payload.mixed_vintage && payload.as_of_date_min && payload.as_of_date_max) {
    75	    return `${payload.as_of_date_min} to ${payload.as_of_date_max}`
    76	  }
    77	  return payload.as_of_date ?? "–"
    78	}

   114	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115	// Score → color
   116	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   117	
   118	function getScoreColor(score: number): { stroke: string; glow: string } {
   119	  const s = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, score))
   120	  if (s <= 25) return { stroke: "#22C55E", glow: "rgba(34,197,94,0.5)" }
   121	  if (s <= 40) {
   122	    const t = (s - 25) / 15
   123	    return {
   124	      stroke: `rgb(${Math.round(34 + (234 - 34) * t)}, ${Math.round(197 - (197 - 179) * t)}, ${Math.round(94 - (94 - 8) * t)})`,
   125	      glow: `rgba(${Math.round(34 + (234 - 34) * t)}, ${Math.round(197 - (197 - 179) * t)}, ${Math.round(94 - (94 - 8) * t)}, 0.5)`,
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	  if (s <= 55) return { stroke: "#EAB308", glow: "rgba(234,179,8,0.5)" }
   129	  if (s <= 70) {
   130	    const t = (s - 55) / 15
   131	    return {
   132	      stroke: `rgb(${Math.round(234 + (239 - 234) * t)}, ${Math.round(179 - (179 - 115) * t)}, ${Math.round(8 + (0 - 8) * t)})`,
   133	      glow: `rgba(${Math.round(234 + (239 - 234) * t)}, ${Math.round(179 - (179 - 115) * t)}, ${Math.round(8 + (0 - 8) * t)}, 0.5)`,
   134	    }
   135	  }
   136	  if (s <= 85) return { stroke: "#EF7300", glow: "rgba(239,115,0,0.5)" }
   137	  return { stroke: "#EF4444", glow: "rgba(239,68,68,0.6)" }
   138	}
   139	
   140	function getScoreTextColor(score: number | null): string {
   141	  if (score === null) return "text-slate-500"
   142	  if (score >= 70) return "text-red-400"
   143	  if (score >= 55) return "text-orange-400"
   144	  if (score >= 40) return "text-amber-400"
   145	  return "text-green-400"
   146	}
   147	
   148	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149	// Horizontal Meter
   150	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   151	
   152	function HorizontalMeter({ score }: { score: number | null }) {
   153	  if (score === null) {
   154	    return (
   155	      <div className="flex items-center gap-4 w-full">
   156	        <div className="flex-1 h-3 bg-slate-800 rounded-full overflow-hidden" />
   157	        <span className="text-3xl font-bold tabular-nums min-w-[3ch] text-right text-slate-500">
   158	          --
   159	        </span>
   160	      </div>
   161	    )
   162	  }
   163	
   164	  const pct = Math.min(Math.max(score, 0), 100)
   165	  const colors = getScoreColor(score)
   166	  return (
   167	    <div className="flex items-center gap-4 w-full">
   168	      <div className="flex-1 h-3 bg-slate-800 rounded-full overflow-hidden">
   169	        <div
   170	          className="h-full rounded-full transition-all duration-700 ease-out"
   171	          style={{ width: `${pct}%`, backgroundColor: colors.stroke, boxShadow: `0 0 8px ${colors.glow}` }}
   172	        />
   173	      </div>
   174	      <span className="text-3xl font-bold tabular-nums min-w-[3ch] text-right" style={{ color: colors.stroke }}>
   175	        {Math.round(score)}
   176	      </span>
   177	    </div>
   178	  )
   179	}

   181	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   182	// Driver Card
   183	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   184	
   185	interface MetricDef {
   186	  key: string
   187	  label: string
   188	  format: (v: number | null) => string
   189	}
   190	
   191	function DriverCard({
   192	  label,
   193	  data,
   194	  metrics,
   195	  loading,
   196	}: {
   197	  label: string
   198	  data: DriverData | null
   199	  metrics: MetricDef[]
   200	  loading: boolean
   201	}) {
   202	  const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false)
   203	  const score = data?.score ?? null
   204	  const level = data?.level ? (LEVEL_LABELS[data.level] ?? data.level) : "--"
   205	  const colors = getScoreColor(score ?? 0)
   206	  const wh = data?.whatsHappening
   207	
   208	  const border =
   209	    score !== null && score >= 65
   210	      ? { borderColor: colors.stroke, boxShadow: `0 0 20px ${colors.glow}` }
   211	      : { borderColor: "rgba(255,255,255,0.08)" }
   212	
   213	  return (
   214	    <div
   215	      className="bg-[#0a0a0a] border rounded-2xl p-6 md:p-8 flex flex-col hover:border-white/20 transition-all duration-300"
   216	      style={border}
   217	    >
   218	      <div className="text-base font-bold text-slate-300 uppercase tracking-wider mb-4 flex items-center gap-2">
   219	        {DRIVER_NAMES[label] ?? label}
   220	        {data?.aiPowered && (
   221	          <span className="px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded text-xs bg-cyan-500/15 text-cyan-300 border border-cyan-500/20">AI</span>
   222	        )}
   223	      </div>
   224	
   225	      <div className="w-full mb-2">
   226	        {loading ? (
   227	          <div className="h-3 bg-slate-700/50 rounded-full animate-pulse" />
   228	        ) : (
   229	          <HorizontalMeter score={score} />
   230	        )}
   231	      </div>
   232	
   233	      <div className={`text-lg font-medium mt-1 mb-4 ${loading ? "text-slate-600" : getScoreTextColor(score)}`}>
   234	        {loading ? "..." : level}
   235	      </div>
   236	
   237	      <div className="w-full space-y-2 border-t border-white/5 pt-4">
   238	        {metrics.map((m) => {
   239	          const val = data?.components?.[m.key] ?? null
   240	          return (
   241	            <div key={m.key} className="flex justify-between items-center text-sm">
   242	              <span className="text-slate-400">{m.label}</span>
   243	              <span className="text-slate-200 font-mono">{loading ? "--" : m.format(val)}</span>
   244	            </div>
   245	          )
   246	        })}
   247	      </div>
   248	
   249	      <div className="mt-4 text-sm text-slate-300 leading-relaxed min-h-[40px]">
   250	        {loading ? "..." : (data?.headline ?? "--")}
   251	      </div>
   252	
   253	      {!loading && data?.dataDate && (
   254	        <div className="mt-2 text-xs text-slate-500 font-mono">Data as of: {data.dataDate}</div>
   255	      )}
   256	
   257	      {wh && !loading && (
   258	        <button
   259	          onClick={() => setExpanded(!expanded)}
   260	          className="mt-4 w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium bg-slate-800/80 hover:bg-slate-700/80 text-slate-400 hover:text-slate-200 transition-all flex items-center justify-center gap-2 border border-slate-700/50"
