   366	
   367	function summarizeDrivers(drivers: Record<DriverKey, DriverData>): {
   368	  average: number;
   369	  topName: string;
   370	  topScore: number;
   371	  alertCount: number;
   372	} {
   373	  const scoredEntries = (Object.values(drivers) as DriverData[]).filter(
   374	    (d) => d.score !== null,
   375	  ) as Array<DriverData & { score: number }>;
   376	
   377	  const average =
   378	    scoredEntries.length > 0
   379	      ? Math.round(
   380	          (scoredEntries.reduce((acc, d) => acc + d.score, 0) / scoredEntries.length) * 10,
   381	        ) / 10
   382	      : 0;
   383	
   384	  const highest =
   385	    scoredEntries.length > 0
   386	      ? scoredEntries.slice().sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)[0]
   387	      : null;
   388	
   389	  const topScore = highest?.score ?? 0;
   390	  const topName = highest?.name ?? "No Data";
   391	  const alertCount = scoredEntries.filter((d) => d.score >= 65).length;
   392	
   393	  return { average, topName, topScore, alertCount };
   394	}
   395	
   396	async function readAiRiskFactorsSnapshot(): Promise<AiRiskFactorsSnapshot | null> {
   397	  const snapshot = await readAiSnapshot<AiRiskFactorsSnapshot>("app/config/dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json");
   398	  if (!snapshot) return null;
   399	  if (!snapshot.drivers || !snapshot.intelligence) return null;
   400	  return snapshot;
   401	}
   402	
   403	function driverFocus(driver: DriverKey): string {
   404	  if (driver === "vix_stress") return "volatility transmission into procurement timing";
   405	  if (driver === "crush_pressure") return "processor margin pressure and oil-share economics";
   406	  if (driver === "china_tension") return "China demand and trade-flow displacement";
   407	  if (driver === "tariff_threat") return "policy shocks and geopolitical tariff channels";
   408	  return "energy complex pass-through into ZL";
   409	}
   410	
   411	function buildDriverProvenance(
   412	  key: DriverKey,
   413	  dataDate: string | null,
   414	  generatedAt: string | null,
   415	): AiCardProvenance {
   416	  const asOf = dataDate ?? generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   417	  return {
   418	    asOf,
   419	    generatedAt: generatedAt ?? asOf,
   420	    method: "verified-db-weekly-ingest-plus-ai-snapshot",
   421	    sourceFeeds: [
   422	      "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   423	      "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   424	    ],
   425	    sourceRecords: [
   426	      {
   427	        source: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   428	        table: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   429	        recordHint: `driver=${key}`,
   430	        observedAt: dataDate ?? undefined,
   431	      },
   432	      {
   433	        source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   434	        table: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   435	        recordHint: `mapped_driver=${key}`,
   436	        observedAt: dataDate ?? undefined,
   437	      },
   438	    ],
   439	  };
   440	}
   441	
   442	function buildIntelligenceProvenance(
   443	  asOf: string | null,
   444	  generatedAt: string | null,
   445	): AiCardProvenance {
   446	  const resolvedAsOf = asOf ?? generatedAt ?? new Date().toISOString();
   447	  return {
   448	    asOf: resolvedAsOf,
   449	    generatedAt: generatedAt ?? resolvedAsOf,
   450	    method: "verified-db-weekly-ingest-plus-ai-snapshot",
   451	    sourceFeeds: [
   452	      "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   453	      "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   454	    ],
   455	    sourceRecords: [
   456	      {
   457	        source: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   458	        table: "analytics.dashboard_metrics",
   459	        recordHint: "latest driver metric set",
   460	        observedAt: resolvedAsOf,
   461	      },
   462	      {
   463	        source: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   464	        table: "analytics.driver_attribution_1d",
   465	        recordHint: "latest factor attribution set",
   466	        observedAt: resolvedAsOf,
   467	      },
   468	    ],
   469	  };
   470	}
   471	
   472	function buildDriverWhatsHappening(
   473	  driver: DriverKey,
   474	  payload: DriverData,
   475	  aggregateSummary: string,
   476	  topDriverName: string,
   477	  averagePressure: number,
   478	  instructions: StrategicSpecialInstructions,
   479	): WhatsHappening {
   480	  const scoreText = payload.score === null ? "No scored signal yet" : `Score ${Math.round(payload.score)}`;
   481	  const commonState = scoreState(payload.score);
   482	  const focus = driverFocus(driver);
   483	
   484	  const c = payload.components;
   485	  let supplyDemand = "Underlying supply/demand metrics are waiting on the next promoted dataset."
   486	  let geopolitical = "No explicit geopolitical pulse is dominating this driver in the current snapshot."
   487	  let sentiment = `Market posture is ${commonState}; regime tag is ${payload.regime}.`
   488	
   489	  if (driver === "vix_stress") {
   490	    supplyDemand = `VIX ${formatMetric(c.vix_value ?? null, "fixed1")} and OVX ${formatMetric(c.ovx_value ?? null, "fixed1")} define the current volatility envelope.`
   491	    geopolitical = "Cross-asset stress is being monitored as an early warning for procurement-cost spikes."
   492	    sentiment = `Volatility regime is ${commonState}; defensive hedging usually increases as this score rises.`
   493	  } else if (driver === "crush_pressure") {
   494	    supplyDemand = `Crush margin ${formatMetric(c.board_crush_value ?? null, "usd2")} and oil share ${formatMetric(c.oil_share_value ?? null, "fixed1")} describe processing economics.`
   495	    geopolitical = "Crush behavior is primarily a microstructure signal unless macro shocks force abrupt repricing."
   496	    sentiment = `Crush pressure is ${commonState}; buyers should watch processing spreads for timing risk.`
   497	  } else if (driver === "china_tension") {
   498	    supplyDemand = `CNY ${formatMetric(c.cny_rate ?? null, "fixed2")} with ${formatMetric(c.soy_china_news_count ?? null, "int")} China/soy headlines tracks demand-flow tension.`
   499	    geopolitical = "Trade-friction headlines and import-flow shifts remain the key geopolitical amplifier here."
   500	    sentiment = `China-linked risk is ${commonState}; abrupt headline shifts can reprice demand assumptions quickly.`
   501	  } else if (driver === "tariff_threat") {
   502	    supplyDemand = `Uncertainty index ${formatMetric(c.uncertainty_value ?? null, "int")} and crude 5D ${formatMetric(c.oil_change_5d ?? null, "pct")} frame policy-to-price transmission.`
   503	    geopolitical = `${formatMetric(c.iran_war_news_count ?? null, "int")} Iran/war headlines and ${formatMetric(c.macro_news_count ?? null, "int")} macro headlines are feeding this channel.`
   504	    sentiment = `Policy risk is ${commonState}; this is the macro/geopolitical lane most likely to force schedule adjustments.`
   505	  } else {
   506	    supplyDemand = `CL ${formatMetric(c.cl_price ?? null, "usd2")}, 5D change ${formatMetric(c.cl_change_5d ?? null, "pct")}, and OVX ${formatMetric(c.ovx_value ?? null, "fixed1")} define energy stress.`
   507	    geopolitical = `Energy headline count is ${formatMetric(c.energy_news_count ?? null, "int")}; supply-shock sensitivity remains active.`
   508	    sentiment = `Energy pass-through is ${commonState}; sustained pressure can lift procurement urgency.`
   509	  }
   510	
   511	  const zlImplication =
   512	    payload.score === null
   513	      ? "Keep standard buying cadence while waiting for fresh promoted metrics."
   514	      : payload.score >= 70
   515	        ? "Increase execution vigilance, shorten decision windows, and preserve optionality on contract timing."
   516	        : payload.score >= 45
   517	          ? "Maintain normal execution with tighter monitoring around upcoming refresh windows."
   518	          : "Pressure is contained; continue schedule-based procurement unless another driver escalates."
   519	
   520	  return {
   521	    whatsHappening: `${payload.name} is currently ${commonState}. ${scoreText} with level ${payload.level} keeps focus on ${focus}. Strategic frame: ${instructions.cardTopic}.`,
   522	    macroContext: `${aggregateSummary} Top pressure is ${topDriverName} and average pressure is ${averagePressure.toFixed(1)}.`,
   523	    supplyDemand,
   524	    geopolitical,
   525	    investorSentiment: sentiment,
   526	    nearTermOutlook: payload.score === null ? "Hard stop: near-term outlook blocked until verified signal fields are populated." : payload.score >= 70 ? "High-risk posture likely to persist near term." : payload.score >= 45 ? "Mixed posture with event-driven volatility risk." : "Low-pressure posture unless headline regime changes.",
   527	    zlImplication,
   528	  };
   529	}
   530	
   531	export async function GET() {
   532	  try {
   533	    const unauthorized = await requireAuthenticatedApiRequest();
   534	    if (unauthorized) {
   535	      return unauthorized;
   536	    }
   537	
   538	    const supabase = createSupabaseAdminClient();
   539	    const aiSnapshot = await readAiRiskFactorsSnapshot();
   540	    const responseGeneratedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   541	
   542	    const [{ data: metricRows, error: metricError }, { data: attributionRows, error: attributionError }] = await Promise.all([
   543	      supabase
   544	        .schema("analytics")
   545	        .from("dashboard_metrics")
   546	        .select("trade_date, metric_key, metric_value")
   547	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   548	        .limit(500),
   549	      supabase
   550	        .schema("analytics")
   551	        .from("driver_attribution_1d")
   552	        .select("trade_date, rank, factor, contribution, confidence")
   553	        .order("trade_date", { ascending: false })
   554	        .order("rank", { ascending: true })
   555	        .limit(100),
   556	    ]);
   557	
   558	    if (metricError || attributionError) {
   559	      return NextResponse.json(
   560	        {
   561	          error: metricError?.message ?? attributionError?.message ?? "Failed to load market drivers",
   562	        },
   563	        { status: 500 },
   564	      );
   565	    }
   566	
   567	    const latestMetricDate = metricRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   568	    const latestMetrics = (metricRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestMetricDate);
   569	    const metricMap = new Map<string, number | null>();
   570	    for (const row of latestMetrics) {
   571	      metricMap.set(normalizeMetricKey(row.metric_key), toNumber(row.metric_value));
   572	    }
   573	
   574	    const latestAttributionDate = attributionRows?.[0]?.trade_date ?? null;
   575	    const latestAttribution = (attributionRows ?? []).filter((r) => r.trade_date === latestAttributionDate);
   576	    const attributionByDriver = new Map<DriverKey, { trade_date: string; factor: string; contribution: number | null }>();
   577	    for (const row of latestAttribution) {
   578	      const driverKey = mapFactorToDriver(String(row.factor));
   579	      if (!driverKey) continue;
   580	      const contribution = toNumber(row.contribution);
   581	      const existing = attributionByDriver.get(driverKey);
   582	      const existingAbs = existing?.contribution === null || existing?.contribution === undefined ? -1 : Math.abs(existing.contribution);
   583	      const currentAbs = contribution === null ? -1 : Math.abs(contribution);
   584	      if (!existing || currentAbs > existingAbs) {
   585	        attributionByDriver.set(driverKey, {
   586	          trade_date: String(row.trade_date),
   587	          factor: String(row.factor),
   588	          contribution,
   589	        });
   590	      }
   591	    }
   592	
   593	    const scoreCandidates = {
   594	      vix_stress: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["vix_stress_score", "vix_score", "market_volatility_score", "driver_vix_score"])),
   595	      crush_pressure: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["crush_pressure_score", "crush_score", "driver_crush_score"])),
   596	      china_tension: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["china_tension_score", "china_score", "driver_china_score"])),
   597	      tariff_threat: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["tariff_threat_score", "tariff_score", "policy_risk_score", "driver_tariff_score"])),
   598	      energy_stress: coerceScore(getMetric(metricMap, ["energy_stress_score", "energy_score", "driver_energy_score"])),
   599	    };
   600	
   601	    for (const key of Object.keys(scoreCandidates) as DriverKey[]) {
   602	      if (scoreCandidates[key] === null) {
   603	        scoreCandidates[key] = coerceScore(attributionByDriver.get(key)?.contribution ?? null);
   604	      }
   605	    }
   606	
   607	    const normalized = (value: number, floor: number, ceil: number): number =>
   608	      Math.max(0, Math.min(100, ((value - floor) / (ceil - floor)) * 100));
   609	
   610	    if (scoreCandidates.vix_stress === null) {
   611	      const vix = getMetric(metricMap, ["vix_value"]);
   612	      const ovx = getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]);
   613	      if (vix !== null || ovx !== null) {
   614	        const vixScore = vix === null ? 0 : normalized(vix, 14, 40);
   615	        const ovxScore = ovx === null ? 0 : normalized(ovx, 20, 60);
   616	        scoreCandidates.vix_stress = Math.round((vixScore * 0.55 + ovxScore * 0.45) * 10) / 10;
   617	      }
   618	    }
   619	
   620	    if (scoreCandidates.energy_stress === null) {
   621	      const cl5d = getMetric(metricMap, ["cl_change_5d", "crude_oil_change_5d"]);
   622	      const ovx = getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]);
   623	      if (cl5d !== null || ovx !== null) {
   624	        const clScore = cl5d === null ? 0 : normalized(Math.abs(cl5d * 100), 0.5, 8);
   625	        const ovxScore = ovx === null ? 0 : normalized(ovx, 20, 60);
   626	        scoreCandidates.energy_stress = Math.round((clScore * 0.5 + ovxScore * 0.5) * 10) / 10;
   627	      }
   628	    }
   629	
   630	    if (scoreCandidates.china_tension === null) {
   631	      const cny = getMetric(metricMap, ["cny_rate"]);
   632	      if (cny !== null) {
   633	        scoreCandidates.china_tension = Math.round(normalized(Math.abs(cny - 7.0), 0.03, 0.35) * 10) / 10;
   634	      }
   635	    }
   636	
   637	    const metricDate = latestMetricDate ? String(latestMetricDate) : null;
   638	    const dateFor = (key: DriverKey): string | null =>
   639	      attributionByDriver.get(key)?.trade_date ?? metricDate;
   640	
   641	    const drivers: Record<DriverKey, DriverData> = {
   642	      vix_stress: {
   643	        name: "VIX Stress",
   644	        score: scoreCandidates.vix_stress,
   645	        level: levelFor("vix_stress", scoreCandidates.vix_stress),
   646	        regime: regimeFor(scoreCandidates.vix_stress),
   647	        headline: headlineFor("Market volatility", scoreCandidates.vix_stress),
   648	        components: {
   649	          vix_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["vix_value"]),
   650	          ovx_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]),
   651	        },
   652	        aiPowered: false,
   653	        dataDate: dateFor("vix_stress"),
   654	      },
   655	      crush_pressure: {
   656	        name: "Crush Pressure",
   657	        score: scoreCandidates.crush_pressure,
   658	        level: levelFor("crush_pressure", scoreCandidates.crush_pressure),
   659	        regime: regimeFor(scoreCandidates.crush_pressure),
   660	        headline: headlineFor("Crush margins", scoreCandidates.crush_pressure),
   661	        components: {
   662	          board_crush_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["board_crush_value", "crush_margin"]),
   663	          oil_share_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["oil_share_value", "soy_oil_share"]),
   664	          oil_share_5d_change: getMetric(metricMap, ["oil_share_5d_change"]),
   665	        },
   666	        aiPowered: false,
   667	        dataDate: dateFor("crush_pressure"),
   668	      },
   669	      china_tension: {
   670	        name: "China Tension",
   671	        score: scoreCandidates.china_tension,
   672	        level: levelFor("china_tension", scoreCandidates.china_tension),
   673	        regime: regimeFor(scoreCandidates.china_tension),
   674	        headline: headlineFor("China demand/trade", scoreCandidates.china_tension),
   675	        components: {
   676	          cny_rate: getMetric(metricMap, ["cny_rate"]),
   677	          soy_china_news_count: getMetric(metricMap, ["soy_china_news_count", "china_soy_news_count"]),
   678	        },
   679	        aiPowered: false,
   680	        dataDate: dateFor("china_tension"),
   681	      },
   682	      tariff_threat: {
   683	        name: "Macro Threat",
   684	        score: scoreCandidates.tariff_threat,
   685	        level: levelFor("tariff_threat", scoreCandidates.tariff_threat),
   686	        regime: regimeFor(scoreCandidates.tariff_threat),
   687	        headline: headlineFor("Macro/geopolitical", scoreCandidates.tariff_threat),
   688	        components: {
   689	          tpu_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["tpu_value", "trade_policy_uncertainty"]),
   690	          uncertainty_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["uncertainty_value", "tpu_value", "trade_policy_uncertainty"]),
   691	          emv_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["emv_value", "trade_policy_index"]),
   692	          soy_tariff_news_count: getMetric(metricMap, ["soy_tariff_news_count", "tariff_news_count"]),
   693	          oil_change_5d: getMetric(metricMap, ["oil_change_5d", "cl_change_5d", "crude_oil_change_5d"]),
   694	          iran_war_news_count: getMetric(metricMap, ["iran_war_news_count", "soy_tariff_news_count", "tariff_news_count"]),
   695	          macro_news_count: getMetric(metricMap, ["macro_news_count", "soy_tariff_news_count", "tariff_news_count"]),
   696	        },
   697	        aiPowered: false,
   698	        dataDate: dateFor("tariff_threat"),
   699	      },
   700	      energy_stress: {
   701	        name: "Energy Stress",
   702	        score: scoreCandidates.energy_stress,
   703	        level: levelFor("energy_stress", scoreCandidates.energy_stress),
   704	        regime: regimeFor(scoreCandidates.energy_stress),
   705	        headline: headlineFor("Energy complex", scoreCandidates.energy_stress),
   706	        components: {
   707	          cl_price: getMetric(metricMap, ["cl_price", "crude_oil_price"]),
   708	          cl_change_5d: getMetric(metricMap, ["cl_change_5d", "crude_oil_change_5d"]),
   709	          ovx_value: getMetric(metricMap, ["ovx_value"]),
   710	          energy_news_count: getMetric(metricMap, ["energy_news_count"]),
   711	        },
   712	        aiPowered: false,
   713	        dataDate: dateFor("energy_stress"),
   714	      },
   715	    };
   716	
   717	    const allDates = (Object.values(drivers) as DriverData[])
   718	      .map((d) => d.dataDate)
   719	      .filter((d): d is string => Boolean(d))
   720	      .sort();
   721	    const asOfDateMin = allDates[0] ?? null;
   722	    const asOfDateMax = allDates[allDates.length - 1] ?? null;
   723	    const asOfDate = asOfDateMin;
   724	    const mixedVintage = Boolean(asOfDateMin && asOfDateMax && asOfDateMin !== asOfDateMax);
   725	
   726	    const mergeDriver = (
   727	      key: DriverKey,
   728	      base: DriverData,
   729	      defaultHeadlineName: string,
   730	      defaultInstructions: StrategicSpecialInstructions,
   731	    ): DriverData => {
   732	      const ai = aiSnapshot?.drivers?.[key];
   733	      const aiScoreRaw = ai?.score;
   734	      const aiScore = aiScoreRaw === null || aiScoreRaw === undefined ? null : coerceScore(Number(aiScoreRaw));
   735	      const mergedScore = aiScore ?? base.score;
   736	      const mergedLevel = ai?.level ?? levelFor(key, mergedScore);
   737	      const mergedComponents = mergeDriverComponents(base.components, ai?.components);
   738	
   739	      const mergedPayload: DriverData = {
   740	        ...base,
   741	        score: mergedScore,
   742	        level: mergedLevel,
   743	        regime: regimeFor(mergedScore),
   744	        headline: ai?.headline ?? headlineFor(defaultHeadlineName, mergedScore),
   745	        components: mergedComponents,
   746	        strategicSpecialInstructions:
   747	          ai?.strategicSpecialInstructions ?? defaultInstructions,
   748	        provenance:
   749	          ai?.provenance ??
   750	          buildDriverProvenance(
   751	            key,
   752	            base.dataDate,
   753	            aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? responseGeneratedAt,
   754	          ),
   755	        aiPowered: Boolean(ai),
   756	        dataDate: base.dataDate,
   757	      };
   758	
   759	      return {
   760	        ...mergedPayload,
   761	        whatsHappening: ai?.whatsHappening,
   762	      };
   763	    };
   764	
   765	    const mergedDrivers: Record<DriverKey, DriverData> = {
   766	      vix_stress: mergeDriver(
   767	        "vix_stress",
   768	        drivers.vix_stress,
   769	        "Market volatility",
   770	        DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS.vix_stress,
   771	      ),
   772	      crush_pressure: mergeDriver(
   773	        "crush_pressure",
   774	        drivers.crush_pressure,
   775	        "Crush margins",
   776	        DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS.crush_pressure,
   777	      ),
   778	      china_tension: mergeDriver(
   779	        "china_tension",
   780	        drivers.china_tension,
   781	        "China demand/trade",
   782	        DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS.china_tension,
   783	      ),
   784	      tariff_threat: mergeDriver(
   785	        "tariff_threat",
   786	        drivers.tariff_threat,
   787	        "Macro/geopolitical",
   788	        DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS.tariff_threat,
   789	      ),
   790	      energy_stress: mergeDriver(
   791	        "energy_stress",
   792	        drivers.energy_stress,
   793	        "Energy complex",
   794	        DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS.energy_stress,
   795	      ),
   796	    };
   797	
   798	    const mergedSummary = summarizeDrivers(mergedDrivers);
   799	    const mergedAverage = mergedSummary.average;
   800	    const mergedTopName = mergedSummary.topName;
   801	    const mergedTopScore = mergedSummary.topScore;
   802	    const mergedAlertCount = mergedSummary.alertCount;
   803	
   804	    for (const key of Object.keys(mergedDrivers) as DriverKey[]) {
   805	      const driver = mergedDrivers[key];
   806	      if (driver.whatsHappening) continue;
   807	      const instructions =
   808	        driver.strategicSpecialInstructions ?? DRIVER_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS[key];
   809	      driver.whatsHappening = buildDriverWhatsHappening(
   810	        key,
   811	        driver,
   812	        mergedTopScore === 0 && mergedTopName === "No Data"
   813	          ? "Hard stop: promoted analytics rows are missing."
   814	          : `Top concern is ${mergedTopName} at ${Math.round(mergedTopScore)}.`,
   815	        mergedTopName,
   816	        mergedAverage,
   817	        instructions,
   818	      );
   819	    }
   820	
   821	    const response: MarketDriversResponse = {
   822	      as_of_date: asOfDate,
   823	      as_of_date_min: asOfDateMin,
   824	      as_of_date_max: asOfDateMax,
   825	      mixed_vintage: mixedVintage,
   826	      drivers: mergedDrivers,
   827	      summary: {
   828	        average_pressure: mergedAverage,
   829	        highest_pressure: { name: mergedTopName, score: mergedTopScore },
   830	        alert_count: mergedAlertCount,
   831	      },
   832	      intelligence: {
   833	        headline:
   834	          aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.headline ??
   835	          (mergedTopScore >= 65 ? "ELEVATED MARKET - Watch Procurement Risk" : "NORMAL MARKET - Buy On Schedule"),
   836	        summary:
   837	          aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.summary ??
   838	          (mergedTopName === "No Data"
   839	            ? "Hard stop: promoted analytics rows are missing for cross-driver synthesis."
   840	            : `Top concern is ${mergedTopName} at ${Math.round(mergedTopScore)}. Average risk is ${mergedAverage}.`),
   841	        drivers: aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.drivers ?? (Object.values(mergedDrivers) as DriverData[])
   842	          .filter((d) => d.score !== null)
   843	          .map((d) => ({
   844	            label: d.name,
   845	            outlook: d.score !== null && d.score >= 65 ? "PRESSURE" : d.score !== null && d.score <= 35 ? "SUPPORTIVE" : "MIXED",
   846	            detail: d.headline,
   847	          })),
   848	        zlOutlook: aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.zlOutlook ?? outlookFromScore(mergedAverage),
   849	        zlColor: aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.zlColor ?? colorFromScore(mergedAverage),
   850	        tradingImplication:
   851	          aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.tradingImplication ??
   852	          (mergedTopScore >= 65
   853	            ? "Risk elevated. Keep procurement flexible and monitor next refresh."
   854	            : "No major pressure signal yet. Continue normal buying schedule."),
   855	        aiPowered: Boolean(aiSnapshot?.intelligence),
   856	        strategicSpecialInstructions:
   857	          aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.strategicSpecialInstructions ??
   858	          MARKET_INTELLIGENCE_STRATEGIC_SPECIAL_INSTRUCTIONS,
   859	        provenance:
   860	          aiSnapshot?.intelligence?.provenance ??
   861	          buildIntelligenceProvenance(
   862	            asOfDateMax ?? asOfDateMin,
   863	            aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? responseGeneratedAt,
   864	          ),
   865	      },
   866	      ai: {
   867	        enabled: Boolean(aiSnapshot),
   868	        source: aiSnapshot?.source ?? "weekly-db-plus-ai-snapshot",
   869	        model: aiSnapshot?.model ?? null,
   870	        reasoningEffort: aiSnapshot?.reasoningEffort ?? null,
   871	        generatedAt: aiSnapshot?.generatedAt ?? responseGeneratedAt,
   872	        refreshScheduleEt: aiSnapshot?.refreshScheduleEt ?? null,
   873	      },
   874	    };
   875	
   876	    return NextResponse.json(response, {
   877	      headers: { "Cache-Control": "no-store" },
   878	    });
   879	  } catch (err) {
   880	    return NextResponse.json(
   881	      { error: String(err) },
   882	      { status: 500, headers: { "Cache-Control": "no-store" } },
   883	    );
   884	  }
   885	}

     4	/**
     5	 * Especially important if using Fluid compute: Don't put this client in a

     9	export async function createClient() {

    11	
    12	  return createServerClient(
    13	    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,

    19	        },

    21	          try {

    25	          } catch {

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba app/api/auth/check/route.ts | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/zincdigital/mnt/SatechiHub/ZINC-FUSION-V16
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2	
     3	import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
     4	import { hasEnvVars } from "@/lib/utils";
     5	
     6	export async function GET() {
     7	  if (!hasEnvVars) {
     8	    return NextResponse.json(
     9	      {
    10	        ok: false,
    11	        authenticated: false,
    12	        error: "Supabase auth env vars are not configured",
    13	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    14	      },
    15	      { status: 503 },
    16	    );
    17	  }
    18	
    19	  const supabase = await createClient();
    20	  const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.getClaims();
    21	
    22	  if (error) {
    23	    return NextResponse.json(
    24	      {
    25	        ok: false,
    26	        authenticated: false,
    27	        error: error.message,
    28	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    29	      },
    30	      { status: 401 },
    31	    );
    32	  }
    33	
    34	  if (!data?.claims) {
    35	    return NextResponse.json(
    36	      {
    37	        ok: false,
    38	        authenticated: false,
    39	        error: "No authenticated user claims",
    40	        checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    41	      },
    42	      { status: 401 },
    43	    );
    44	  }
    45	
    46	  return NextResponse.json({
    47	    ok: true,
    48	    authenticated: true,
    49	    checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    50	  });
    51	}

    14	|---|---|
    15	| `/dashboard` | V15 |

    21	## Global Layout and Fidelity Contract
    22

    24	2. The current narrow/containerized V16 widths are not allowed.

    27	   - font family, size, weight, line-height, letter spacing

    31	   - hover, active, focus, selected, disabled

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# V16 Data Contracts (Weekly Batch Pivot - 2026-05-07)
     2	
     3	## Core Market Contracts (High-Frequency Path Retained)
     4	- `mkt.price_1h`: hourly OHLCV feed for chart freshness.
     5	- `mkt.price_1d`: daily OHLCV rollup for daily-bar path.
     6	- `mkt.latest_price`: most recent price for live status.
     7	- `forecasts.target_zones`: P30/P50/P70 horizontal levels.
     8	
     9	## Weekly Batch Contracts (Reduced Source Surface)
    10	- Non-price market, macro, supply, and alternative source ingestion runs in weekly batch windows.
    11	- Active source surface is reduced to approximately 25% of prior volume.
    12	- Batch output must be traceable by run metadata and ingest timestamps.
    13	
    14	## Forecast/Training Cadence Contract
    15	- Weekly retraining is the default cadence.
    16	- Weekly forecast publication follows successful weekly retrain.
    17	- Training and publication must remain explicit and auditable in `training.*` and `forecasts.*` tables.
    18	
    19	## Sentiment and Legislation Contracts
    20	- Sentiment and Legislation page content is GPT-driven from approved news/policy sources.
    21	- Generated summaries/scores must remain attributable to source records and run timestamps.
    22	
    23	## Dashboard AI Card Instruction Contract
    24	- `/api/dashboard/risk-factors` must provide per-card `strategicSpecialInstructions` for each driver card and the AI Market Intelligence card.
    25	- Instructions must be topic-specific, quant-research oriented, and include:
    26	  - strategic objective
    27	  - neural connection thesis
    28	  - quant research protocol
    29	  - inference constraints
    30	  - output requirements
    31	- Generic instruction text is not allowed for AI card instruction payloads.
    32	- AI narrative content is sourced from `app/config/dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json` (no OpenAI API key path).
    33	- Snapshot metadata must include `model`, `reasoningEffort`, `source`, `generatedAt`, and daily `refreshScheduleEt`.
    34	- Current locked runtime target: `gpt-5.5-fast` with `high-think`, refreshed daily at `07:00 America/New_York`.
    35	
    36	## AI Daily Card Pull Contract (All Pages)
    37	- Daily AI pull is the default source for card content on all primary pages.
    38	- Snapshot files are committed in-repo under `app/config/` and must be refreshed daily:
    39	  - `dashboard-risk-factors-ai.json`
    40	  - `strategy-posture-ai.json`
    41	  - `sentiment-overview-ai.json`
    42	  - `legislation-feed-ai.json`
    43	  - `vegas-intel-ai.json`
    44	- API routes must read their page snapshot and expose AI-derived card payloads, while preserving controlled DB fallback behavior.
    45	- AI card payloads should include market prices, reasoning, risk framing, and math-oriented interpretation wherever applicable.
    46	- Every AI-powered card on every page must include:
    47	  - `strategicSpecialInstructions` (topic-specific, quant-research, non-generic)
    48	  - `provenance` with table/source feeds and record hints
    49	- Card-level provenance must identify the source tables/feeds used for the AI narrative and include `asOf` + `generatedAt`.
    50	
    51	## Operational Contracts
    52	- `ops.ingest_run`: mandatory ingest lifecycle logging for batch and price paths.
    53	- `ops.pipeline_alerts`: freshness and pipeline alerting.
    54	- `ops.source_registry`: source ownership, cadence, and enablement state.
    55	
    56	## Freshness Expectations
    57	- Hourly contracts: update hourly for active market sessions.
    58	- Daily rollup contract: refresh from hourly path on daily schedule.
    59	- Weekly batch contracts: update on weekly schedule.
    60	- Forecast contracts: refresh after successful weekly retrain.

     1	# V16 Security Model (Scaffold)
     2	
     3	## Access Tiers
     4	- Browser users: authenticated session/JWT with RLS-constrained reads.
     5	- System jobs: service role for controlled writes.
     6	
     7	## Route Boundaries
     8	- `/api/cron/*` uses `CRON_SECRET` verification.
     9	- `/api/auth/check` validates active claims.
    10	- Protected API routes enforce explicit server auth guards (`requireAuthenticatedApiRequest`).
    11	- Protected pages enforce explicit server auth guards via route-group layout (`requireAuthenticatedPageSession`).
    12	
    13	## Secret Handling
    14	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`: URL + publishable key only.
    15	- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`: server-only usage (`lib/server/supabase-admin.ts`).
    16	- ProFarmer credentials stay off Vercel.
    17	
    18	## Required Controls
    19	- RLS enabled for all created tables.
    20	- Ingestion runs logged to `ops.ingest_run`.
    21	- Build/typecheck gates required before merge.

     1	# Fail-Closed Checklist
     2	
     3	This checklist is enforced by `scripts/fusion_guard.py` and documented in
     4	`docs/agent-safety-gates.md`.
     5	
     6	- [ ] Authority docs read in order from `docs/INDEX.md`.
     7	- [ ] Write-set declared before changes.
     8	- [ ] `git status --short` reviewed before and after changes.
     9	- [ ] Source lane and generated-quality lane handled separately.
    10	- [ ] Behavior/config/schema/gate changes updated docs or contracts in the same change.
    11	- [ ] Quality artifacts regenerated after source/config/tooling changes that affect quality evidence.
    12	- [ ] `npm run lint` passed.
    13	- [ ] `npm run build` passed.
    14	- [ ] Required tests ran; unavailable tests are recorded as `NOT RUN` and block completion.
    15	- [ ] Quality Playbook doctor/smoke passed when Quality Playbook is installed.
    16	- [ ] `quality/mechanical/verify.sh` passed when quality artifacts exist.
    17	- [ ] Installed Quality Playbook gate passed with zero warnings.
    18	- [ ] Quality Playbook finalizer did not abort.
    19	- [ ] Unknowns explicitly marked and either resolved or reported as blockers.
    20	- [ ] Final status uses `PASS`, `FAIL`, `NOT RUN`, or `INCOMPLETE`.
    21	
    22	If any required item is unchecked, the final status is `INCOMPLETE`.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# V16 Engineering Principles (Executable)
     2	
     3	## Fail-Closed Core
     4	- If safety or data integrity is unclear, stop and mark the item blocked.
     5	- Never assume schema compatibility: verify with migration SQL and table introspection.
     6	- Never claim behavior without command output or file evidence.
     7	
     8	## Data Contract-First
     9	- API contracts are derived from schema contracts.
    10	- Every table must have a writer and a reader.
    11	- Every external feed write must include `ingested_at` and source traceability.
    12	
    13	## Quant Safety
    14	- No future leakage into features.
    15	- Target labels remain explicit and versioned (`target_price_{h}d`).
    16	- Forecast writes must include model version and feature snapshot metadata.
    17	
    18	## Security Baselines
    19	- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` is server-only.
    20	- Every `/api/cron/*` route must enforce `CRON_SECRET`.
    21	- RLS is mandatory for all schemas from initial migration.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# ZINC-FUSION-V16 Migration Plan
     2	
     3	## Clean-Room Rebuild on Supabase
     4	
     5	**Date:** 2026-03-17
     6	**Status:** Approved design — ready for execution
     7	**Approach:** Audit-first blueprint — product requirements drive architecture, not legacy baseline code
     8	
     9	---
    10	
    11	## Table of Contents
    12	
    13	1. [Principles](#1-principles)
    14	2. [Product Surface — What V16 Must Deliver](#2-product-surface--what-v16-must-deliver)
    15	3. [Target Architecture](#3-target-architecture)
    16	4. [Schema Design (9 Schemas)](#4-schema-design-9-schemas)
    17	5. [Job Architecture — Replacing Inngest](#5-job-architecture--replacing-inngest)
    18	6. [API Surface](#6-api-surface)
    19	7. [Auth & Security Model](#7-auth--security-model)
    20	8. [Python Pipeline Rebuild](#8-python-pipeline-rebuild)
    21	9. [Frontend Wireframe](#9-frontend-wireframe)
    22	10. [Evaluation Gates & Quality Checks](#10-evaluation-gates--quality-checks)
    23	11. [Phased Execution Sequence](#11-phased-execution-sequence)
    24	12. [Risk Matrix](#12-risk-matrix)
    25	13. [Highest-Value Validation Steps](#13-highest-value-validation-steps)
    26	14. [Dependency-Aware Remediation Sequence](#14-dependency-aware-remediation-sequence)
    27	
    28	---
    29	
    30	## 1. Principles
    31	
    32	### What V16 Is
    33	
    34	- A from-scratch rebuild — **no code transferred from legacy baseline**
    35	- legacy baseline stays live as reference and rollback during the entire build
    36	- legacy baseline is a reference for _what the product does_, not a source of code to port
    37	- Purpose: eliminate all drift, errors, mismatches, legacy baggage, Inngest complexity
    38	
    39	### What V16 Is Not
    40	
    41	- Not a migration-in-place
    42	- Not a refactor of legacy baseline
    43	- Not a copy-paste with cleanup
    44	- Not allowed to inherit: the old Prisma migration chain, copied `.vercel/` state, hand-copied `.env.local` habits, the Inngest estate, dead pages, dead jobs, or unclear contracts
    45	
    46	### Non-Negotiable Rules
    47	
    48	1. No old migration history crosses the boundary
    49	2. Every table must have a reader AND a writer
    50	3. Every page must justify its data dependencies
    51	4. Every job must justify its existence
    52	5. Start from the screen, not the schema
    53	6. Rewrite the chart from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — settings were hard-won, preserve behavior exactly
    54	7. Rewrite the landing page from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — specific and intentional design to preserve
    55	8. ProFarmer is mandatory ($500/month)
    56	9. 11 specialists — never 10. `trump_effect` is the 11th.
    57	10. Target = future price level. Target Zones = horizontal lines. Never cones, bands, or funnels.
    58	
    59	---
    60	
    61	## 2. Product Surface — What V16 Must Deliver
    62	
    63	### Primary Users & Their Needs
    64	
    65	| User                                                                        | Need                                                 | V16 Surface                                       |
    66	| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
    67	| **Chris** (owner, US Oil Solutions — BUYS raw soybean oil by the trainload) | Know where ZL price is heading to time purchases     | Dashboard chart + Target Zones + Strategy posture |
    68	| **Chris**                                                                   | Understand what's driving price                      | Top 4 Drivers, Regime state, Specialist signals   |
    69	| **Kevin** (sales director)                                                  | Pitch restaurants, time service visits around events | Vegas Intel page                                  |
    70	| **Both**                                                                    | Policy/legislation impact on soy oil                 | Legislation page                                  |
    71	| **Both**                                                                    | Market sentiment read                                | Sentiment page                                    |
    72	
    73	### V16 Page Surface (6 pages — Quant dropped)
    74	
    75	1. **`/`** — Landing page (rewrite from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference — ZERO code copied, ZERO mock data)
    76	2. **`/dashboard`** — ZL candlestick chart (rewrite from scratch using legacy baseline as visual reference) + Target Zones + live price + regime + drivers + cards
    77	3. **`/strategy`** — Posture recommendation, contract impact calculator, factor waterfall (keep content, redesign layout)
    78	4. **`/legislation`** — Federal Register / policy tracking (clean rebuild)
    79	5. **`/sentiment`** — News sentiment, CoT positioning, narrative (keep first 3-4 rows)
    80	6. **`/vegas-intel`** — Restaurant/casino event intelligence (keep ALL content, better layout — events = everything)
    81	
    82	### Critical Data Contracts
    83	
    84	| Contract                  | Powers                                | Freshness             |
    85	| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
    86	| ZL OHLCV daily            | Chart rendering                       | Daily by market close |
    87	| ZL OHLCV 1h               | Intraday chart                        | Hourly during session |
    88	| ZL live/latest price      | Live ticker, status bar               | Real-time or near-RT  |
    89	| Forecast Target Zones     | P30/P50/P70 horizontal zones on chart | After each model run  |
    90	| Specialist signals        | Driver attribution, regime            | Daily after pipeline  |
    91	| Monte Carlo / probability | Probability statements                | After each MC run     |
    92	| Regime state              | Dashboard regime chip                 | Daily                 |
    93	| Legislation events        | Legislation page                      | Daily                 |
    94	| News/sentiment            | Sentiment page                        | Daily                 |
    95	| Vegas operations          | Vegas Intel page                      | Event-driven          |
    96	
    97	### What Dies (legacy baseline baggage that does NOT cross)
    98	
    99	- 34 Prisma migrations
   100	- 104 Inngest functions (replaced by pg_cron + http extension + Python workers)
   101	- Local Inngest dev server and healing scripts
   102	- Duplicate/overlapping data ingestion jobs
   103	- Dead tables (`eia_biodiesel_1w` with 0 rows, `uco_prices_1w` with 0 rows)
   104	- Tables with no reader (orphaned)
   105	- `inngest_receipts` table
   106	- Quant page (dropped)
   107	- The entire `.vercel/` state
   108	- Manual `.env.local` habits
   109	- Over-normalized Vegas tables (17 → 7)
   110	- ProFarmer local orchestration complexity (rebuilt as Python Playwright)
   111	
   112	---
   113	
   114	## 3. Target Architecture
   115	
   116	### Stack
   117	
   118	| Layer                  | legacy baseline (current)       | V16 (target)                                                       |
   119	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
   120	| **Database**           | Prisma Postgres (cloud)         | Supabase Postgres                                                  |
   121	| **Schema mgmt**        | Prisma + 34 migrations          | Supabase migrations (SQL-first)                                    |
   122	| **Frontend**           | Next.js on Vercel               | Next.js on Vercel (new project) + shadcn/ui + Radix + Tailwind CSS |
   123	| **Scheduling**         | Inngest (104 functions)         | pg_cron + http extension (all ingestion inside Postgres)           |
   124	| **DB client (TS)**     | pg.Pool + Prisma for validation | Supabase JS client + pg.Pool for bulk                              |
   125	| **DB client (Python)** | psycopg2 direct                 | psycopg2 direct to Supabase Postgres                               |
   126	| **ML**                 | AutoGluon (local, CPU)          | AutoGluon (local, CPU) — rebuilt clean                             |
   127	| **Specialists**        | 11 Python signal generators     | 11 Python signal generators — rebuilt clean                        |
   128	| **Auth**               | Custom cookie-based             | Supabase Auth                                                      |
   129	| **Env mgmt**           | Manual .env.local               | Vercel <> Supabase integration, `vercel env pull`                  |
   130	| **API secrets**        | Manual env vars                 | Supabase Vault (`current_setting()`)                               |
   131	| **UI system**          | Mixed CSS + shadcn/ui           | shadcn/ui + Radix + Tailwind CSS + ZINC Fusion brand tokens        |
   132	
   133	### Cloud Supabase ONLY — No Local Supabase
   134	
   135	| Scenario                      | Approach                                                                                                                                                  | Why                                                                                                                                                   |
   136	| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   137	| **Frontend dev**              | Reads from cloud Supabase                                                                                                                                 | Single source of truth. No local/cloud drift. `vercel env pull` provides connection.                                                                  |
   138	| **Python training/inference** | Reads canonical raw data from cloud Supabase, can materialize the bounded AG training source into local PostgreSQL for training, and writes intermediates to **local parquet files** during compute. Promotes validated outputs back to cloud. | Cloud is canonical. Local PostgreSQL is a training-source staging database only, not Supabase and not serving truth. Intermediates stay local during iteration. Only validated artifacts are promoted back to cloud DB. |
   139	| **Data ingestion**            | Runs **INSIDE Postgres** via pg_cron + http extension                                                                                                     | No Vercel cron routes. No external orchestrator. Ingestion is a database-native operation.                                                            |
   140	| **Supabase CLI**              | Used for migrations ONLY (`supabase db push`, `supabase db diff --linked`)                                                                                | No `supabase status`. No `supabase start`. No local Supabase runtime. Local PostgreSQL, when used, is a separate AG training-source database. |
   141	
   142	**Guard rail:** Create a `training` Postgres role that can only write to `training.*` and `forecasts.*` schemas. The Python pipeline uses this role. Frontend service role is read-only on those schemas.
   143	
   144	### Architecture Diagram
   145	
   146	```
   147	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   148	|                      BROWSER                               |
   149	|  Next.js App (Vercel) -- 6 pages                          |
   150	|  Supabase JS client for auth + reads                      |
   151	+---------------------------+-------------------------------+
   152	                            |
   153	+---------------------------v-------------------------------+
   154	|                       VERCEL                               |
   155	|  Frontend hosting ONLY + read-only API routes              |
   156	|  No cron routes. No data ingestion.                        |
   157	|  Env: auto-injected via Supabase integration               |
   158	+---------------------------+-------------------------------+
   159	                            |
   160	+---------------------------v-------------------------------+
   161	|                      SUPABASE                              |
   162	|  Single cloud DB (9 schemas: mkt, econ, alt, supply,      |
   163	|    training, forecasts, analytics, ops, vegas)              |
   164	|  pg_cron + http extension (ALL data ingestion ~22 jobs)    |
   165	|  Vault (API keys via current_setting())                    |
   166	|  Auth (user authentication)                                |
   167	|  RLS policies per schema                                   |
   168	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   169	
   170	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   171	|              LOCAL MACHINE (compute workspace)              |
   172	|  Python ML Pipeline (rebuilt from scratch)                  |
   173	|  - Reads raw data from cloud Supabase via psycopg2         |
   174	|    (cloud is canonical; local PostgreSQL is AG staging only) |
   175	|  - Writes intermediates to local parquet files              |
   176	|  - promote_to_cloud.py pushes validated outputs to cloud   |
   177	|                                                            |
   178	|  ProFarmer Scraper (Python Playwright, system cron)        |
   179	|  - Writes directly to cloud Supabase                       |
   180	+-----------------------------------------------------------+
   181	```
   182	
   183	### Cloud-Canonical Data Contract
   184	
   185	| Layer                                                             | Canonical Location          | Notes                                                        |
   186	| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
   187	| Raw ingest tables (mkt, econ, alt, supply)                        | **Cloud Supabase**          | pg_cron + http writes directly to cloud                      |
   188	| Serving tables (analytics, forecasts.target_zones)                | **Cloud Supabase**          | Dashboard reads from cloud                                   |
   189	| Published forecasts / Target Zones                                | **Cloud Supabase**          | Pre-computed, served to chart                                |
   190	| Training metadata (model_registry, training_runs)                 | **Cloud Supabase**          | Registry of what was trained and when                        |
   191	| Ops observability (ingest_run, pipeline_alerts)                   | **Cloud Supabase**          | All logging in cloud                                         |
   192	| Wide intermediate artifacts (feature matrix, specialist parquets) | **Local compute workspace** | Parquet files during processing only — not canonical storage |
   193	| Model artifacts (AutoGluon model files)                           | **Local compute workspace** | Large binary artifacts, not in any database                  |
   194	
   195	**Training data storage architecture** (whether wide training artifacts should be retained locally vs promoted to cloud `training.*` tables) is a separate checkpoint decision, still gated. Current posture: cloud Supabase `training.*` tables exist and are the intended destination for `promote_to_cloud.py`. Local parquet files are ephemeral compute intermediates. As of the 2026-05-09 training-prep remediation, local prep artifacts live under `data/fusion/`, model artifacts live under `models/fusion/`, and cloud promotion is isolated behind the explicit `promote_to_cloud.py` approval gate. Promotion strips `target_price_{h}d` labels out of cloud `training.matrix_1d.feature_snapshot`; labels remain local-only training columns.
   196	
   197	### Cron-First Ingestion Contract
   198	
   199	All data ingestion runs inside Supabase Postgres via pg_cron + http extension. No ingestion readiness can be claimed until:
   200	
   201	1. **Functions exist** — plpgsql ingestion functions are deployed via migration
   202	2. **Cron jobs are registered** — `SELECT count(*) FROM cron.job` returns expected schedule count
   203	3. **First successful run** — each function has at least one `status = 'ok'` row in `ops.ingest_run`
   204	4. **Vault keys are stored** — `current_setting('app.<key>')` returns non-null for all configured sources
   205	
   206	**Current state (as of planning):** `cron.job` has 0 entries. Extensions pg_cron, http, pg_net are enabled. No ingestion functions exist. This is an explicit blocker for Gate 4.
   207	
   208	---
